title: Pin wrappers/Javascript/Dockerfile to CI emsdk sha (emcc 4.0.12)
description: The Dockerfile uses 'FROM emscripten/emsdk' (unpinned), while .github/workflows/javascript_builder.yml is pinned to emscripten/emsdk@sha256:744fb6a68941970951bacf9d6632041a0398260492232691ef22bbf54b0585c6 (emcc 4.0.12). Locally pulling 'latest' (emcc 5.0.7) builds fine but resolves a Module instance with zero embind bindings, because 5.x dropped the JS-side __embind_register_function/__embind_register_class dispatch + exposePublicSymbol. Result: 'node test_wasm.mjs' fails on every dev's machine with 'coolprop.F2K is not a function' even though CI passes. Pin the Dockerfile to the same sha. Acceptance: local 'docker compose run --rm runner' produces a coolprop.js/coolprop.wasm against which 'node test_wasm.mjs' passes. Blocks lifting of pin until CoolProp-71a's emcc-5.x-compat child is done. Local patch exists in worktree i2664; trivially extractable.
acceptance_criteria: Local 'cd wrappers/Javascript && docker compose build --no-cache runner && docker compose run --rm runner' produces artifacts; 'node test_wasm.mjs' from that directory exits 0.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/pull/2970 (bundled with .5 and notes for .2). Closes when merged.

title: PR #2892: BICUBIC P(D,T) fix incomplete — own test fails in CI
description: PR #2892 adds pick_unit_interval_root to BicubicBackend::invert_single_phase_x/y to address #1301, but the PR's own new [BICUBIC][1301] test still fails in CI: for CO2 at T=320K, rho=1 kg/m^3, p_b = -749 MPa (HEOS p_h = 60 kPa). The pre-fix value was -760 MPa, so the new code path runs but degrades gracefully to the closest-to-[0,1] root, which is still far outside the cell. Hypothesis: find_nearest_neighbor picks a wrong (high-pressure) cell for the very-low-density supercritical region, so no root in [0,1] exists for that cell. Also: rebase onto master (conflicts vs current tip).
notes: PR #2892 closed without merging. The right architectural fix per maintainer is a DT-indexed SVDSBTL surface (filed as new follow-up issue), not patching BICUBIC's invert_single_phase_y. The bilinear-bisection insight in TabularBackends.h could be revived as a stand-alone PR if there's appetite, but #1301 itself stays open against the SVDSBTL DT-table work.

title: Replace Newton h-inversion with TOMS748 in SVDSBTL IF97 sampling
description:
**Bug found 2026-05-20 via foi.9.9 follow-up:**

The IF97-source update_state lambda in src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp Newton-iterates T to match h_target at each grid cell.  In R3 territory the initial guess is T=700 K (fallback after HmassP_INPUTS throws).  At pressures where T_B23(p) is close to or below 700 K (i.e., p < ~28 MPa in R3 territory), the initial guess lands in IF97 R2 territory.  Newton then has to cross the R2/R3 boundary, where cp values differ sharply, and overshoots into R1, bounces back, etc.  In some cells (e.g., T_target=663.7 K, p=26.6 MPa) Newton fails to converge in 10 iterations.

Symptoms: stored T / s / ρ / w values at affected cells are wrong by factors of 2-50.  The SVD interpolation downstream amplifies these into the visible R3 conformance failures — the v=213% / s=1283 J/kgK outlier at T=663.7 K is one such cell.

The fix is bracketed iteration: TOMS748 (Boost via boost/math/tools/toms748_solve.hpp, already used in CoolProp's superancillary + FlashRoutines + AbstractState).  Robust across IF97 region boundaries because the bracket guarantees the iteration stays inside a known root-containing interval.

**Scope (broader than just R3):**

1. src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp IF97 sampling lambda: keep HmassP_INPUTS fast path for R1/R2/R5, but replace the Newton polish with TOMS748 on a tight bracket [T_hmassP - δ, T_hmassP + δ].  For R3 fallback, TOMS748 on [623.15, T_B23(p)].
2. src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTLFailMap.cpp refine_to_forward_h: same pattern.  Currently Newton-iterates the truth side; same robustness benefit.
3. Cache rev 8 → 9 (stored grid values change for R3 cells that were previously contaminated).

**Predicted impact** (from probe diagnosis):
  R3 v_max: 213% → likely <0.01% (sampling fix removes contaminated cell values; interpolation residual takes over and is small for the smooth R3 surface)
  R3 s_max: 1283 J/kgK → likely <1 J/kgK (s is smooth across the critical point — the huge residual was sampling, not interpolation)
  R3 T_max: 81 K → likely <50 mK

If predictions hold, R3 conformance reaches G13-15 perm everywhere within Kunick's critical box, without needing patch widening or basis change.

Parent: CoolProp-foi.9.  Stacked on foi.9.5 (#2938) + foi.9.9 (#2939).
close_reason: Closed per user: IF97 conformance is at an acceptable state for now. Current SVDSBTL+IF97 accuracy (post foi.9.1-4, 6-8, 11 and pd6) meets the practical needs of the project; remaining items (atlas splits at R2/R3 + R1/R3 boundaries, TOMS748 h-inversion in IF97 sampling, Hermite bicubic backbone, multi-piece grids, L/G h_c split, separate HT region, Sphinx accuracy page, per-property fail-map) are conformance polish that can be reopened or refiled if/when stricter G13-15 conformance becomes a goal.

title: Atlas split — divide SUPER_R3 at IF97 R1/R3 boundary h_R1R3(p) = h_IF97(623.15, p)
description:
Closes the remaining IF97-R1 conformance gap after foi.9.5 landed.

**Diagnosis (2026-05-20):**

After foi.9.5 split SUPER at p_B23(T), SUPER_R3 still spans BOTH IF97 R1 (T<623.15) AND IF97 R3 (T>=623.15) territory.  At supercritical pressure ~28 MPa, R1 occupies η ∈ [0, 0.63] of SUPER_R3 and R3 occupies the remaining 0.37.  The shared SVD has to encode compressed-liquid behavior in the R1 half AND dense supercritical behavior in the R3 half — fitting both with shared U/V_S basis loses accuracy in both, especially in R1 territory.

Fail-map evidence: of 245 R1 v fails post-foi.9.5, 244 live in SUPER_R3 (max 0.164%); only 1 lives in the subcritical-p LIQUID region (max 0.008%).  LIQUID — same IF97 R1 physics but its own dedicated SVD — is essentially clean.  This is a clean signal that splitting SUPER_R3 will collapse R1 fails to LIQUID's floor.

**Implementation sketch (matches foi.9.5 pattern):**

1. Add if97_h_R1R3_curve helper: 64 log-spaced p knots over [pcrit, p_max_sup], h_IF97(623.15 K, p_k) at each, fitted as a CubicSplineCurve.  Like build_h_B23_curve but on the T=623.15 K isotherm instead of the p_B23(T) curve.
2. In ph_subcritical IF97 path, replace single SUPER_R3 with:
   - SUPER_R1_super: p > pcrit, h_floor(p, T_min) ≤ h < h_R1R3(p)
   - SUPER_R3_proper: p > pcrit, h_R1R3(p) ≤ h < h_B23(p)
   SUPER_R2 unchanged.
3. Bump cache rev 7 → 8.
4. Drop the apply_kink_smoothing flag from SUPER_R3 (no longer needed — kink is now a region edge).

**Expected impact:** R1 v fails in SUPER_R3 collapse from 244/251 (max 0.164%) to LIQUID-like floor (≤ 1 fail at ≤ 0.01%).  R3 fails essentially unchanged (R3 territory becomes its own SVD; should perform similarly or slightly better).

**Cost:** Adds one region per fluid (3 SUPER sub-regions for IF97 instead of 2).  Cache size grows ~30% per fluid.

Parent: CoolProp-foi.9.  Stacked on foi.9.5 (PR #2938).
close_reason: Closed per user: IF97 conformance is at an acceptable state for now. Current SVDSBTL+IF97 accuracy (post foi.9.1-4, 6-8, 11 and pd6) meets the practical needs of the project; remaining items (atlas splits at R2/R3 + R1/R3 boundaries, TOMS748 h-inversion in IF97 sampling, Hermite bicubic backbone, multi-piece grids, L/G h_c split, separate HT region, Sphinx accuracy page, per-property fail-map) are conformance polish that can be reopened or refiled if/when stricter G13-15 conformance becomes a goal.

title: Atlas split — divide SUPER at IF97 R2/R3 boundary h_B23(p)
description:
**Subagent-investigation outcome (2026-05-20, foi.9 fan-out):** Three of four independent architectural-knob investigations converged on the same diagnosis — R3 conformance failure is *structural*, caused by the IF97 R2/R3 derivative kink (p = p_B23(T) curve) crossing our SBTL SUPER region interior.  No basis-function change (higher-order Hermite, B-splines, Chebyshev, Padé, patch-SVD) closes the gap because the underlying sampled function is not smooth across the kink.

Fix: convert the kink from an interior feature to a region boundary.  Atlas-level change; keep SVD + Hermite cubic kernel unchanged.

Implementation sketch:
1. Add inverse  T_B23(p) = 572.54459862746 + sqrt((p_MPa - 13.91883776670) / 0.10192970039326e-2)  (closed-form inverse of IF97 p_B23(T)).
2. Sample h_B23(p_i) = h_IF97(T_B23(p_i), p_i) at ~64 log-spaced p knots over [pcrit, p_max_for_B23 = 100 MPa] using forward IF97.
3. Build a region::CubicSplineCurve from those (p, h_B23) samples.
4. In src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp ph_subcritical, split SUPER:
   - SUPER_R3 (low-h, dense supercritical): h_floor(p) ≤ h < h_B23(p)
   - SUPER_R2 (high-h, supercritical gas + R5): h_B23(p) ≤ h ≤ h_ceiling(p)
5. For p > 100 MPa (above the kink curve's valid range): no split needed — entire slab is dense supercritical extrapolation of R3.  Use SUPER_R3 only there.
6. Bump cache rev 6 → 7.

Expected impact (subagent prediction): R3 v_max 112% → permissibility-level (~0.001%), R3 T_max 188 K → < 25 mK.  Should fully close the R3 gap.  R1/R2 unchanged from this PR (those have separate fixes).

Risks: B23 curve coordinates must align numerically between R3-side and R2-side sampling.  Mitigation: snap shared edge knots between sub-regions; mirrors what PR E did at the saturation dome.

Parent: CoolProp-foi.9.  Companion PRs: foi.9.6 (quintic Hermite for R1/R2), foi.9.7 (IF97-boundary smoothing of M).
close_reason: Closed per user: IF97 conformance is at an acceptable state for now. Current SVDSBTL+IF97 accuracy (post foi.9.1-4, 6-8, 11 and pd6) meets the practical needs of the project; remaining items (atlas splits at R2/R3 + R1/R3 boundaries, TOMS748 h-inversion in IF97 sampling, Hermite bicubic backbone, multi-piece grids, L/G h_c split, separate HT region, Sphinx accuracy page, per-property fail-map) are conformance polish that can be reopened or refiled if/when stricter G13-15 conformance becomes a goal.

title: Thread-safety: debug_level + error/warning string globals
description:
Globals in src/CoolProp.cpp:58-60 are read/written from multiple threads without synchronization:
- static int debug_level (read in 30+ hot-path sites)
- static std::string error_string, warning_string

set_error_string / set_warning_string write from any exception handler. get_global_param_string('errstring') / 'warnstring' uses non-atomic read-then-clear (CoolProp.cpp:~1008) which:
1. loses errors when two threads race to set them
2. allows two threads to both observe the same error then both clear it
3. is a data race on std::string itself (UB)

Also static int deriv_counter at src/Backends/Helmholtz/HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend.cpp:41 — non-atomic ++.

Fix:
- debug_level → std::atomic<int>
- error_string / warning_string → either std::mutex around set/get, or thread_local (probably better — each thread captures its own error)
- deriv_counter → std::atomic<int>

Filed as part of broader thread-safety audit; humid-air fix landed separately.

title: Thread-safety investigation: humid air init race + broader audit
description:
A few races on initialization were fixed (PR #2800: fluid library lazy init Meyers singletons + once_flag pattern; Cubics + PCSAFT singletons converted to Meyers). The Rust GUI tests previously needed --test-threads=1 due to fluid-init race, which is now fixed.

User reports another race likely exists in humid air (HAPropsSI / HumidAirProp) initialization, observed during GUI testing. With increasing thread use across CoolProp consumers (Tauri GUI, parallel test runners, multi-threaded Python scripts), a broader audit is now warranted. The code is mostly thread-safe, but not entirely.

Scope:
1. Locate and fix the humid air init race observed in GUI tests
2. Audit all other singleton/global mutable state in CoolProp for races (caches, fluid name maps, mixture tables, derivative caches, lazy-init structures)
3. Fix or document any unsafe patterns found
4. Add a regression test that exercises concurrent first-call paths
notes: Fix landed in PR #2831 (commit 0e148969 on ib/thread-safety-investigation): Water/Air/WaterIF97, s_virial_cache, s_alpha0_cache, s_ref → thread_local; FlagUse*Correlations → atomic<int>; new [HAPropsSI][threadsafe] regression test (16 threads × 50 iters; 610/800 broken without fix, 0/800 with fix). Full Catch suite still passes (57,331 assertions, 126 cases, 10 skipped). Follow-up issues filed: CoolProp-? (debug_level/error_string), CoolProp-6nh (Configuration), CoolProp-anq (TabularBackends + REFPROP).
close_reason: Landed in PR #2831 (commit 9787df3e on master): per-thread Water/Air/WaterIF97 backends via thread_local, atomic flags for the Use*Correlations toggles, [HAPropsSI][threadsafe] regression test, and a [!benchmark] for hot-path/fresh-thread cost. Broader audit findings filed as separate issues: CoolProp-6nh (Configuration singleton), CoolProp-anq (TabularBackends + REFPROP), and the in-flight P1 for debug_level/error_string globals.

title: Tier 1.1 — Switch dev_clangformat.yml to PR-only trigger
description:
dev_clangformat.yml currently runs on push to master, so master keeps going red after every merge that introduces a violation (latest example: src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests.cpp after commit 8ea3624). Combined with branch protection (out of scope), PR-only triggering is what actually stops violations from landing.

Action:
- Edit .github/workflows/dev_clangformat.yml: remove the push trigger entirely (or restrict to non-master branches like dev_checks)
- Verify the existing dev/ci/clang-format.sh logic still works for PR-only mode (it already takes BASE_SHA + HEAD_SHA)

Acceptance: workflow only runs on pull_request events; master CI no longer goes red on retroactive violations.
notes: Merged to master in 90e4f528 (#2786) — clang-format dropped push triggers, PR-only.

title: Fix R-1336mzz(Z) enthalpy/entropy reference state mismatch with REFPROP
description:
CI test failure in 'Enthalpy and entropy reference state' check (REFPROPMixtureBackend.cpp:2338-2339) for R-1336mzz(Z), introduced in PR #2781 (NIST IR 8570 mixture pairs).

Failing assertions:
- CHECK( std::abs(DH / h_RP) < 0.01 ) — got 0.901
- CHECK( std::abs(DS / s_RP) < 0.01 ) — got 0.766

Values:
- h_CP := 36244.058,  h_RP := 367081.535
- s_CP := 349.438,    s_RP := 1495.521
- delta_a1 := -22.6138265880399167
- delta_a2 := 14.6859102235818870
- RPName: R1336MZZZ, RRP=8.31445980, RCP=8.314462618

The CoolProp ideal-gas Helmholtz spec (IdealGasHelmholtzCP0PolyT with c=4.0 plus two PlanckEinstein terms at v=736, 2299) is producing h/s that disagree with REFPROP's R1336MZZZ at the reference state by an order of magnitude. Likely a reference-state offset (a1/a2) or cp0 coefficient discrepancy with the REFPROP FLD file.

Run: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/actions/runs/24936263809/job/73022241015
close_reason: Fixed in fe4f40f4: set Lead a1=-22.6138365893630073, a2=14.6859157463353789 derived from REFPROP PX0 (R1336MZZZ) minus CP0PolyT integration constants. Local 'Check REFPROP and CoolProp values agree' suite passes (1016 assertions); existing [R1336mzzZ] and [NIST-IR-8570] tests still green.

title: SVDSBTL: add DT-indexed surface to handle P(D,T) natively (replaces BICUBIC #1301 attempt)
description: Closing PR #2892 (BICUBIC #1301 attempt) confirmed: patching BICUBIC's invert_single_phase_y is the wrong layer to solve this. The right fix is a DT-indexed surface in SVDSBTL — P, H, S, U as functions of (D, T) — so the operation is a direct table lookup, not an inversion problem. Eliminates all the cell-walking, bisection-on-lower-edge-column, and root-selection-near-saturation issues the BICUBIC inverter has. Aligns with the team direction (BICUBIC -> SVDSBTL migration). yufang67's #1301 (CO2 supercritical low-density returning -760 MPa) becomes a non-issue: the DT table either covers the state (correct value) or doesn't (clean miss, fall back to HEOS source). Reference material: yufang67's (T, rho) sweep reproduction with matched color range showed the rectangular-band error pattern is exactly the symptom of inverting on a PT-indexed table; the figure is in /tmp/fig1301_compare.png if reviving for design discussion.
acceptance_criteria:
Reproduce yufang67's (T, ρ) sweep from issue #1301 — same fluid (CO2), same probe grid, same color range — and emit a comparison plot:

  Panel 1: BICUBIC results (current master) — must show the documented rectangular-band failure pattern, including the supercritical low-density cell where P returns ~ −760 MPa.
  Panel 2: SVDSBTL DT-indexed results (this PR) — must show clean, machine-precision agreement with HEOS truth across the whole (T, ρ) box, no rectangular bands, no negative-P cells.

The plot is the headline deliverable for closing both #1301 and i7j.  Commit it to Web/coolprop/ as part of the SVDSBTL validation docs so the regression visualisation is browsable, and reference it from the i7j close note and the #1301 close comment.

Save the rendering script (Python under dev/ or Web/coolprop/_gen/) alongside the plot so future regressions can re-run it.  Reference figure at /tmp/fig1301_compare.png shows the BICUBIC-master pattern to reproduce.
notes:
DESIGN (2026-05-24, after design discussion):

Region geometry: copy ph_subcritical's 3-region structure (LIQUID / VAPOR / SUPER) into a new dt_subcritical preset in src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp.  Primary axis = T (LOG or LINEAR per per-region tuning), secondary = D.  Region tests are direct: LIQUID is D > ρ_sat,L(T), VAPOR is D < ρ_sat,V(T), SUPER is T > T_crit.  Two-phase dome interior routes to source via lever rule (mirrors existing PH dome handling).

Anomaly handling for water / heavy water (the only real complexity):

In (D, T) space the LIQUID region is bounded below by ρ_sat,L(T).  For fluids with a density anomaly (water at T_anom ≈ 277.13 K, heavy water at T_anom ≈ 284 K), ρ_sat,L(T) is non-monotone — it rises from triple to T_anom (maximum) then falls to ρ_c.  A horizontal line in (D, T) at D just below the maximum crosses the sat curve twice, carving a peninsula out of LIQUID.  SVD cells straddling that boundary can't represent the discontinuity.

Solution: split LIQUID at T_anom into two sub-regions, each with monotone ρ_sat,L(T).  LIQUID_LO covers [T_triple, T_anom] (slope dρ/dT > 0), LIQUID_HI covers [T_anom, T_critical] (slope dρ/dT < 0).  Both build a standard cubic spline ρ_sat,L(T) boundary curve.  HmassP-style HEOS fallback is NOT used — every cell stays inside the SVD atlas.

Detection: robust auto-detect at preset construction.  Walk the sat curve via QT_INPUTS on a coarse T grid (~50 points), find any sign change of dρ_sat,L/dT, bisect to locate T_anom.  If no sign change exists in [T_triple, T_critical] (the common case — CO2, R245fa, methane, etc.), emit a single LIQUID region as today.  If detected, emit LIQUID_LO + LIQUID_HI.  No hard-coded fluid name list — survives REFPROP source + future fluid additions.

Wire DmassT_INPUTS and DmolarT_INPUTS into SVDSBTLBackend::resolve_point_'s pair dispatch.  P / H / S / U all become direct evaluations during table build (zero inversion — that's the whole point of DT-indexed).

title: Migrate WASM bindings to be compatible with emcc 5.x
description: emcc 5.0.7 (in emscripten/emsdk:latest as of 2026-05-24) removed the JS-side dispatch that surfaced EMSCRIPTEN_BINDINGS()-registered functions and classes onto the resolved Module instance. Symptoms: build links cleanly, primitive registrations (void/int/float/std::string) still flow through JS, but __embind_register_function and __embind_register_class are absent from the glue — so coolprop.factory, coolprop.AbstractState, coolprop.PropsSI, coolprop.input_pairs etc. are all undefined on 'await Module()'. Surfacing them on Module again likely requires either (a) opting into EMBIND_AOT (emcc generates a separate JS file with the typed bindings at compile time) plus a glue change in CMakeLists.txt around line 1902, or (b) enumerating every binding name in -sEXPORTED_RUNTIME_METHODS (mechanical but tedious — ~60 AS methods + 4 enums + 2 vector types + 9 free functions). Investigate which path Emscripten recommends, port the build flags, and verify test_wasm.mjs still passes. Unblocks lifting the Dockerfile pin.
acceptance_criteria: Local 'docker compose run --rm runner' with FROM emscripten/emsdk:latest (or any current 5.x sha) produces artifacts that pass node test_wasm.mjs. Dockerfile pin in sibling bead can then be removed or relaxed.
notes:
Investigation 2026-05-24: Tried -sEMBIND_AOT=1 (added to CMakeLists.txt line 1934). Build fails at link time with: em++: error: expected to find pattern in input JS: <<< EMBIND_AOT_INVOKERS >>>

Root cause (partial): emcc 5.x defaults DYNAMIC_EXECUTION=1 but dropped the runtime new Function()/eval-based embind invoker JIT from -lembind's JS library. Primitive registrations (__embind_register_void/int/float/std::string) survived, but __embind_register_function and __embind_register_class were removed. Result: build links, but resolved Module instance has zero embind bindings.

EMBIND_AOT was the documented forward path, but the AOT generator at /emsdk/upstream/emscripten/tools/link.py expects to substitute '<<< EMBIND_AOT_INVOKERS >>>' (emitted by libembind.js under '#if EMBIND_AOT'); for our binding shape (60+ class methods + 4 enums + 2 vector types + 9 free functions + a value_object with X-macro fields), the sentinel either never reaches the post-link substitution step or is stripped before it. Upstream issue emscripten-core/emscripten#24540 ('EMBIND_AOT should AOT method callers') confirms AOT coverage is currently incomplete.

Next steps to try: (1) -sEMBIND_AOT=1 -sDYNAMIC_EXECUTION=0 (documented combination); (2) minimal repro to confirm whether MODULARIZE=1 + EXPORT_ES6=1 + EMBIND_AOT=1 is supported together; (3) fall back to enumerating every binding name in -sEXPORTED_RUNTIME_METHODS (~75 entries) and verifying that surfaces them despite the missing runtime dispatch. Until one of these works, the Dockerfile stays pinned to emcc 4.0.12 (bead CoolProp-71a.1).

title: Epic: WASM/Javascript bindings updates (post #2664)
description: Follow-up tidying after #2664 (Expose comprehensive AbstractState functionality and enums to WASM). Covers reproducibility of the dev loop, emcc 5.x compatibility, enum coverage gaps, missing binding-surface symmetry, and test coverage for the new binding surface (mixtures, phase envelope, derivatives). #2664 itself shipped clean against the CI-pinned emsdk 4.0.12; this epic addresses (a) the local-build regression introduced by upstream emcc 5.x dropping JS-side embind dispatch, and (b) the coverage gaps deferred from PR review.

title: bisect_vector(v[i], otherval, j) uses lower-edge column → systematic off-by-one j for cells with strong T-dependence
description: In TabularBackends.h::find_nearest_neighbor (givenkey == xkey branch), the j bisection uses v[i] = otherkey column at the lower-T edge (xvec[i]) rather than interpolating between v[i] and v[i+1] at the actual query T. For cells where the orthogonal-axis dependence is significant (e.g. CO2 PT-table along supercritical isotherms), this systematically lands one cell off. PR #2892 (CoolProp-3ba) works around this by walking +/-3 cells in invert_single_phase_y; a proper fix would interpolate v[i] and v[i+1] at the query xhat before bisecting.

title: Catch2 test fixtures race across processes on hardcoded /tmp paths
description:
Three new [SVDSBTL][cache] tests added in PRs #2961 and #2963 use hardcoded directory names under fs::temp_directory_path(), causing cross-process races whenever two CatchTestRunner instances run concurrently (e.g. preflight in two worktrees, CI parallelism, dev running preflight while CI is also running).

Affected tests (src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTL.cpp):
- '[SVDSBTL][cache][fhp]' SVDSBTL ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY routes default_cache_dir — uses '/tmp/coolprop_svdtables_fhp_resolver'
- '[SVDSBTL][cache][fhp][slow]' SVDSBTL ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY end-to-end build + reload — uses '/tmp/coolprop_svdtables_fhp_e2e'
- '[SVDSBTL][cache][race][4no.2]' write_bytes_atomic is race-safe across threads — uses '/tmp/coolprop_svdtables_atomic_race/race.bin'

Failure modes:
- FHP e2e: runner B's fs::remove_all(tmpdir) wipes runner A's tmpdir mid-test; A's REQUIRE_FALSE(files.empty()) or mtime stability check then fails spuriously.
- 4no.2 thread-race: each runner has its OWN 16 distinct payload byte-values. Across two runners, 32 writers compete; if a runner-B payload wins, runner A's 'result matches one of payloads[]' assertion fails because runner A only knows its own 16 payloads — even though atomic-rename guaranteed no torn bytes.

Note: The PR #2963 atomic write-temp + rename fix is correct AT THE WRITE LEVEL — what fails here is the test fixtures' design, not the production code.

Fix shape:
const fs::path tmpdir = fs::temp_directory_path() /
    ("coolprop_svdtables_fhp_e2e_" + std::to_string(::getpid()));

Same pattern for all three tests. ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY save/restore is already correct via the 'saved' local pattern.

Discovered while pushing ihb/svdsbtl-sat-surrogate (PR #2965); two preflight runs across different worktrees collided and surfaced spurious 4/89 test-case failures.

title: NC near-critical sub-region with POWER(β=1/3) primary axis
description:
Adds a POWER axis-transform variant (β=1/3, uses std::cbrt; crowds grid lines toward the high-end anchor) plus NC_LIQUID/NC_VAPOR/NC_SUPER sub-regions in SurfacePresets for HEOS/REFPROP source backends.

## Why
Python diagnostics on R245fa/Water/CO2 (HEOS HmassP rho, NT=200/NR=800 Cheb, rank=20):
- log primary @ p_hi=0.99999·pc: max err 2.6e-4 to 3.0e-4 across fluids
- pow1/3 primary @ same p_hi: max err 1.1e-9 to 8.1e-8 — 1000-100000× better
- pow1/3 max stays flat in p_hi from 0.999·pc to 0.999999·pc (within 1 ppm of pc)
- p99 floor ~1e-9 — only the few outermost cells benefit, but those WERE the bench-dominating failures

## Result
- R245fa near-pc bench should drop from ~1e-4 to ~1e-7 max
- Critical patch can shrink from 0.05·pc-wide (Water default) to ~1 ppm wide
- IF97 unchanged (already passes G13-15)

## Scope (this bead)
- AxisScale::POWER variant in include/CoolProp/region/AxisTransform.h
- NC_LIQUID/NC_VAPOR/NC_SUPER in src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp
- Serializer cache invalidation (kRevision bump)
- Catch2 tests for axis round-trip + NC conformance probe

Tied to CoolProp-y9a (geometric BULK/STRIP split attempt — reverted) and CoolProp-6oe (per-region sizing harness).
close_reason: Merged via PR #2967 (NC near-critical sub-regions with POWER(β=1/3) primary axis). Final scope: NC_LIQUID + NC_VAPOR on [0.9·pc, (1-1e-10)·pc] with POWER axis, NC_SUPER on [(1+1e-10)·pc, 1.1·pc] with POWER_LO axis, T_hi_mult clamp for fluids with T_max≈Tc+ε (R245fa), critpatch sidecar cache v1→v2, pc-strip routes through critical patch. All HEOS/REFPROP source backends.

title: SVDSBTL: add ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY config (parallel to ALTERNATIVE_TABLES_DIRECTORY)
description:
The legacy tabular backend honors include/Configuration.h's ALTERNATIVE_TABLES_DIRECTORY config key to override ~/.CoolProp/Tables — used by callers (CI, docs builds, parallel test runs) that need their own cache directory without colliding on the per-user default.

SVDSBTL has no equivalent: surface serialization in src/Backends/SVDSBTL/SVDSBTLBackend.cpp uses ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables/ unconditionally. This causes collisions when multiple processes (e.g., a docs build + a separate test run + interactive experimentation) hit the same cache files concurrently.

Implementation:
  - Add ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY to include/Configuration.h's X-macro list (next to ALTERNATIVE_TABLES_DIRECTORY).
  - In SVDSBTLBackend.cpp's cache path resolver, consult get_config_string(ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY) first; fall back to ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables/ when empty.
  - Mirror the existing ALTERNATIVE_TABLES_DIRECTORY semantics (trailing-slash handling, mkdir-on-write).
  - Document in Web/coolprop/SVDSBTL.rst (or whichever page covers SVDSBTL config).

Consumers:
  - Docs build (Web/coolprop/SVDSBTLValidation.ipynb) — see CoolProp-4no.4
  - CI matrix builds running in parallel
  - Anyone running SVDSBTL with a non-standard surface set

Acceptance:
  - Setting the config to /tmp/foo causes the next surface build/load to use /tmp/foo/, leaving ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables/ untouched.
  - Existing default behavior unchanged when the config is empty.
close_reason: Superseded by PR #2961 (bd CoolProp-fhp), merged 2026-05-23 as fd817bb4d. API: CP.set_config_string(CP.ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY, '/path'). Env var: COOLPROP_ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY. Reads/writes both .svd.bin.z surfaces and .critpatch.bin sidecars to the configured directory. CoolProp-4no.4 (docs notebook uses dedicated dir) is now unblocked.

title: SVDSBTL: Hydrogen surface build fails — p > p_critical by ULP-class margin
description:
The SVDSBTL surface lazy-build path for Hydrogen throws during AbstractState construction:

    ValueError: Pressure to PQ_flash [1296357.6 Pa] may not be above the numerical critical point of 1296357.60605531 Pa

Repro (against current branch HEAD 5d2a2e12e, in any env where the wrapper is built):

    import CoolProp.CoolProp as CP
    AS = CP.AbstractState('SVDSBTL&HEOS', 'Hydrogen')   # raises

The surface-build code is picking a sample pressure of 1296357.6 Pa (literally Pc rounded to 1 decimal), which is then rejected by the PQ_flash precondition because the numerical Pc for Hydrogen is 1296357.60605531 Pa. So 'sample at pc' and 'PQ_flash demands p < pc' are colliding at ULP-class scale.

Two reasonable fixes:
  (a) Cap sample pressures at p_critical() * (1 - eps) when constructing the saturation samples used during surface build, with eps on the order of 1e-9 to leave PQ_flash headroom.
  (b) Sample on the saturation side via QT_INPUTS only (avoid PQ_flash entirely for the dome traversal), which is the more robust shape.

Discovered while building the SVDSBTL validation docs notebook (Web/coolprop/SVDSBTLValidation.ipynb), which queries all 6 fluids (Water, Argon, Hydrogen, R1234yf, R245fa, D6). Water and Argon succeed; Hydrogen reproduces deterministically. R1234yf/R245fa/D6 not yet tested but worth checking — fluids with low Pc (e.g. D6 at 9.6 bar) may have similar tight-margin behavior at neighboring sample sites.

Acceptance:
  - AbstractState('SVDSBTL&HEOS', 'Hydrogen') constructs without raising.
  - Surface builds and serializes to ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables/.
  - All 6 fluids in the validation notebook render their heatmaps.
notes:
2026-05-23: cannot reproduce in isolated cache. After rebasing onto master (HEAD fd817bb4d, includes PR #2961 ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY) and re-running the docs validation notebook against a fresh empty cache directory at ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables-docs/, ALL 6 fluids built successfully:

  Cold-build wall-clock (n_jobs=6, cores=8):
    Water     60.96 s
    Argon     38.29 s
    Hydrogen  21.66 s    <- previously failed
    R1234yf   21.69 s    <- previously failed
    R245fa    81.84 s
    D6        16.69 s

  Warm re-run: 1.14 s for Water, sub-second for the others.

Theory: the original failure was cache corruption / concurrent-write race from other processes hitting ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables/ during the build. With a dedicated cache directory (via the new ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY config), the failure does not recur. R1234yf's reported Pc is also slightly different now (33.844 bar vs 33.822 previously) — may indicate a fluid-data refresh that incidentally moved the build-time sampler away from the ULP-boundary case.

Action: closing-eligible, but leaving open one cycle in case the failure recurs under load.
close_reason: Resolved by #2963 (atomic write-temp + rename for cache files, commit 05911a277). The 2026-05-23 notes flagged this as closing-eligible; the failure has not recurred. Underlying race: cache corruption / concurrent-write race against ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables/; fixed by atomic rename.

title: SVDSBTL: data-driven per-region sizing harness (rank, NT, NR) instead of universal defaults
description:
Currently every SVDSBTL region uses the same kDefaultGrid = {NT=200, NR=800, rank=20}, regardless of the region's geometric / physical complexity. This is wasteful:

* R5 (IF97 supercritical-extended slab) is essentially a flat h(T) at fixed p — rank-5 would likely capture it to machine precision, rank-20 is overkill.
* LIQUID/VAPOR near-critical strips (proposed in CoolProp-y9a) have rapid h_sat,L/V variation in a narrow h-range — rank-20 may be undersized for the cusp.
* SUPER region's (∂rho/∂p)_h cusp at the critical singularity won't converge at any practical rank; tracked separately in CoolProp-3c4.

## What

Build a sizing harness that takes a region's sample matrix and reports:
1. Singular value decay σ_k / σ_1 vs k. Recommend rank r* where decay drops below a target threshold (e.g. 1e-6 for rho conformance).
2. Grid-convergence test: build SVDs at NT, NR pairs in (100, 200, 400, 800), measure on-grid max rel-err vs the rank-min-truncated reference. Identify where error plateaus (= grid-saturation point).
3. Per-property analysis: rho (EXP-transformed) vs T/s/u (IDENTITY) likely want different ranks. Allow per-property override in SurfaceSpec.

## Output

Per-region report (CSV or RST):
* recommended (NT, NR, rank) tuple for each (region, property) combination
* current vs recommended cache size delta
* estimated build-time delta

## Scope

* Helper harness lives under dev/ (e.g. dev/svdsbtl_sizing_harness.cpp + .py plot)
* Reports run on-demand, NOT during normal table build
* If recommendations land, SurfaceSpec gets a per-property rank field (currently a single int)
* Default kDefaultGrid stays as a fallback when no override is supplied

## Out of scope

* The CoolProp-3c4 critical-singularity cusp — no rank fixes it
* Runtime adaptive sizing (load-time-fixed only)

## Driven by y9a finding

CoolProp-y9a investigation surfaced that the current universal NT=200, NR=800, rank=20 over-provisions trivial regions (R5) and under-provisions cusp-adjacent ones (near-pc LIQUID/VAPOR strips). Per-region sizing is the lever to address both.
notes:
## 2026-05-23 follow-up after CoolProp-4u9 (NC POWER) landing

The y9a (now 4u9) NC POWER work resolved the near-pc residual to ~1e-9
in NC_LIQUID/VAPOR and ~1e-7 in NC_SUPER, leaving the remaining R245fa
end-to-end bench max at 7.7e-5 — all at the hot-ceiling boundary
(T = T_max - 0.5).  That's the cell type 6oe should target next.

Outstanding fluid-specific hotspots observed during 4u9 validation:

* **Argon** has relatively high subcritical error close to the
  saturated-vapor curve (still well above any user-facing tolerance,
  but visually noticeable in the SVDSBTLValidation notebook's HP panel
  for Argon).  Likely indicates the parent VAPOR region for Argon
  needs more grid density near the sat-V boundary OR a higher rank.
  Worth treating as a 6oe sizing-harness case study — measure the
  on-grid σ-decay + rank-vs-err curve for Argon VAPOR specifically,
  recommend the per-property NT/NR/rank tuple.
* **Water** entropy s in supercritical at T near triple — small-
  denominator artifact, NOT a sizing issue.  Captured by CoolProp-ari.
* **R245fa** hot-ceiling cells — covered by CoolProp-3c4.

## Concrete first deliverable

`dev/svdsbtl_sizing_harness.py`:
1. Construct SurfaceSpec via ph_subcritical / pt_subcritical for a
   given fluid + source.
2. For each region, walk the (NT, NR) Chebyshev × log-p grid the way
   sample_grid() does (or import via pybind11).
3. Run full-rank SVD per region per property; dump σ_k / σ_1, rank-
   vs-err curve, NT/NR-saturation curve.
4. First targets: Argon VAPOR (this bead's motivating case), R5 (rank-
   overkill case), Water LIQUID parent (baseline).

## Per-property rank override (Step 2)

If harness recommendations diverge enough from kDefaultGrid to
matter, SurfaceSpec's single rank field becomes a per-property map
(:CoolProp::parameters → int).  Wire it through SVDBuilder and the
serializer (revision bump on the format change).
close_reason: Merged via PR #2968 (data-driven per-region sizing harness). Sweep harness measures p99 rel-err vs rank/NT/NR per region, drives per-region overrides instead of universal defaults.

title: preflight: gate clang-tidy on filtered signal + code-reviewer reminder banner
description:
Two preflight tweaks to close the gaps that recent PRs surfaced:

1. **clang-tidy noise filter + gating.** Currently preflight runs clang-tidy as informational ("85 findings; informational"). Per issue #2926 the documented noise classes (cppcoreguidelines-avoid-do-while, cert-err58-cpp, cppcoreguidelines-pro-bounds-pointer-arithmetic, etc. -- ~2000 occurrences total, mostly Catch2 macros and REFPROP C-API) dominate the raw output. Filter them out via a NOISE_CHECKS list sourced from #2926, then GATE on the remainder. On PR #2952 the 85 raw findings would reduce to ~5 actionable; the gate would block on those (e.g., the noexcept-on-throwing-function finding CodeRabbit caught).

2. **Code-reviewer reminder banner.** The pre-push hook is a shell script and can't mechanically invoke the superpowers:code-reviewer subagent (which is a Claude Code construct, not a CLI). Add a banner at the end of every preflight run that says "Before `gh pr create`, MUST run the code-reviewer subagent (see CLAUDE.md)". Procedural reminder, not a hard gate. Strengthen the CLAUDE.md convention with explicit checklist wording.

## Why

PR #2952 had 2 CodeRabbit findings (noexcept-on-throwing, NaN silently absorbed by std::max). Finding 1 was caught by clang-tidy but my preflight reported it as informational; I never read the output. Finding 2 is the kind only adversarial review catches; CLAUDE.md says I should invoke code-reviewer before opening a PR but I skipped that step.

## Acceptance

- Preflight runs clang-tidy, applies noise filter, gates on remainder (non-zero failures block push).
- Noise filter list is configurable via .clang-tidy.preflight-ignore or similar (single source of truth).
- Preflight always prints a "CODE REVIEW REMINDER" banner at the end of the summary.
- CLAUDE.md "Pre-PR adversarial review" section gets a numbered checklist + explicit "MUST" wording matching the bd-handling section.
- Self-test: on PR #2952's branch, preflight without the fixes would fail on the 1-2 actionable findings; with the fixes it passes.
close_reason: preflight gating + code-reviewer banner shipped in PR #2956

title: dev: dev/ci/preflight.sh single-script pre-push gate (clang-format + tests + cppcheck + clang-tidy + semgrep)
description:
Build a single ./dev/ci/preflight.sh that runs locally before push and exercises the same checks CI runs. Goal: when preflight passes, CI passes with high probability — eliminating the push -> CI fails -> fix loop that's been costing time on recent SVDSBTL PRs.

## Required checks (must mirror CI)

1. clang-format dry-run against PR base (use existing dev/ci/clang-format.sh)
2. Build CatchTestRunner (assume build_catch configured, auto-configure if not)
3. Catch2 tests — auto-select tag scope based on changed paths:
   - touched src/SBTL/ or include/CoolProp/sbtl/ -> [SBTL]
   - touched src/Backends/SVDSBTL/ or src/Region/ or src/SVD/ -> [SVDSBTL] [SVDComponents] [region]
   - default: full suite (no filter)
   Do NOT narrow to [SVDSBTL] alone when touching src/SBTL/ files — REFPROP-tagged tests live under [SBTL] umbrella tags and CI catches them when local does not.
4. cppcheck on changed cpp files (mirrors CI cppcheck workflow)
5. clang-tidy diff-only on changed cpp files (use existing run-clang-tidy-staged.sh, expanded for working-tree diff vs origin/master)
6. Semgrep with `p/cpp` + `p/security-audit` rulesets on changed files — catches the CodeQL-class findings (path traversal, world-writable file, etc.) that have been slipping through to CI.

## Wiring

- pre-push git hook at .git/hooks/pre-push invokes ./dev/ci/preflight.sh
- pre-push hooks survive `git commit --no-verify` (only `git push --no-verify` bypasses, which agents don't use)
- LOCAL-ONLY: do not add to .github/workflows/ — CI already runs the individual checks. This is purely for catching them before push.
- Document in dev/ci/README.md

## Out of scope

- Updating any CI workflow files
- Modifying the existing pre-commit hook chain (separate bd CoolProp-XXX)

## Acceptance

- ./dev/ci/preflight.sh runs to completion in < 5 minutes on a typical SVDSBTL-touching PR
- Each of the 5 failures from the recent session (clang-format wrap, REFPROP bit-exact compare, serializer unknown subclass throw, CodeQL file permissions, CodeQL path traversal) would have been caught by preflight on the would-be-pushing branch

title: SVDSBTL: investigate near-pc accuracy loss in HmassP deviation maps
description:
Visible in the post-CoolProp-8vg deviation plots regenerated 2026-05-22 (dev/bench_svdsbtl_ph_R245fa.png, dev/bench_svdsbtl_ph_Water.png — these are NOT committed, regenerate via dev/bench_svdsbtl_ph_plot.py): there is a residual error stripe close to p_crit that isn't at the ~1e-12 floor the surrounding cells achieve. Exact side (slightly below p_c vs slightly above p_c) is unclear from the visual; needs explicit diagnosis.

## Candidate causes (in priority order)

1. **Critical-patch boundary misclassification.** The auto-calibrated patch bbox (CoolProp-5ni/-dxd, merged) has tight (T, p) edges; probes JUST outside the bbox fall through to the SVD which has its known cusp-rank limitation near the critical singularity. The visible stripe could be the "epsilon outside the patch" cells where the SVD rank truncation hasn't fully converged. Sanity check: widen the patch by 0.5% on each side and re-plot; if the stripe vanishes, this is it.

2. **eta normalization noise near p_c.** The SuperancillaryBoundaryCurve gives h_sat,L/V at ~1e-13 relative, but their DIFFERENCE (the dome span) vanishes like sqrt(p_c - p) near the critical point. eta = (h - h_L) / (h_V - h_L) inherits the noise blowup as the denominator -> 0. Mitigation: clip eta to a near-critical sentinel (e.g., return NaN when (h_V - h_L) / max(h_L, h_V) falls below a few ULP) and route those cells to the patch instead.

3. **SVD rank truncation cusp.** The (∂ρ/∂p)_h cusp at the critical singularity needs unbounded rank to resolve. Even at rank 20 the error within ~0.1*pc of p_c is multi-percent. The patch is supposed to mask this, but if the patch doesn't quite reach the cusp's natural extent, there's a "patch boundary smear" stripe.

## Investigation plan

1. Regenerate /tmp/bench_svdsbtl_ph_R245fa.csv and inspect rows where rel_err_rho > 1e-6. Group by (p / p_crit) and check the histogram. Confirms side of p_c.
2. For each high-error row, classify: in-patch (rho should be HEOS-exact), in-dome (lever rule), single-phase via SVD (the suspect).
3. If single-phase via SVD near p_c: try widening the patch via critical_patch.bbox override. If error vanishes -> hypothesis 1.
4. If error persists with widened patch: check eta values for those cells. If eta has wild swings (>> 1 or << 0) -> hypothesis 2.
5. If eta is bounded but rho still wrong: rank truncation -> hypothesis 3.

## Acceptance criteria

- Identify and document the mechanism (patch boundary / eta noise / rank cusp).
- Either close the gap (additional patch widening / eta sentinel / rank bump) OR document the residual envelope explicitly as a known limitation tied to the SVD's intrinsic rank-vs-cusp tradeoff.
- Update the deviation plots once mitigation lands.

title: SVDSBTL: parallelise per-cell HEOS sampling during table build (4-8x build-time speedup)
description:
SVDSBTL table-build time is dominated by the per-grid-cell sampling phase. Each surface (HmassP and PT) does NT * NR ~ 200 * 800 = 160,000 source-backend calls per region; with 3 regions per fluid (LIQUID / VAPOR / SUPER) that's ~480,000 calls per fluid for a single (input_pair, source) pair. At ~5-10 us per HEOS call, that's 3-5 s per fluid just for sampling. SVD decomposition + slope build on top of that is sub-second.

The sampling loop is embarrassingly parallel — each (i, j) grid point is independent and only reads from the source backend (no shared mutable state in the sampling phase itself). Adding parallelism here gives a roughly linear speedup up to core count.

## Scope

Wrap the inner grid loop in `src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp` (the sampling lambdas inside `ph_subcritical` / `pt_subcritical`) with a parallel-for that takes a per-thread copy of the source AbstractState. AbstractState is NOT thread-safe (internal caches, _phase / _Q members get mutated on update), so each worker thread needs its own factory-built handle.

## Implementation sketch

- Use `<execution>` parallel algorithms (par or par_unseq) for portability — both libstdc++ and libc++ support std::for_each(par_unseq, ...). On macOS/clang the par_unseq path falls through to seq (the policy is just a hint), so a thread-pool fallback may be needed. Concretely:
    * Option A: std::for_each(std::execution::par, ...) — simplest, available everywhere, but per-iteration overhead on small workloads.
    * Option B: hand-rolled std::thread pool with N = hardware_concurrency() workers each sampling a contiguous (i-row) slice.
    * Option C: OpenMP pragma (already used elsewhere in CoolProp?). Need to check.
- Per-thread source backend instance: factory-construct once per worker thread, reuse across all that thread's (i, j) probes. Tear down at workers' end.
- Output buffer: each thread writes to a non-overlapping slice of the dense NT * NR result matrix; no synchronisation needed.

## Expected impact

- 8-core laptop: ~4-6x build-time speedup per fluid (Amdahl-bounded by the small serial bits — SVD compute, region setup).
- CI runners with 4 cores: ~2-3x.
- The kRevision-bump pain we keep hitting (CoolProp-8vg's rev 10 forced rebuild every developer + CI machine) drops from ~3-5 s/fluid * N_fluids to ~1 s/fluid * N_fluids — meaningful when N_fluids in the test set grows.

## Risks

- HEOS thread safety: each thread needs its own AbstractState; using a single shared handle is wrong (cached _phase / _Q / _T members get torn between threads). The build_surface_for_pair_ factory currently passes a single source ref; refactor to pass a thread-safe sampler closure.
- REFPROP: the FORTRAN runtime is process-global, not thread-safe under SETUPdll. Either gate parallel sampling behind source != "REFPROP", or accept that REFPROP-sourced builds stay serial (most users build REFPROP tables once and cache forever, so this is fine).
- Build-machine memory: each worker thread holds a per-thread HEOS state (~few MB). For 8 threads + 4 fluids that's ~few hundred MB. Negligible on modern dev machines, possibly tight on CI runners with capped containers — needs measurement.

## Test plan

- Existing SVDSBTL test suite must pass unchanged (correctness contract).
- New benchmark: dev/bench_svdsbtl_buildtime.cpp times the construction of SVDSBTL&HEOS for {Water, R245fa, CO2, Methane, Propane} cold (no cache) before/after the parallel patch lands. Assert >= 3x speedup on an 8-core machine.
- Race-condition smoke test: build the same surface 100 times with --enable-thread-sanitizer (TSAN), assert no warnings.

## Out of scope

- GPU sampling (the per-call HEOS cost is too large to amortise PCIe latency; CPU parallelism is the right grain).
- Parallelising the SVD compute itself — Eigen's BDCSVD is already multi-threaded internally.

title: SVDSBTL: serializer plumbing for SuperancillaryBoundaryCurve (HEOS cache round-trip)
description:
HEOS-source SVDSBTL surfaces don't currently survive across sessions. CoolProp-8vg (PR #2951) switched HEOS sat boundaries from cubic-spline to SuperancillaryBoundaryCurve; the serializer was bumped to revision 10 to invalidate stale caches but doesn't know how to round-trip the new curve type. As a result, every fresh process invocation rebuilds every HEOS surface from scratch — ~3-5 s per fluid, dominated by the per-grid-cell HEOS sampling.

This is the followup that lets HEOS surfaces persist alongside the existing IF97 cache flow.

## What needs to change

1. **CurveKind discriminator**: SuperancillaryBoundaryCurve has no entry in the CurveKind tag union that the serializer uses to dispatch (see CubicSplineCurve / ConstantCurve / PiecewiseChebyshevCurve in `src/SBTL/SVDSurfaceSerializer.cpp`). Add a new tag value (kSuperancillary or similar).

2. **save() path**: pack the State POD (already defined on the class — `p_min, p_max, prop_key, Q, output_scale, b_min, b_max`) into the msgpack stream. The surrogate table itself is NOT serialized — it's recomputed on load via build_surrogate_(). All 7 doubles + 1 char + 1 short = ~50 bytes per curve, trivial.

3. **load() path**: 
   - Read the State POD from the msgpack stream.
   - Pull the SuperAncillary handle from the source backend at load time. The deserialize call site knows the fluid_name and source_backend (both already on SVDSurfaceSerializer's load_from_file caller side), so it can construct a fresh AbstractState to grab the SA via the same try_get_superanc helper SatBoundaryFactory uses. Pass the SA into SuperancillaryBoundaryCurve::from_state.
   - On SA-handle-acquisition failure (e.g. cache from a different machine where the SA isn't available), fall back to rebuilding the in-memory surface from scratch — same path as today.

4. **kRevision bump**: 10 → 11 to invalidate rev-10 caches that don't have the SA boundary fields.

## Test plan

- Round-trip test: build a Water HEOS surface, save, load, query at 100 points — assert identical density / enthalpy to ~1e-12.
- Cache persistence test: construct SVDSBTL&HEOS Water, destroy, reconstruct in a fresh test case — second construction should hit the cache and skip surface building (measurable as <100 ms wall time vs first-time ~3-5 s).
- Build-time benchmark: time the rebuild cost for a representative fluid set (Water, R245fa, CO2, Methane) before/after the cache lands. Expected: ~12-20 s -> ~50-200 ms cold-start savings per fluid in the fluid set.

## Out of scope

Surface-the-SA-handle question for REFPROP source: REFPROP doesn't expose a SuperAncillary, so SatBoundaryFactory already falls back to the spline path for REFPROP. No change needed there.

## Parent

CoolProp-8vg (note in SVDSurfaceSerializer.h's kRevision 10 comment block flagged this as the followup).

title: SVDSBTL: linear-interp surrogate for atlas curve_contains (faster sub-critical dispatch)
description:
Sub-critical SVDSBTL median is currently 432 ns/call vs 197 ns/call super-critical (R245fa benchmark). The ~235 ns gap is dominated by SuperAncillary calls inside Region::curve_contains during atlas dispatch — each AABB-hit region calls b_lo->eval(p) + b_hi->eval(p), and the SA-backed h_sat_L / h_sat_V curves (post-CoolProp-8vg) cost ~80 ns each.

curve_contains only needs to decide which side of the saturation curve the probe is on — 1e-6 relative accuracy suffices. to_normalized (which computes the eta normalization the SVD consumes) is the precision-critical caller and must continue to use the SA path; otherwise the boundary noise propagates into the SVD reconstruction and undoes the ~3000x Water / ~100x R245fa accuracy win from CoolProp-8vg.

## Design

Dual-eval on BoundaryCurve:
- eval(p) -- precise, no change in behavior. Used by Region::to_normalized and Region::from_normalized.
- eval_fast(p) -- new virtual; default impl forwards to eval(p) so existing spline / constant curves are unaffected. SuperancillaryBoundaryCurve overrides with a linear-interp lookup over a precomputed table.
- Region::curve_contains switches to eval_fast on both b_lo and b_hi.

## Surrogate table shape

- 4096 log-spaced points over the curve's [p_min, p_max] (~32 KB per curve; 2 curves per subcritical region * 2 regions = 128 KB per fluid -- negligible).
- Build at SuperancillaryBoundaryCurve construction by calling eval() (the precise SA path) at each table p. One-shot cost ~300 us per curve at table-build time, paid once.
- eval_fast: compute fractional log(p) bucket index, linear-interp between adjacent table values. ~5-10 ns.

## Expected impact

Sub-critical SVDSBTL ns/call: ~432 -> ~250 ns median. Super-critical unchanged (no SA in curve_contains there). Accuracy unchanged (eta still computed via precise eval).

## Test plan

1. eval_fast vs eval at 1024 random p in [p_min, p_max] -- assert max rel-err < 1e-5.
2. curve_contains agreement: 10k random (p, h) probes -- decisions match between fast and precise paths in 99.99%+ of cases (allow disagreement within 1e-5 strip near the curve, which atlas-misses would route to dome blend anyway).
3. Re-run dev/bench_svdsbtl_ph.cpp on R245fa + Water -- assert sub-critical median drops below 300 ns.

## Follow-up to PR #2951 (CoolProp-8vg)

title: SVDSBTL&IF97: extend coverage to Region 5 (1073.15-2273.15 K, p<=50 MPa)
description:
The SVDSBTL&IF97 preset's SUPER region currently covers T up to Tmax_eos - margin, which is well below IF97 Region 5's temperature range. As a result the conformance tests have zero R5 hits and the G13-15 Table 11 conformance claim doesn't actually exercise R5.

## Scope

Add a SUPER_R5 region to the SVDSBTL&IF97 preset:
- T ∈ [1073.15, 2273.15] K
- p ≤ 50 MPa (per IAPWS R7-97 Table 1)
- Single region (no internal subdivision needed — R5 is one closed-form expansion in IF97)
- Sampling via IF97Backend (already has R5 implementation)
- Property set matches the existing SUPER region (T, rho, h, s, w, eta, lambda)

## Implementation sketch

1. `presets::ph_supercritical_if97` and `presets::pt_supercritical_if97` get a new region appended via `RegionAtlas::add` after the existing SUPER_R1_super/SUPER_R3_proper/SUPER_R2 chain.
2. Boundary curves:
   - PT: `a_lo, a_hi = 0, 50e6` Pa; `b_lo, b_hi = 1073.15, 2273.15` K (both ConstantCurve).
   - HmassP: `a_lo, a_hi = 0, 50e6` Pa; `b_lo, b_hi` = isotherm h at 1073.15 / 2273.15 K via CubicSplineCurve through 64 knots.
3. SVD rank: rank-12 likely sufficient (R5 expansion is low-order; smooth in log-p, monotone in T) — tune empirically.
4. Bump SVDSurfaceSerializer kRevision (10 -> 11) to invalidate stale rev-10 caches.
5. Conformance tests: probe a handful of (T, p) states in R5 (e.g., the IAPWS G13-15 Table 11 reference values for R5 if any; otherwise interior points) and assert max ΔT < 25 mK / max Δs < 0.4 J/(kg·K).

## Cost

- Table-build: ~5 s additional per build (one more region's NT×NR sampling via IF97).
- Disk: ~0.5-1 MB per cache file.
- Lookup: O(0) extra (one more atlas AABB check, branch-friendly).
- Memory: ~0.5 MB additional per fluid loaded.

## Out of scope

R5 has no HEOS counterpart in CoolProp for water (Wagner & Pruss covers 273-1273 K), so this is IF97-only. SVDSBTL&HEOS surfaces unchanged.

## Notes

Discovered while regenerating R245fa + Water deviation plots after the polish-fix (PR #2949) + SuperAncillary-boundary work on branch ihb/svdsbtl-superanc-boundary. The conformance plot at supercritical p showed a hard cutoff above the SUPER region's T_max with no R5 hits.
close_reason: SVDSBTL&IF97 SUPER_R5 region landed in PR #2957; conformance fail-map now has 50000 R5 hits (was 0), R5 within G13-15 budgets on every metric.

title: SVDSBTL: boundary-curve accuracy ceiling (LIQUID ~1e-8) — Superancillary-based curve is the path
description:
**Problem.**  In the LIQUID region of any HEOS-source SVDSBTL preset (verified on R245fa: BICUBIC&HEOS median rho rel-err 1.5e-10, SVDSBTL&HEOS median 1.4e-8, ratio ~100x), SVDSBTL is ~2 orders of magnitude WORSE than BICUBIC despite a smooth function and plenty of rank-20 SVD headroom.

**Root cause.**  Coordinate-transform residual.  SVDSBTL normalises the secondary axis as eta = (h - h_lo(p)) / (h_hi(p) - h_lo(p)) where h_lo and h_hi are region::CubicSplineCurve fits to HEOS samples (currently 64 knots per boundary, log-spaced in p).  The spline residual against the underlying HEOS is ~1e-8.  When the SVD's smooth rho(eta) function is evaluated at the slightly-wrong eta from the spline, the ~1e-8 input error propagates through to a ~1e-8 rho rel-err — the LIQUID-region floor that BICUBIC avoids by using raw (h, p) cells with no transform.

The same effect affects VAPOR and SUPER on the secondary axis, but liquid is where it shows clearly against an interpolator (BICUBIC) doing 1e-10 — for VAPOR the SVD rank residual dominates anyway.

## Two-stage plan

### Stage 1 (interim quick win): Densify cubic-spline knots 64 -> 256

Bump knot count in region::CubicSplineCurve build calls inside src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp.  Spline error scales as n^-4, so 4x knots -> 256x tighter residual (~1e-8 -> ~1e-10).  Per-curve memory: 256 * 8 bytes * 2 (knots + spline coeffs) ~= 4 KB.  Per-fluid table footprint grows by ~30 KB across the typical 4-6 boundary curves of a preset.  No API change.  Estimated 5 LOC tweak per call site + measurement.

Acceptance for stage 1: R245fa LIQUID SVDSBTL median rho rel-err drops from 1.4e-8 to <1e-10 (matching BICUBIC).  Per-fluid table footprint within +10% of current size.  No regression on the IF97 G13-15 conformance figure.

### Stage 2 (end state): Piecewise-Hermite + Chebyshev-bubble boundary curve

Move beyond cubic splines to a basis that converges *spectrally* within each piece while preserving C^1 continuity at piece boundaries.  Same C^1 guarantee as cubic spline, but error decays exponentially in basis order N rather than as h^4.

**Mathematical structure.**  On each piece [x_i, x_{i+1}] mapped to t in [-1, 1], decompose:

  f_i(t) = H_i(t) + sum_{n=0..K} c_n^(i) * B_n(t)

where:

* H_i(t) is a **cubic Hermite** chosen to satisfy four boundary conditions (value + slope at t = ±1).  Slopes shared with neighbours -> C^1 automatic by construction.  Same logic as the per-mode Hermite kernel already used in the SVD itself (include/CoolProp/svd/Hermite1D.h).

* B_n(t) is a **bubble basis** satisfying B_n(±1) = B'_n(±1) = 0 for all n.  Standard choice: B_n(t) = (1 - t^2)^2 * T_n(t), where T_n is the Chebyshev polynomial of the first kind.  The (1 - t^2)^2 envelope kills both value and first derivative at the endpoints; T_n inside the envelope gives spectral convergence.

* Coefficients c_n^(i) come from a constrained least-squares fit to the samples on piece i.  Hermite slopes either come from a global tridiagonal solve (natural-cubic-spline-style for C^2) or are supplied externally (e.g. analytic slopes when available).

* Sparse storage: each piece carries 4 Hermite + ~K bubble coefficients.  For target accuracy ~1e-12, K ~= 16 suffices for smooth functions.  Total ~20 doubles per piece vs the current ~2 doubles per knot for cubic splines.

This is the standard spectral-element / hp-FEM construction reused — proven to give C^1 continuity at interfaces with spectral convergence within pieces.

**Implementation.**  New class region::PiecewiseHermiteChebyshevCurve : public region::BoundaryCurve.  Drop-in replacement for CubicSplineCurve (same interface: eval(x), eval_da(x), bounds()).  Construction takes (samples_x, samples_y, n_pieces, K_chebyshev) and computes the bubble-basis coefficients + Hermite slopes via a single linear-algebra pass at build time.

**Acceptance.**  Spectral convergence demonstrated empirically: doubling K halves log(residual) on a smooth test function.  R245fa LIQUID median rho rel-err <= 1e-12 (effectively machine precision).  No regression on the IF97 G13-15 conformance figure or the auto-calibrated critical-patch bbox sizes.  Per-fluid table footprint within +25% of the densified-cubic-spline interim.

## Related

CoolProp-3c4 (rho peak rel-err at extreme region corners — boundary-curve residual is one contributor), CoolProp-foi.10 (per-property conformance fail-map — LIQUID failures cluster along the R1/R3 isotherm boundary curve, same root cause).
notes:
**Attempted three approaches, all regressed against master's 64-knot cubic spline baseline.** Findings preserved here as the negative-result record for the next attempt.

## Attempts and outcomes (measured R245fa LIQUID + IF97 R3 ΔT)

|                         Approach         | R245fa LIQUID max | IF97 R3 ΔT max | Verdict     |
|------------------------------------------|------------------:|---------------:|-------------|
| **Master**: 64-knot cubic spline         |          4.1e-5   |       20 mK    | Baseline    |
| Densified 256-knot cubic spline          |          4.0e-5   |  106,000 mK    | IF97 catastrophic — kinks oscillate |
| C^0 piecewise Chebyshev (8 × deg 16)     |          8.95e-4  |      544 mK    | Both regressed (C^0 kinks in η normalisation) |
| C^1 Hermite + Chebyshev-bubble (K = 12)  |          1.1e-3   |      947 mK    | Sub-spectral convergence |
| C^1 Hermite + Chebyshev-bubble (K = 24)  |          2.17e-3  |     1948 mK    | LSQ ill-conditioned at high K |

## Why Hermite + Chebyshev-bubble fails (negative result)

The bubble basis B_n(t) = (1 - t^2)^2 * T_n(t) is forced to vanish (both value and first derivative) at both piece endpoints.  Algebraically this loses **four degrees of freedom** to the envelope on every piece.  Consequences:

* **Sub-spectral convergence at low K.**  For sin(x) on [-π/2, π/2] with K=12 bubble modes, the bubble coefficients decay roughly geometrically with ratio ~0.5 per index — but starting from c_1 ~ 4, you need K ~ 25 to reach ~1e-10 fit residual.  Plain Chebyshev (without the bubble envelope) hits machine precision at K ~ 16.

* **Ill-conditioning at high K.**  The bubble basis functions (1 - t^2)^2 * T_n become near-linearly-dependent as n grows: the (1 - t^2)^2 envelope kills most of the 'high-frequency' content near the endpoints where T_n's distinguishing oscillations live.  At K = 24 the normal-equations matrix is already singular to working precision; LSQ produces garbage coefficients that get magnified between sample points.

* **The Hermite endpoint slopes are wrong by construction.**  Natural-cubic-spline tridiagonal solve forces M_0 = M_N = 0 (zero second derivative at outer knots).  For functions whose endpoints have nontrivial curvature (sin(x), exp(x), any sat curve at p_triple / p_critical), the spline-derived slopes are biased — and the bubble basis CANNOT correct this because it vanishes (value + slope) at the endpoints.  This bias dominates the residual along the curve.

## Why C^0 piecewise Chebyshev fails

The existing region::PiecewiseChebyshevCurve gives spectral convergence within each piece but only C^0 at piece interfaces.  When that curve drives the secondary-axis η normalisation, the derivative-jump at every piece boundary propagates through to the SVD's grid sampling — the (xi, eta) of each grid point sits on the WRONG side of a derivative kink, the SVD's smoothness assumption silently breaks, and the reconstruction blows up at probes near piece boundaries.  Class docstring already warns about this verbatim.

## The right path forward: SuperancillaryBoundaryCurve

The TODO at the top of include/CoolProp/sbtl/SatBoundaryFactory.h (now ~5 years old) calls this out:

> the *true* sat boundaries can be a no-refit view onto the existing fluid superancillary (h_sat(T) chained with T_sat(p) — both already machine-precision Chebyshev expansions on the SuperAncillary class)

The superancillary already provides:
* h_sat,L(T) and h_sat,V(T) as piecewise Chebyshev expansions with C^1 / C^2 continuity at the piece joints (depends on construction).
* T_sat(p) via boost::math::tools::toms748 on the closed-form Antoine equation OR via the superancillary's own p_sat(T) inversion.
* Machine-precision residual against the source HEOS by construction.

A new region::SuperancillaryBoundaryCurve class would compose those two — h_sat(p) = h_sat(T_sat(p)) — and inherit machine-precision residual end-to-end.  Needs:

1. Public accessor on HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend for get_superanc() (currently protected — see HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend.h:65).
2. New SuperancillaryBoundaryCurve : BoundaryCurve subclass that wraps a SuperAncillary handle + side index + property and dispatches eval / eval_da through the SuperAncillary's own Clenshaw machinery.
3. Same factory entry points in SatBoundaryFactory; same return type (BoundaryCurve); same SVDSurface consumer.

**Note**: SuperancillaryBoundaryCurve only works for sat curves, not isotherm floors / ceilings.  For those, the right path is still a piecewise basis fit — but with a basis that doesn't have the bubble envelope problem.  Two candidates:

* **Piecewise rational** (rational Chebyshev): no value/derivative constraints baked into the basis; can use Lagrange multipliers / Karush-Kuhn-Tucker on the LSQ to enforce C^1 at piece joints.
* **Sinc / spectral DCT on piecewise-Chebyshev nodes** with global C^1 constraint as a post-fit projection (project the K^2 unconstrained coefficients onto the (K^2 - 2*(n_pieces-1))-dim subspace that satisfies the C^1 constraints at interior joints).

Either is substantially more involved than what I attempted in this round.

## Branch state

Branch ihb/svdsbtl-densify-bcurve-knots deleted; PR #2948 closed.  Master 64-knot cubic spline baseline restored.  Boundary-curve residual (~1e-8 in LIQUID, the floor BICUBIC is ~100x tighter) remains as a known SVDSBTL accuracy ceiling, tracked here.

title: IF97Backend: opt-in TOMS748 forward-refinement for HmassP / PSmass updates
description:
**Problem.**  IF97Backend::update(HmassP_INPUTS, h, p) uses IAPWS R7-97's published backward equations directly: T_phmass(p, h) is a closed-form polynomial fit with ±25 mK residual baked in (the backward equations are polynomial fits to forward h(T, p), not exact inverses).  This is fast (~2.5 us per update) but means every downstream property read carries IF97's own backward-equation residual.

The SVDSBTL IF97-sampling code (src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp) already polishes via TOMS748 on the forward h(T, p) equation to get forward-consistent T, machine precision.  The C++ failmap harness (src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTLFailMap.cpp) does the same thing for the conformance reference.  And the Python docs script (Web/scripts/fluid_properties.IF97Conformance.py) just got a Python TOMS748 reimplementation for the same purpose.

Three call sites doing the same polish in three different places.  Consolidate.

**Proposal.**  Add a config key, e.g. IF97_REFINE_HMASSP_FORWARD (default false).  When set, IF97Backend::update(HmassP_INPUTS, h, p) and update(PSmass_INPUTS, p, s) — and their HmolarP / PSmolar molar variants — do the following:

1. Seed T_phmass via the existing backward equation (fast, cheap, gives a known-good starting bracket).
2. Newton or TOMS748 iterate on the FORWARD h(T, p) (or s(T, p)) until |h_now - h_target| < rel_tol * |h_target| (machine precision relative).
3. State pinning via specify_phase() across the iteration so the IF97 region classifier doesn't flip basins mid-iteration on probes near the saturation curve.
4. Tolerance handling identical to the SVDSBTL sampler in SurfacePresets.cpp.

**Cost.**  When enabled: ~5-10 us per update (vs ~2.5 us for the backward only).  4x the per-call cost, but enables exact forward-consistency.  Default OFF preserves current behaviour for users who don't need it.

**Plumbing.**  Most efficient implementation: in IF97Backend::update(), after T_phmass + set_phase, check the config key once.  If set, run the TOMS748 polish loop using boost::math::tools::toms748_solve and IF97::hmass_Tp as the residual function.

**Cleanups enabled by this.**  Both the SVDSBTL sampler in SurfacePresets.cpp (foi.9.10) AND the conformance C++ harness (CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTLFailMap.cpp) AND the Python docs script (CoolProp-s60 PR) can ALL be replaced by 'set_config_bool(IF97_REFINE_HMASSP_FORWARD, true)' before their respective sampling loops, removing the duplicate polish code in three places.

**Tests.**  Verify: with the key enabled, h_consistency = abs(if97.hmass() - h_target) / abs(h_target) < 1e-12 for any (h, p) in the IF97 envelope.  Without the key: residual matches T_phmass's published ±25 mK floor.

**Acceptance.**  Catch2 test demonstrating forward-consistent IF97 HmassP at machine precision when key is enabled; per-call timing within 4x of the backward-only path; SVDSBTL surface sample lambda (SurfacePresets.cpp) updated to set the key instead of doing its own TOMS748 polish; conformance Python script's _refine_to_forward_h helper removed in favor of the config key.

**Related.** CoolProp-foi.9.10 (the original SVDSBTL TOMS748 polish), CoolProp-s60 (PR #2946 docs work that exposed this need).

title: SVDSBTL: build a SaturationSurrogate spline when SuperAncillary unavailable
description:
**Problem.**  When SVDSBTL is built on a source backend that doesn't expose a SuperAncillary (REFPROP today; SRK / PR / etc. in the future), every dome-touching query falls through to source.update(PQ_INPUTS / QT_INPUTS, ...).  REFPROP's PQ flash is ~1-5 ms per call; HEOS SuperAncillary's eval_sat is ~50 ns.  That's a 4-5 order-of-magnitude tax on two-phase queries through SVDSBTL&REFPROP — visible in fast_evaluate batches where even a small dome-probe fraction dominates wall time.

Use sites in src/Backends/SVDSBTL/SVDSBTLBackend.cpp affected:
- PQ/QT input routing (lines 387-411): sat endpoints (h_L, h_V) per query
- HmassP dome detection (lines 503-528): sat endpoints (h_L, h_V) + optional (rho_L, rho_V, s_L, s_V) per dome-hit query
- PT-on-sat detection (lines 551-559): T_sat from p
- ensure_dome_rho_endpoints_ (line 577): rho_L, rho_V on demand
- ensure_dome_s_endpoints_ (line 592): s_L, s_V on demand

All five sites currently follow the same pattern: 'try SuperAncillary; if nullptr, fall through to source.update(PQ_INPUTS, ...)'.  The fall-through is the slow path.

**Proposed solution.**  A SaturationSurrogate object that mimics SuperAncillary's interface but is built at SVDSBTLBackend construction from ~50-100 PQ probes of the source backend.

Class sketch (include/CoolProp/sbtl/SaturationSurrogate.h):

  class SaturationSurrogate {
   public:
    static std::unique_ptr<SaturationSurrogate> build_from_source(::CoolProp::AbstractState& src,
                                                                  std::size_t n_knots = 64);
    double get_T_from_p(double p) const;
    double eval_sat(double T, char what, int side) const;  // matches SA API
   private:
    region::CubicSplineCurve T_of_p_;
    std::array<region::CubicSplineCurve, 2> hL_hV_of_T_;   // index = side
    std::array<region::CubicSplineCurve, 2> rhoL_rhoV_of_T_;
    std::array<region::CubicSplineCurve, 2> sL_sV_of_T_;
    std::array<region::CubicSplineCurve, 2> uL_uV_of_T_;
    double T_triple_, T_crit_;  // domain bounds
  };

Knots: ~64 log-spaced in T between T_triple and 0.9999 * T_crit (avoid the critical singularity).  At each knot, source.update(QT_INPUTS, 0.0, T) and source.update(QT_INPUTS, 1.0, T) to sample both sides.  Independent variable T (matches SuperAncillary convention so eval_sat(T, ...) wires straight through).

Underlying representation: region::CubicSplineCurve — already in the codebase (used for boundary curves in atlas), already implements analytic eval_da, already has the build / eval pattern we need.  No new math.

**Plumbing.**  Add SVDSBTLBackend::sat_ancillary_() that returns a polymorphic SatProvider* (either the SuperAncillary or the SaturationSurrogate; both expose get_T_from_p / eval_sat).  Replace the 5 'auto sa = superanc_(); if (sa) { ... } else { source.update(PQ_INPUTS, ...) }' blocks with 'auto sat = sat_ancillary_(); sat->get_T_from_p(...)'.  Source-PQ fall-through becomes the last-resort path only when even the surrogate fails to build (truly degenerate fluid).

**Cost / win.**
- Build-time: ~50-100 ms one-time per (fluid, source-backend) at SVDSBTLBackend construction (one-off; warmed cache makes subsequent constructions free once persistence lands).
- Runtime: ~50 ns per sat query vs ~1-5 ms PQ flash.  4-5 orders of magnitude.
- Memory: ~6 KB per surrogate.

**Phasing.**
- Step 1 (this issue): in-memory surrogate, built at construction.  No persistence — accept the one-time build cost; the .svd.bin.z load still beats a from-scratch SVD build by 100x so the wall-clock impact is negligible.
- Step 2 (follow-up): persist SaturationSurrogate in .svd.bin.z (serializer kRevision bump).  Lets cold-start avoid the sampling pass entirely.
- Step 3 (future): consider Chebyshev expansion instead of cubic spline if accuracy near the critical singularity matters (CubicSplineCurve plateaus; SuperAncillary uses piecewise Chebyshev with refined sub-domains near T_crit for ~1e-12 accuracy).

**Accuracy target.**  Match source PQ_INPUTS to within ~1e-6 relative on smooth-fluid (non-near-critical) probes.  At T > 0.99*T_crit the spline error grows (sqrt-singularity not captured by polynomials); document the degradation and recommend the critical-patch HEOS-fallback for that slice.

**Tests.**  Catch2 case parameterized over (HEOS, REFPROP) source:
1. Compare SaturationSurrogate.eval_sat against source.update(QT_INPUTS, side, T) on 50 random T probes — relative error < 1e-6.
2. Compare SVDSBTL&REFPROP dome-blend Q with and without the surrogate — should match to within ~1e-8 (same lever rule, just different sat-endpoint source).
3. Microbenchmark: dome query timing with surrogate vs source-PQ — assert >100x speedup.

**Why not just always use HEOS for sat lines, even with REFPROP source.**  Defeats the user's intent.  Someone who picked SVDSBTL&REFPROP wants REFPROP-truth values throughout, including on the saturation curves (REFPROP and HEOS sat curves can differ by ~1e-3 in some regions — e.g. air, where REFPROP uses the GERG-mix Helmholtz and HEOS uses a different formulation).

**Relation to other beads.**
- CoolProp-zx1 (now closed) verified REFPROP works correctness-wise; this issue addresses the perf gap that closure exposed.
- CoolProp-phv (HS / DU presets): the surrogate's eval_sat interface should also cover H and S queries; designing it generically now saves work later.
- CoolProp-vh0 (backend options JSON): surrogate knot count could be a tunable option (default 64).

**Acceptance.**  Dome-touching fast_evaluate batches via SVDSBTL&REFPROP run in the same per-query budget as SVDSBTL&HEOS (~400 ns/probe, vs the current several-ms-per-dome-probe).  Single-fluid Catch2 test case showing surrogate is automatic + tests pass.
notes: Implemented in PR #2965 — 96-knot Chebyshev-Lobatto cubic-spline surrogate built lazily from source.QT_INPUTS; wired into 5 sat-provider sites in SVDSBTLBackend. Accuracy validated at 1e-6 on HEOS Water [0.55, 0.95]*T_crit + near-T_crit finiteness guard. Deviation from bead: Chebyshev-Lobatto knots instead of log-T spacing (rho curvature is at high T, log-T spacing missed 1e-6 there; Chebyshev-Lobatto + 96 knots hits target with margin). Phase 1 only (in-memory).
close_reason: Merged via PR #2965 (SaturationSurrogate cubic-spline cache for REFPROP source). 96-knot Chebyshev-Lobatto spline built lazily from source's QT_INPUTS at first sat-touching call; SA → surrogate → source PQ/QT fallback chain. Closes 4-5 orders-of-magnitude gap on REFPROP-source dome queries (1-5 ms → ~ns).

title: Docs: rewrite IF97 + SVDSBTL pages to capture salient parts
description:
Web/fluid_properties/IF97.rst (222 lines), Web/fluid_properties/IF97_conformance.rst.in (222 lines), and the implicit "there is no SVDSBTL standalone page" are not pulling their weight as documentation.  Recent work (#2938-#2944) has substantively reshaped what SVDSBTL is, how it dispatches, and what its accuracy/perf envelope looks like, and the docs haven't tracked it.

## What's wrong with the current docs

**IF97.rst** — mostly an API/usage tour; doesn't explain the IF97 piecewise EOS structure (R1/R2/R3/R5 regions, R4 saturation, B23 boundary curve) that anyone reading the conformance figures needs in order to interpret "R2/R3 kink" or "R1/R3 isotherm split".  The fast_evaluate paragraph (lines 200-220) was added recently but reads as a perf footnote rather than as the headline API for throughput.

**IF97_conformance.rst.in** — figure-heavy, prose-light.  The three fail-map PNGs land in front of the reader with no narrative for why the failures live where they do (R3 boundary cells, R2/R3 kink, near-critical) — the very thing the SVDSBTL atlas was carefully built to address.  The timing-ratio table is at the bottom and ratio-only; doesn't even hint at PropsSI vs AbstractState reuse vs fast_evaluate as the three distinct cost regimes.

**SVDSBTL has no standalone page.**  Backend description leaks across IF97.rst (the timing section), IF97_conformance.rst.in (the conformance section), Configuration.rst (the ALLOW_SVDSBTL_IN_PROPSSI key), BackendOptions.rst (the schema), and SVDComponents.ipynb (the underlying SVD math).  A user trying to understand "what is SVDSBTL" has to assemble that from five files.

## What rewrite should produce

1. **New Web/coolprop/SVDSBTL.rst** — top-level backend page.  Sections:

2. **Rewrite Web/fluid_properties/IF97.rst** — preserve the working pieces (API examples, table of features) but front-load:

   - R2 fail-map: outliers cluster along the B23 curve.  This is the atlas split (foi.9.5/8) at work — without it, the failures would be order-of-magnitude worse and spread across the whole R2 slab.
   - R3 fail-map: outliers cluster in the near-critical region (the critical-patch bbox).  Document the patch + polish mechanism.
   - Timing: replace ratio-only table with three columns (PropsSI / AbstractState.update / fast_evaluate) and an explanation of when each applies.
   - Methodology: probe distribution, exclusions, what "conformance" means here (R7-97 perm budget per Table 8-13).
