title: Per-region NT/NR override + bump LIQUID 2× for R1 w conformance
description:
Closes the R1 w conformance gap revealed by 540k-probe dense fail-map.

**Diagnosis (2026-05-20):** 2541 R1 w fails clustered at h > 1000 kJ/kg, which corresponds to T > 450 K compressed liquid — the descent side of the w(T,p) peak.  15% of R1 probes in that h range fail w, vs 0.26% at h ≤ 1000.  Not a kink or boundary; w just has steep curvature in the descent region and our shared 200×800 SVD grid can't resolve it at rank 20.  Kunick uses a dedicated 300×200 w^L grid for this (Table A4).

**Implementation:**

1. Add NT_override / NR_override fields to SurfaceSpec::RegionSpec (default 0 = use spec.NT/NR).
2. Modify build_surface + sample_grid to use per-region NT/NR via the override if set.
3. SurfacePresets sets LIQUID's override to NT=400, NR=1600 (4× cells, ~16× residual reduction predicted from Hermite-cubic O(h^4)).
4. Cache rev 9 → 10.

**Expected impact:**
  R1 w_max: 0.013% → ~0.0008% (under 0.001% perm)
  R1 w fails: 2541 → likely < 50

**Cost:**
  LIQUID cache size: 2× (storage = rank·(NT+NR), so 20·(200+800)=20k → 20·(400+1600)=40k per property)
  LIQUID build time: 4× (cell count, not rank)
  Eval time: unchanged (locate is O(log NT + log NR), negligible difference)

**Validation:** dense fail-map probe + dev/bench_svdsbtl_ph.cpp benchmark to confirm eval timing unchanged.

Parent: CoolProp-foi.9.  Stacked on foi.9.5/9.9/9.10.
notes:
**Experiment result (2026-05-20):** Bumping LIQUID to NT=400, NR=1600, rank=40 (2× each axis + 2× rank to preserve compression ratio) drops the bulk R1 conformance failures dramatically but exposes boundary-cell residuals at the η=0/η=1 and p_min edges.

  R1 w fails: 2541 → 106  (-96%)   — descent-zone blob closed
  R1 s fails:  574 → 156  (-73%)
  R1 v fails:   15 →  12  (-20%)

But worst-case got worse:

  R1 w max:  0.013% → 0.031%   (boundary cells near p_min)
  R1 s max:  0.029  → 0.103
  R1 T max:  4.5 mK → 12.3 mK

The new worst cells are at T ≈ 273 K (η ≈ 0 cell at triple-point floor) and p ≈ 1 kPa (xi ≈ 0 cell at log-p axis bottom).  The denser Chebyshev cosine grid puts cells extremely close to the boundary where the natural-cubic-spline endpoint BC artifact dominates.  Bulk improvement was real but not a clean win — the boundary-cell issue needs a separate fix (clamped or hybrid spline BC at η=0/η=1).

Profiling: 430 ns/call avg (was ~200 ns baseline).  2× slower for LIQUID cells with rank=40, but still 20× faster than HEOS (8684 ns).  Profiling envelope intact.

**Decision:** revert the LIQUID-specific override; keep the per-region NT/NR/rank infrastructure in case future work wants a different attempt (e.g., NT=400 only, or different spline BC at boundary).  Re-open with a different approach (clamped BC) later.

title: Bump NT/NR defaults 200/800 → 400/1600 for foi.9 conformance
description:
Measured grid-density sweep on foi.9.6 ruled out kernel improvements (cubic Hermite with natural-spline slopes IS the natural cubic spline; quintic with same data degenerates).  Higher cell density is the next available leverage.  4× cells (NT 200→400, NR 800→1600) measured at rank=20:

  R1 v max: 1.28%  → 0.10%   (13×)
  R2 v max: 11.54% → 2.47%   (4.7×)

at the cost of ~4× storage and ~4× build time per (region, property).

Implementation:
1. SVDSBTLBackend.cpp: kDefaultGrid {200, 800, 20} → {400, 1600, 20}
2. Bump cache rev 7 → 8 to force rebuild of existing rev-7 caches at the old shape.
3. Verify existing options-blob NT/NR overrides still work.

Cost: cache file size ~7 MB → ~28 MB per (fluid, input pair).  Build time ~30 s → ~2 min per fluid.  GHA actions/cache for ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables (PR #2937) amortizes the build cost across CI runs.

Parent: CoolProp-foi.9.
notes:
**Investigation 2026-05-20 — NT/NR default bump from 200/800 → 400/1600 gives ZERO conformance gain on top of PR1 (foi.9.5 atlas split).**

Stacked on ihb/svdsbtl-split-b23, measured Water/IF97 fail-map at NT=400, NR=1600:

  R1 v_max:  0.1640% (PR1)  →  0.1648%  (essentially unchanged)
  R2 v_max:  0.0089% (PR1)  →  0.0529%  (REGRESSED — exposed a sat-dome-edge artifact)
  R3 v_max:  213.1%  (PR1)  →  213.1%   (critical-region outlier, unchanged)

The pre-PR1 density sweep that suggested ~13× R1 gain was BEFORE the B23 atlas split landed.  Post-PR1, the SUPER region's IF97 R2/R3 kink is no longer interior — the dominant residual driver shifted to:
  (a) the η=0 boundary cells of SUPER_R3 (compressed-liquid corner)
  (b) the critical-point neighborhood (the single R3 outlier at T=663.7, p=26.6)
Neither is a grid-density problem.  4× more cells just made cells smaller in dense regions where they were already adequate, while exposing new sat-dome-edge artifacts.

Cost: 4× cache size (7 MB → 18 MB per file), 4× build time.  Not justified by the data.

Closing as 'won\'t fix'.  Future R1 gains need the boundary-condition or sub-region-split work tracked in foi.9.7's notes.

title: Quintic Hermite kernel for R1/R2 interpolation residuals
description:
**Subagent-investigation outcome (2026-05-20):** R1/R2 worst-case residuals (1.28% / 11.5% v) are *interpolation-residual-limited*; the rank=20→120 sweep showed truncation isn't the bottleneck.  Replacing Hermite cubic (O(h^4)) with quintic (O(h^6)) is predicted to cut R1/R2 worst-case residuals by ~one order of magnitude on the same grid, beating Kunick's perms (R1 v 0.001%) — at a fraction of the storage cost of denser grids.

CAVEAT: higher-order Hermite *worsens* residuals at derivative discontinuities via Gibbs-type overshoot.  Must be gated behind an options-blob flag and only enabled in regions known to be smooth.  After foi.9.5 (atlas split) closes the IF97 R2/R3 kink, the SUPER_R3 and SUPER_R2 sub-regions are smooth enough to safely enable quintic.

Implementation sketch:
1. include/CoolProp/svd/Hermite1D.h: add quintic basis (h0..h5: values + 1st + 2nd derivatives).
2. include/CoolProp/svd/SVDDecomposition.h: add d2U_dx2 / d2V_S_dy2 slope buffers; add KernelOrder enum (CUBIC, QUINTIC) — persisted, self-describing.
3. include/CoolProp/svd/SVDEvaluator.h: dispatch hot loop on d.kernel_order.
4. src/SVD/SVDBuilder.cpp: extend slope computation to also return second derivatives.  Natural cubic spline already gives these.
5. Schema option: 'interpolation': {'order': 'cubic'|'quintic'} under SVDSBTLOptions.
6. Bump cache rev (or piggyback on foi.9.5's bump).

Storage cost: +50% for quintic slope buffers (~16 KB extra per region per axis at default rank=20).
Eval cost: +~30% FLOPs in inner accumulator, well under +30% wall-clock (binary search and exp dominate).

Parent: CoolProp-foi.9.  Depends on foi.9.5 landing first (so kinks are at region boundaries, not interior).
notes:
**Investigation 2026-05-20 — quintic Hermite via cubic-spline-derived d² is mathematically equivalent to cubic Hermite; gives ZERO improvement.**

Built the full plumbing (Hermite1D quintic basis, KernelOrder enum, d2 buffers on SVDDecomposition, builder extension, evaluator dispatch, serializer rev 7→8) and measured on Water/IF97 stacked on top of foi.9.5 atlas split.  Numbers byte-identical to cubic (verified to 4 decimal places on every R1-R5 worst-case).

Root cause discovered by sanity check: the cubic spline through (x_i, y_i) with natural BC IS a degree-3 polynomial in each cell.  Quintic Hermite with the same y_i, the spline's m_i, AND the spline's M_i (2nd-derivative table) satisfies 6 conditions on a degree-5 polynomial — but those 6 conditions are EXACTLY the conditions a degree-3 polynomial satisfies plus zero higher-order terms.  So the unique quintic Hermite polynomial degenerates to the cubic spline polynomial.  Verified in /tmp via direct numeric check: feeding the quintic basis with cubic-spline-extracted d² reproduces the cubic Hermite output to machine precision.

Equivalent statement: our current cubic-Hermite-with-natural-spline-slopes IS the natural cubic spline of each SVD mode.  We're already at the C² interpolant's accuracy floor for the given (x, y) data.

To get a real quintic gain we need d² values that are NOT consistent with a cubic spline of the same slopes.  Options:
  a) Quintic spline construction (degree-5 piecewise C⁴, penta-diagonal solve)
  b) High-order FD on the raw M values (5-point central FD → O(h^4) d²)
  c) Analytic 2nd derivatives from the source EOS at sample points
All are more work than a kernel swap.

Plumbing is stashed for future revival.  Branch ihb/svdsbtl-quintic-hermite kept on top of ihb/svdsbtl-split-b23 as documentation.

**Conclusion: closing foi.9.6 as 'won\'t fix via this approach'.  The realistic high-leverage paths for R1/R2 are now:
  - NT/NR default bump (foi.9.8?) — predictable ~30× gain, 4× storage
  - Quintic spline construction (real foi.9.6 reincarnation) — more work but matches Kunick
  - Pre-smoothing of M (foi.9.7) — Kunick §5.1 trick, cheap experiment**

title: Phase 2: per-property primary-axis transform (sqrt(p), ln(p), identity p)
description:
Make svd primary AxisTransform per-property-overridable instead of region-level only.

Per Kunick Tables A4 (L), A5 (G), A6 (HT):
  - T: p̄(p) = p (identity) in L, G, HT
  - v: p̄(p) = p in all regions (the variable transformation is on v itself — see foi.9.1)
  - s: p̄(p) = sqrt(p) in L; ln(p) in G and HT
  - w: p̄(p) = p in L and HT; sqrt(p) in G
  - η: p̄(p) = p in L, G

Currently our SVDSBTL uses AxisScale::LOG on p as a per-region constant — all properties in the same region share the same p-axis transform.

Implementation:
1. Add SQRT to AxisScale enum (alongside LINEAR and LOG).
2. Extend SurfaceSpec::PropertySpec with an optional primary-axis override (default: use region's axis).
3. Plumb the override through SVDSurfaceFactory::build_surface and SVDDecomposition serialization.
4. Wire the Kunick per-property recipes into SurfacePresets ph_properties.
5. Bump cache rev.

CAVEAT (per Ian, 2026-05-20): some property transforms (notably log-of-something) break for fluids whose reference-state choice yields negative entropy in some part of the envelope.  This phase operates only on the primary axis (p), so negative s isn't a direct concern.  If we later add a log(s) output transform, we'd need to tabulate an absolute-entropy offset per fluid first.

Parent: CoolProp-foi.9.  Depends on foi.9.1 landing first.

title: Phase 1: v→p·v OutputTransform for density in supercritical regions
description:
Add PV variant to svd::OutputTransform enum, mirroring how EXP works today.  The stored value at each grid cell is p·v = p/ρ (with units J/kg, well-behaved across the gas/supercritical envelope because p·v ≈ RT in the ideal-gas limit).  Recovered at evaluation by ρ = p / stored.

Per Kunick (G13-15) Tables A5 / A6, this transform is used for v in the G and HT regions of his IF97-based (p, h) splines.  Our SVDSBTL SUPER region covers essentially the same physical territory (T < 1073.15 K, p > pcrit), so this is the natural place to start.  Leave LIQUID and VAPOR on the existing EXP (log(ρ)) transform — Kunick keeps L on the identity v transform too.

Implementation:
1. Add PV = 2 to svd::OutputTransform enum (after IDENTITY=0, EXP=1).
2. SVDSurfaceFactory::sample_grid: when transform == PV, store p * (1.0 / rhomass()) = p / ρ (i.e. p·v) instead of log(ρ).
3. SVDEvaluator::eval: extend signature to take physical p (or thread it through differently — TBD during impl).  When transform == PV, return p / stored_value.  Currently the eval takes (xi, eta) ∈ [0,1]² — we either also pass p, or compute p inside via the AxisTransform.inverse().
4. SVDSurfaceSerializer kRevision bump (existing mechanism, see PR E).
5. Update src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp:ph_properties — gate iDmass transform on region (need to plumb the region into the PropertySpec, or just split ph_properties into per-region helpers).
6. Re-run fail-map probe (the uncommitted /tmp file) to confirm SVDSBTL&IF97 R3 worst-case v residual drops below 0.001% perm.

Acceptance: SVDSBTL&IF97 R3 v fails drop from 928/959 → < 10 (current state from foi.X investigation 2026-05-20).

Parent: CoolProp-foi.9
notes:
**Investigation 2026-05-20 continued — h_c-split + PV combined attempt also mixed-results; abandoning the PV-transform approach.**

Tried h_c-split (offset by +50 kJ/kg above h_c per Kunick Tables A4/A5) with PV-on-SUPER_G:
- R1 v max: 1.28% → 0.16%  (8× better — clear win)
- R1 w max: 3.77% → 0.32%  (12× better)
- R2 s fails: 15 → 72 (5× worse count, max actually halved)
- R3 v max: 112% → 199% (worse; R3 was already broken)

Structural findings:

1. **PV is only a clean win far from the critical point.**  At our R3 worst-case (T=708 K, p=40 MPa, h=2329 kJ/kg), the compressibility Z = pv/RT ≈ 0.56 — significant non-ideality.  p·v varies 5× more than log ρ across nearby cells.  Kunick's G region works because it extends to subcritical superheated vapor where p·v ≈ RT cleanly; our SUPER is supercritical-only so it's never in the clean dilute-gas regime.

2. **Our shared-grid-per-region constraint** means h_c splitting tessellates all properties at once.  Kunick has per-property grids per region (Tables A4 vs A5 — different node counts for v vs s vs w in the same region).  The R2 s-fail count regression came from this: the smaller SUPER_G grid pushed ~57 marginal s cells from just-under to just-over the 1e-3 J/kgK perm budget (max only 0.13 J/kgK — barely fails).

3. **Our SVD+Hermite-cubic kernel struggles near singular boundaries** regardless of which transform we pick.  The leverage for R3 conformance is elsewhere.

Conclusion: closing foi.9.1 with no change landed.  Approach was wrong-headed for our SVD+Hermite-cubic architecture.  Real R3 fix is either (a) wider critical-patch routing (CoolProp-5ni / -dxd / -dxd) or (b) interpolant change (CoolProp-foi.9.3 — bi-quadratic spline per Kunick §3.2.1) or (c) higher SVD rank + natural-cubic-spline slope source.

Branch ihb/svdsbtl-pv-transform deleted (never pushed).  Infrastructure (PV enum, per-region OutputTransform override, eval-with-physical-pressure signature) is stashed for resurrection if a future change re-justifies it.

title: EPIC: SVDSBTL conformance — port Kunick (G13-15) variable transformations and region layout
description:
The SVDSBTL backend's R1/R2 conformance failures against IF97 truth (CoolProp-foi.X investigation, 2026-05-20) are not boundary-routing issues — they're missing variable transformations.  Kunick et al. (IAPWS G13-15, 2015) achieve their published perm budgets across IF97 R1/R2/R3 boundaries by smoothing through them with bi-quadratic interpolating splines on transformed variables, NOT by partitioning at IF97 boundaries.

This epic ports the relevant pieces of Kunick's recipe.  Four phases, increasing scope.

PHASE 1 — v→p·v output transform for density in supercritical regions.  Slam-dunk: density varies over 6 decades in (p, h) but p·v ≈ RT in the ideal-gas limit and stays bounded, so the SVD only needs to capture the residual non-ideality.  Kunick Tables A5 / A6 use this for G and HT regions of IF97-based (p, h) splines.

PHASE 2 — per-property primary-axis transform (sqrt(p) for s in L and w in G, ln(p) for s in G/HT, etc.).  Kunick Tables A4-A6 give per-(region, property) recipes.  Requires extending our SVDSBTL AxisTransform from region-level to per-property-override level (small architectural change).

Caveat (per Ian, 2026-05-20): some property transforms (e.g. log(s)) break for fluids where reference-state choice yields negative entropy in some part of the phase envelope.  Phase 2 axis transforms operate on p, not on the dependent variable, so they aren't directly affected — but if we later try a log(s_shifted) output transform we'd need to tabulate an absolute-entropy offset per fluid.  Worth keeping in mind so we don't generalize PV to other properties in a way that traps us later.

PHASE 3 — close the residual gap.  Either: (a) keep SVD + Hermite cubic but increase grid density 2-3× per Kunick's Tables A4-A6 counts; or (b) replace SVD + Hermite cubic with a bi-quadratic interpolating spline per §3.2.1.  Measure (a) first.

PHASE 4 — switch the SBTL preset's region partition from LIQUID + VAPOR + SUPER to Kunick's L + G + HT (sat dome at subcritical, h_c isenthalp above pcrit, T=1073.15 isotherm).  Eliminates the residual ~19 mK SVDSBTL&HEOS L/G straddle in the SUPER region.

Parent: CoolProp-foi (SBTL G13-15 conformance epic).
close_reason: Closed per user: IF97 conformance is at an acceptable state for now. Current SVDSBTL+IF97 accuracy (post foi.9.1-4, 6-8, 11 and pd6) meets the practical needs of the project; remaining items (atlas splits at R2/R3 + R1/R3 boundaries, TOMS748 h-inversion in IF97 sampling, Hermite bicubic backbone, multi-piece grids, L/G h_c split, separate HT region, Sphinx accuracy page, per-property fail-map) are conformance polish that can be reopened or refiled if/when stricter G13-15 conformance becomes a goal.

title: CI: cache ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables in test_catch2.yml
description:
The Catch2 CI workflow rebuilds every SVD surface from scratch on every run because GHA doesn't persist $HOME between workflow invocations.  Within a single run the in-process cache works (subsequent same-fluid tests load in ~1 s), but cold-cache builds cost 30-60 s per (fluid × input_pair), and the multi_fluid test sweeps ~8 fluids × 2 pairs = ~8 minutes of avoidable rebuild on every push.

Inflection point: test_catch2 wall-clock jumped from ~35 s to ~440 s when PR #2919 (Phase 2c — SVDSBTL backend) landed.  Subsequent PRs nudge it higher; current master sits at ~10 minutes.

Fix: wire actions/cache@v4 in .github/workflows/test_catch2.yml against ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables, keyed on a hash of the relevant source paths so the cache invalidates whenever sampling / grid / serializer / preset code changes:

  - name: Cache SVDSBTL tables
    uses: actions/cache@v4
    with:
      path: ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables
      key: svdsbtl-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles(
        'include/CoolProp/sbtl/SVDSurfaceSerializer.h',
        'include/CoolProp/Backends/SVDSBTL/**',
        'src/SBTL/**',
        'src/Backends/SVDSBTL/**'
      ) }}
      restore-keys: |
        svdsbtl-${{ runner.os }}-

The hashFiles() captures: serializer kRevision (cache-format invalidation), backend logic (sampling / dispatch changes), preset definitions (region geometry), grid / Newton-refine code.  Anything in those paths bumps the key → fresh build → updated cache.  Other paths leave the cache hot.

The on-wire revision check already provides a safety net (load throws → ensure_surface_ rebuilds) if hashFiles() ever misses an invalidation trigger.

Expected impact: cold cache (first PR with code changes in SBTL paths) ~10 min as today; warm cache (most PRs, since SBTL changes are rare in any given PR) drops the test phase from ~10 min back toward the ~35 s pre-#2919 baseline.

Not in scope: persisting between branches (only the master cache propagates via restore-keys), tag-based opt-in for slow tests in PR CI (separate decision).

title: SVDSBTL&IF97: split SUPER region at R2/R3 boundary (p_B23(T))
description:
Follow-up surfaced by PR #2934 (Chebyshev + Newton + ε-band phase-pin).

After landing PR E the SVDSBTL&IF97 conformance fail-map drops to:
- R1 s fails: 37 / 1546
- R2 s fails: 15 / 1546

Remaining outliers cluster at one structural artifact: the IF97 R2/R3 boundary curve p_B23(T) (approx. 0.348 GPa·K^-1 line from 623.15 K to 863.15 K) crosses the SBTL preset's SUPER region.  IF97 switches between R2 (polynomial backwards) and R3 (rho-T basis) across that curve, so cp / s / w have a structural kink in (T,p) at the boundary.  A single SVD over a region containing the kink can't resolve it — measured |ΔT| residual is ~11 K at T=725.79 K, p=37.58 MPa where p_B23(T)≈37.85 MPa.

Fix: in SurfacePresets for Water/IF97, split the SUPER region at p_B23(T) into SUPER_R2 and SUPER_R3 sub-regions.  Each gets its own SVD; the dispatch chooses by comparing p against p_B23(T) (closed-form polynomial — IF97::B23p_T or analogue).  Generalises the boundary-curve machinery already used for the saturation dome.

Acceptance: R2 |ΔT| max residual < 25 mK (Tables 8-13 perm budget), no regression on R1 or VAPOR/LIQUID fail-map.

title: SVDSBTL: Chebyshev η-spacing + Newton-refined IF97 sampling + ε-band phase-pinning (PR E)
description:
Three coupled SVDSBTL build-time accuracy improvements for IF97 conformance:

1. **Chebyshev η-spacing on the secondary axis** in sample_grid and grid_axes (src/SBTL/SVDSurfaceFactory.cpp): cells crowd toward η=0 and η=1 boundaries. Saturation curve is one of the η boundaries in LIQUID/VAPOR regions, and properties have sharp curvature in a thin η-strip right against it. Uniform η spacing puts only ~1 grid cell into that strip; Chebyshev crowds them at both ends, dropping per-region failure counts 6-9× on the SVDSBTL&HEOS conformance fail-map.

2. **Newton-refined update_state lambda for IF97 source** (src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp): IAPWS R7-97 backwards equations carry a documented ±25 mK residual, which propagates to ~0.25 J/(kg·K) in s — well above IAPWS perm budget of 1e-3 J/(kg·K). Newton iteration (≤5 steps, cp-weighted) against forward h(T,p)=h_target closes that gap so the SVD grid stores forward-consistent properties. HEOS/REFPROP sources skip the refinement (HmassP path already converges to forward precision).

3. **IF97 backend honors specify_phase()** (src/Backends/IF97/IF97Backend.h): adds calc_specify_phase / calc_unspecify_phase overrides and an imposed-phase hint check in update(PT_INPUTS). Newton iterates within the IF97 ε=3.3e-5 band of the saturation curve previously tripped the ε-band reject; phase pinning eliminates that without changing default behavior.

Measured impact on SVDSBTL&IF97 conformance fail-map vs IF97 truth (NT=200, NR=800, rank=28):
- R1 s fails: 900 → 37
- R2 s fails: 1470 → 15
- R2 |ΔT| max residual: down from R2/R3-boundary-kink artifact range to ~11 K (remaining outlier is structural to the SBTL preset's region topology, separate follow-up).

Branch: ihb/svdsbtl-cheb-newton, base master.
notes: PR E open: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/pull/2934 against master. Three commits: 1784c20f (IF97 specify_phase), 59a95749 (Chebyshev η + rev 5→6), 715eb9e1 (Newton-refined IF97 source). Smoke test (SVDSBTL&IF97 PT lookup) passes after rev=6 cache rebuild (70s).

title: SVDSBTL: IF97-source w sampling bug — NaN-fill median produces constant w surface
description:
SVDSBTL&IF97 returns the SAME speed_sound value (~1402 m/s for Water) at every (T, p) — ρ/h/s vary correctly.

Reproducer: factory("SVDSBTL&IF97", "Water"), then call update(PT_INPUTS, p, T) at distinct (T, p) and read speed_sound() — observe constant value.

Root cause: when SurfacePresets samples IF97 at (T, p) points near the η-axis padding boundary, IF97 reports iphase_twophase (its epsilon-band around p_sat is wider than the η padding 0.001), and speed_sound() throws.  The sample_grid catch leaves NaN.  Whole rows of NaN survive the row-fill pass and get median-imputed by the all-NaN-row guard, producing a near-constant w field.  ρ/h/s don't trip this because IF97 has valid two-phase formulas for them (lever-rule blend), so they get finite values and don't NaN-fill.

Fix candidates (any one would close this):
1. Pass imposed_phase=iphase_liquid|iphase_gas to source->update() when sampling LIQUID/VAPOR cells — forces IF97 to single-phase path even right at the saturation curve.
2. Shrink IF97's epsilon-band (its 'within 1e-4 % of psat' two-phase tag).  Touches IF97 outside SVDSBTL's scope.
3. Detect 'IF97 source + near-sat sampling' and use a wider η padding for that combination.

Visible only on SVDSBTL&IF97; SVDSBTL&HEOS works because HEOS doesn't have the wide-epsilon band.

Tagged !mayfail on the SVDSBTL-IAPWS conformance test cases until fixed.  ρ/h/s assertions in those tests still pass and gate against regression.
notes: Root cause: IF97Backend::clear() override skipped cache.clear(); _speed_sound stayed stuck at first sampled value across SBTL build. Fix: cache.clear() in IF97 clear(). R1/R2/R4 IAPWS conformance now passes; !mayfail tag dropped on those three. R3 mayfail-tagged for separate rank-truncation issue near critical.

title: SVDSBTL: confirm IF97 can serve as the underlying truth source for water
description:
Same shape as the REFPROP issue (CoolProp-???), restricted to Water. Goal: confirm SVDSBTL can be backed by IF97 (instead of HEOS) for users who want IAPWS-IF97-truth tables — useful for steam-cycle applications where IF97 is the contractual truth.

Scope:
1. Plumb a backend-name argument through SVDSBTLBackend constructor (shared with the REFPROP work).
2. IF97-specific caveats:
   - IF97 has limited input-pair support compared to HEOS (PT_INPUTS works; HmassP needs the IF97::T_phmass backward function which exists). Verify the SVDSBTL update_state lambdas can map cleanly.
   - IF97's 'fluid' is implicitly Water — reject any other fluid name when IF97 is selected.
   - IF97 doesn't have a SuperAncillary; two-phase routing must use IF97::psat97 + Q-weighted blending of saturated-side properties.
   - Critical-patch HEOS fallback: should be IF97-fallback when IF97 is the source backend (otherwise we'd be mixing truth sources). Implementation: the fallback uses the same backend the SVD was built against, configurable per construction.
3. Cache key encoding: Water.IF97.28.svd.bin.z, distinct from Water.HEOS.28.svd.bin.z.
4. Conformance: with IF97 as both source and truth, the conformance test (dev/iapws_conformance_probe.cpp) should pass with rho/h/s/w under budget by construction (modulo SVD truncation residual).

Build on the same plumbing as the REFPROP issue — both are 'specifying a non-HEOS source backend' which is one piece of design.
close_reason: All four scope items shipped across the foi.* + pd6 PR cycle. SVDSBTL&IF97 source backend is plumbed (factory string), IF97-only-Water gate enforced, cache key distinguishes IF97 from HEOS, and the conformance fail-map (PR #2957) confirms R1-R5 all within G13-15 budgets (R1 1.34 / R2 2.21 / R3 6.60 / R5 0.53 mK ΔT vs 10-25 mK budgets).

title: SVDSBTL: confirm REFPROP can serve as the underlying truth source
description:
The SVDSBTL backend currently hardcodes HEOS (factory("HEOS", fluid_name_)) as the source for table sampling, the SuperAncillary lookup, and the critical-patch HEOS fallback. Goal: confirm REFPROP can be plugged in instead of HEOS for users who want REFPROP-truth tables.

Scope:
1. Audit every site in src/Backends/SVDSBTL/SVDSBTLBackend.cpp where heos_reference_() is called or factory("HEOS", ...) is invoked. Check whether REFPROP can satisfy the same interface (rhomass, hmass, smass, umass, T, p, Q, first_partial_deriv, phase, etc.).
2. Plumb a backend-name argument through SVDSBTLBackend constructor (default "HEOS"). Cache key needs to encode the backend name (e.g. Water.HEOS.28.svd.bin.z vs Water.REFPROP.28.svd.bin.z) so the two don't collide — touches src/SBTL/SVDSurfaceSerializer's default_cache_path.
3. SuperAncillary path: REFPROP backends won't expose the per-fluid SuperAncillary (that's a CoolProp-internal Helmholtz expansion). The two-phase routing needs a graceful fallback — likely route to REFPROP's PQ_INPUTS / QT_INPUTS directly when SuperAncillary is unavailable.
4. Critical-patch fallback: works automatically since heos_reference_() just dispatches via the AbstractState interface. But verify behaviour when REFPROP throws on near-critical PT_INPUTS (REFPROP's flash machinery is different from HEOS's).
5. Tests: extend src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTL.cpp with a REFPROP-backed parameterization for at least one fluid where REFPROP is available.

Reproducer for current failure: factory("SVDSBTL", "REFPROP::Water") or similar — currently the fluid-name parser treats the whole string as the fluid name and feeds it to HEOS, so it would fail at HEOS construction. Need backend-vs-fluid disambiguation in the SVDSBTL constructor or via a separate argument.
notes: Audit + verification completed via PR #2945.  All code paths in src/Backends/SVDSBTL/SVDSBTLBackend.cpp + src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp confirmed REFPROP-compatible (every HEOS-specific cast/path has a graceful AbstractState-interface fallback).  CI builds REFPROP from source via .github/workflows/buildREFPROP.sh, so the 6 gated tests (3 pre-existing PT/PQ/cache + 3 new HmassP single-phase / HmassP dome-blend / fast_evaluate batched) actually execute in the master test_catch2 matrix.  No code changes needed beyond the new tests — the constructor arg + cache encoding + SuperAncillary fallback all already shipped in earlier PRs.

title: SVDSBTL: auto-calibrate critical-patch bbox per fluid at table-build time
description:
Currently SVDSBTLBackend::set_critical_bbox_multipliers() lets a user override the (T, p) bbox multipliers at runtime; defaults are 0.95/1.05 in T and 0.75/1.15 in p sized from Water's empirical IAPWS conformance footprint. CO2 (and likely other fluids) want a different shape — narrower in T, possibly wider in p.

Plan: at table-build time (dev/build_svd_tables.cpp), AFTER the SVD surfaces are built, run a coarse (T, p) probe over a wide candidate region [0.85, 1.20]·T_c × [0.5, 4.0]·p_c, sampling SVDSBTL vs HEOS density/h/s/w. Identify cells where rel-err exceeds the IAPWS conformance budget (200 ppm rho, 100 ppm h, 200 ppm s, 1000 ppm w). Fit a tight axis-aligned (T, p) bbox around the failing cells, with a small padding fraction.

Persist the four multipliers in the .svd.bin.z cache file as a small piece of metadata (4 doubles) — bump kRevision in SVDSurfaceSerializer accordingly. At backend construction, load the multipliers from cache; if missing (older cache or auto-calibration disabled), fall back to the conservative defaults.

Runtime override (set_critical_bbox_multipliers) remains available so users can hand-tune for their specific use case.

Reproducer: dev/probe_co2_khoopes.cpp on branch ihb/svd-clamped-bc shows the visible difference between default and tightened bbox for CO2.
notes: Bundled with CoolProp-dxd (the shrink loop) + CoolProp-229 (the docs) — all three land together in one PR.  5ni provides the persisted-multipliers + cache-revision plumbing; dxd provides the binary-search-shrink algorithm; 229 documents the resulting bbox numbers (per fluid) in Web/coolprop/SVDSBTL.rst once the new doc page from CoolProp-s60 exists.  Per-fluid bbox tables become a docs deliverable rather than an out-of-band reference.

title: fast_evaluate hardening: overflow + null-order + row-NaN contract (CoolProp #2920 review)
description:
Coderabbit flagged 4 real issues in #2920 (ihb/fast-evaluate) that should land in a follow-up commit before merge:

1. IF97Backend.h fast_evaluate (~line 651): N_inputs * N_outputs can overflow size_t before size validation. Also the N_outputs==0 branch writes status_flags[k] before null-pointer validation, so a caller passing N_outputs=0 and status_flags=nullptr derefs null.
2. IF97Backend.h fast_evaluate (~line 824): per-output eval_failed only marks the failing output as NaN. API contract (AbstractState.h docstring) says the entire row out_buffer[k*N_outputs..(k+1)*N_outputs] should be NaN when status_flags[k] != ok.
3. TabularBackends.cpp fast_evaluate (~line 885): same overflow + ordering as #1.
4. TabularBackends.cpp fast_evaluate (~line 1002): same row-NaN contract as #2.

Fixes for each are 5-10 lines (overflow guard with std::numeric_limits<size_t>::max() / N_outputs, reorder null-check before any status_flags writes, and a fill_nan_row(k) helper called when eval_failed). Optionally tighten AbstractState.h's docstring to make the row-NaN contract explicit.

Branch: ihb/fast-evaluate. Land in one commit before merging #2920.

Adds a new virtual method to AbstractState that performs vectorized, cache-bypassing direct evaluation with caller-allocated buffers (zero internal allocations), multi-output, per-point status flags, optional imposed phase, and Cython memoryview binding. First pass covers Tabular (Bicubic + TTSE) and IF97 backends. Plan: /Users/ianbell/.claude/plans/i-have-a-proposal-humming-kahn.md

title: Phase 2c — SVDSBTLBackend (AbstractState subclass, IF97-style)
description:
Phase 2c of the SVDSBTL rollout: ship a working SVDSBTL_BACKEND.  Plan at /Users/ianbell/.claude/plans/phase-2-svdsbtl.md.

ARCHITECTURE DECISION (deviation from plan):

The plan said "Inherits TabularBackend".  Code survey showed that TabularBackend's API is shaped around BicubicBackend's per-cell bicubic-grid model: 8 pure-virtual `evaluate_single_phase_*` methods that take `(i, j)` cell indices, `find_native_nearest_good_indices`, `find_nearest_neighbor`, `invert_single_phase_x/y`, etc.  All of these assume the lookup is a per-cell bicubic polynomial — wrong abstraction for SVDSBTL, where evaluation is region-atlas dispatch + a global rank-r SVD per region.

Also: TabularBackend::available_in_high_level() returns false, blocking PropsSI.  IF97Backend bypasses this by inheriting from AbstractState directly and gets PropsSI for free.

So the Phase 2c implementation inherits SVDSBTLBackend from AbstractState (mirroring IF97Backend), holds a std::unordered_map<input_pairs, std::unique_ptr<sbtl::SVDSurface>>, and uses the AbstractStateGenerator factory-registration pattern.  No structural concessions to the bicubic cell-grid model.

MVP SCOPE (this phase):
- PH (HmassP) and PT input pairs
- Output properties: rho, T, h, s, u (whatever the active surface stores)
- Pure-fluid only — explicit mixture rejection
- Lazy-build cache on first call (factory + serializer from Phase 2b)
- Critical region: no special handling in MVP (single-phase SVD already valid outside dome)
- Two-phase detection: deferred to a follow-up — SVDSurface itself can't represent two-phase

OUT OF SCOPE (deferred to follow-ups):
- DU, HS, PS surfaces (extensible via the same map-based dispatch)
- Critical-point fallback to direct HEOS
- Mixture support
- High-level PropsSI tests beyond pure-fluid PT and HP

FILES TO CHANGE:
- include/CoolProp/Backends/SVDSBTL/SVDSBTLBackend.h (new)
- src/Backends/SVDSBTL/SVDSBTLBackend.cpp (new)
- include/DataStructures.h — add SVDSBTL_BACKEND_FAMILY + SVDSBTL_BACKEND enum entries
- src/DataStructures.cpp — add to backend_family_list + backend_list
- src/AbstractState.cpp — register SVDSBTLBackendGenerator
- src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTL.cpp (new)
- CMakeLists.txt — pick up the new backend directory glob
notes:
MVP committed at 9d7b997fd, pushed to origin/ihb/svd-sbtl-backend-2c.  7 SVDSBTL Catch2 cases / 26 assertions green (combined 2042 across Phase 1 + 2b + 2c).  PR not opened yet — stacked on #2917 which is still in CI.

Deferred follow-ups (file separate beads if/when needed):
- Critical-region fallback to direct HEOS (subcritical surfaces stop short of p_crit)
- Two-phase detection (in-dome queries currently return NaN)
- DU / HS / PS / DT surfaces (extensible via the map dispatch)
- Performance benchmarks vs BICUBIC head-to-head
- Set _phase correctly post-update (currently best-effort iphase_twophase tag for outside-region)

title: CI: consolidate dev_* workflows + gate wheel matrix on event/label
description:
Two related CI improvements in one PR:

1. Consolidate the six dev_*.yml files (asan, clangformat, clangtidy, codeql, cppcheck, iwyu) into a single dev_checks.yml with parallel jobs. Preserves parallelism — just collapses the Actions UI from 6 separate workflow runs to one run with 6 jobs. Single checkout/setup overhead per workflow run.

2. Gate the python_buildwheels.yml matrix: on PRs build only a representative subset (~3 jobs: 1 Linux x86_64, 1 Windows AMD64, 1 macOS arm64, newest Python only). On master pushes / tags / a 'full-wheels' label, run the full 42-job matrix. Cuts ~93% of wheel CI minutes per PR while still catching cross-platform breakage pre-merge.

User asked for both in one PR.

title: SBTL conformance: L/G split by h_c at supercritical p
description: Kunick splits L vs G by h_c=2087.546845 kJ/kg (water) for all pressures.  CoolProp currently splits by sat-curve at p<p_crit and routes everything else through SUPER.  Add h_c-aware split for p>=p_crit so L (compressed liquid + supercritical liquid extension) and G (vapor + supercritical gas extension) each get own table.

Current SVDSBTL+IF97 accuracy (post foi.9.1-4, 6-8, 11 and pd6) meets the practical needs of the project; remaining items (atlas splits at R2/R3 + R1/R3 boundaries, TOMS748 h-inversion in IF97 sampling, Hermite bicubic backbone, multi-piece grids, L/G h_c split, separate HT region, Sphinx accuracy page, per-property fail-map) are conformance polish that can be reopened or refiled if/when stricter G13-15 conformance becomes a goal.

title: SBTL conformance: multi-piece grids per Kunick Tables A8-A10
description: Replace uniform 200x200 in (log p, xnorm) with piecewise grids matching G13-15 Tables A8-A10.  Up to ~400 nodes in p with different transformations per piece: p̄=p, p̄=√p, p̄=ln p depending on region.  Per-property grid density.
notes: From [hermite_accuracy] diagnostic (commit 9493011a4) the PH-path max-rho-error state sits at p/p_c=0.965, T/T_c=0.77 (subcritical liquid, just below p_crit).  This is exactly the region where h_sat,L(p) has a sqrt-cusp tangent and any single-piece Cheb fit's derivative goes wild.  Concrete multi-piece grid design that would help: add a tightly-packed isobar grid in p/p_c ∈ [0.95, 1.0] using sqrt-spacing (so the cusp gets resolved analytically) — Kunick uses up to ~30 nodes in this strip versus the current 1-2.  Also worth investigating: a near-critical Cheb piece on h_lo/h_hi with sqrt-of-(p_crit - p) coordinate instead of log p, which collapses the cusp to a linear function.

Current SVDSBTL+IF97 accuracy (post foi.9.1-4, 6-8, 11 and pd6) meets the practical needs of the project; remaining items (atlas splits at R2/R3 + R1/R3 boundaries, TOMS748 h-inversion in IF97 sampling, Hermite bicubic backbone, multi-piece grids, L/G h_c split, separate HT region, Sphinx accuracy page, per-property fail-map) are conformance polish that can be reopened or refiled if/when stricter G13-15 conformance becomes a goal.

title: SBTL conformance: Hermite bicubic on normph backbone
description: Replace cubic B-spline (corner values only) with Hermite bicubic (corner values + first derivatives + cross derivatives).  Each cell becomes a true 16-coefficient bicubic interpolant exact at corners in value, x-derivative, y-derivative, and cross-derivative.  Expected: ~3 order-of-magnitude improvement in v/T/s accuracy.  Requires populating ∂f/∂xnorm, ∂f/∂(log p), ∂²f/∂xnorm∂(log p) at each corner via HEOS first_partial_deriv / second_partial_deriv, then chain-ruling through (xnorm, log p) coordinate.  Chain rule depends on h_lo(p) / h_hi(p) sat-curve derivatives — needs Chebyshev derivative or finite-difference fallback.  Estimated 2-3 days.
notes:
ACCURACY VALIDATED on commit 454201ec7.  CO2 random PT sweep (n=200, seed 42):

  rho  Hermite  median=3.86e-08  p99=2.11e-04  max=6.61e-04
       Cubic    median=4.62e-08  p99=5.43e-04  max=1.59e-03
       ratio    median=0.84      p99=0.39      max=0.42

Hermite is 2.5× better at the tail (p99/max).  Median improvement is
modest (~17%) because the median ~4e-8 sits at the round-trip noise
floor of HEOS-supplied derivatives.  Steep-gradient cells (near-saturation,
supercritical-shoulder) get the biggest improvement — that's where the
old cubic-B-spline backbone's interpolation error dominated.

To close the remaining gap to G13-15 bounds (1e-12 in v, 10-25 mK in T,
etc.) we still need foi.2 / foi.4 / foi.5.  foi.1 is now essentially
complete: the Hermite math + chain rule are correct (verified by the
[hermite_accuracy] regression guard), and the path is default-on for
both normph and normpt tables.

title: Thread-safety: deriv_counter and IdealHelmholtzContainer cache
description:
Surfaced by ThreadSanitizer run during PR #2835 review.

# Issues

## 1. static int deriv_counter at HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend.cpp:41

```cpp
static int deriv_counter = 0;  // line 41
...
void HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend::calc_all_alphar_deriv_cache(...) {
    deriv_counter++;  // line 3328 — non-atomic global increment
    ...
}
```

File-scope counter incremented from any thread doing an EOS evaluation. Benign in the sense that nothing reads the counter for correctness, but a strict data race.

Fix: `static std::atomic<int> deriv_counter{0};` and `deriv_counter.fetch_add(1, std::memory_order_relaxed);`. ~2 lines.

## 2. IdealHelmholtzContainer cache race (potential)

The all-derivatives cache in IdealHelmholtzContainer (Helmholtz.h:742, 874, 1476) is mutated when called with cache_values=true. Multiple HEOS instances of the same fluid share one CoolPropFluid (via the library map), so concurrent calls into this code path race.

TSan attributed this race to calc_all_alphar_deriv_cache, which makes sense because that path eventually drives writes to the Ideal cache via residual_helmholtz->all(...).

Investigation needed: trace whether this is genuinely shared state across HEOS instances or if each HEOS gets its own Ideal container. If shared: needs a lock or per-thread caching.

## Reproduction
Build with -fsanitize=thread, run CatchTestRunner '[2773][thread_safety]'. The test (introduced in PR #2835) spawns 8 threads × 50 iterations on water HmolarQ flashes; TSan reports both races.

## Related
- CoolProp-6nh: Configuration singleton thread-safety
- CoolProp-58x: thread-safety audit
- CoolProp-anq: TabularDataLibrary + REFPROP thread-safety
- PR #2835's own caloric-superancillary code is TSan-clean — this is the surrounding pre-existing infrastructure
notes: GitHub issue: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/issues/2844

Remove dev_coverity.yml workflow, the Coverity badge in README.md, and the stale Coverity Scan section in Web/develop/testing.rst.

Background: PR #2794 (closes #2717) added a step to dump Coverity defects as JSON in CI. That step is broken end-to-end — scan.coverity.com's free build-tool tarball ships only cov-build (capture); cov-analyze/cov-format-errors are licensed components, so the Analyze step exits 127 with 'cov-analyze: command not found'. Verified on dispatched run 25107196032. The Scan free service also doesn't expose a project-token-authenticated REST API for retrieving defects (only the web-UI's session-cookie 'table.json' endpoint, which is undocumented and brittle).

CodeQL (.github/workflows/dev_codeql.yml, cpp + python with security-and-quality queries) and cppcheck (.github/workflows/dev_cppcheck.yml, advisory artifact) already provide CI-side static analysis with machine-readable output. Removing Coverity simplifies the matrix and frees the weekly Coverity Scan submission quota for nothing.

title: EPIC: Dynamic h/s/rho saturation superancillaries with Chebyshev extrema-finding (#2773 root cause)
description:
Replace the static h_L / h_LV / s_L / s_LV ancillaries (currently fit at JSON-build time with a baked-in reference state) with dynamic Chebyshev superancillaries constructed at fluid load time, mirroring the teqpflsh ChebyshevApproximation1D design.

# Why
The existing h/s ancillaries are polynomial fits with bounded accuracy and a frozen reference-state convention; they silently disagree with the EOS when the user calls set_reference_state. They also lack derivatives suitable for extrema location, which is what GitHub #2773 actually needs (branch selection on saturated-vapor enthalpy / saturated-liquid density max for water and D2O).

# Design (canonical Chebyshev recipe per teqpflsh::ChebyshevApproximation1D)
- Piecewise Chebyshev expansion of h_sat_L(T), h_sat_V(T), s_sat_L(T), s_sat_V(T) built at HEOS load by sampling (T, rho_sat(T)) → EOS → h, s at Chebyshev-Lobatto nodes, dyadic-split for tolerance.
- At construction, find extrema by: take Chebyshev derivative coefficients, build companion matrix, balance, compute eigenvalues (via Eigen — already vendored), filter |imag|<1e-15 and real in [-1,1], sort.
- Partition [Tmin, Tcrit] into monotonic IntervalMatch records.
- At runtime, branch selection becomes: O(log K) lookup of interval containing guess.T, then TOMS748 within that monotonic interval.

# Reference state — addressed cleanly
A constant shift in h or s only changes c_0 of the Chebyshev coefficients; derivative coefficients are unchanged, so extrema locations are invariant under reference-state change. set_reference_state shifts c_0 only, no rebuild. (Same trick for s_ref + h_ref - T_ref·s_ref shift via two-constant lookup.)

# Expected runtime cost (from teqpflsh benchmarks)
- Forward eval ~16 ns per call
- Inverse with branch selection ~0.5 μs
- Full DH / DS / HS / HQ / SQ flash ~2-3 μs
- Construction: ~50K flops per superancillary (~ms at fluid load), one-time

# Migration plan
1. Port ChebyshevApproximation1D + companion-matrix extrema finder into CoolProp under src/superancillary/.
2. Build h_sat_{L,V}(T), s_sat_{L,V}(T) at HEOS construction; cache on backend.
3. Add saturation_extrema(parameter) API on the backend (covers iDmolar, iHmolar, iSmolar uniformly).
4. Migrate FlashRoutines::DQ_flash_with_guesses (currently a 64-point coarse scan) to use this API.
5. Add HQ_flash_with_guesses + SQ_flash_with_guesses + dispatch in HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend::update_with_guesses for HmolarQ_INPUTS / QSmolar_INPUTS.
6. Migrate SaturationSolvers::saturation_PHSU_pure IMPOSED_HV/SV/HL/SL branches to use the new superancillaries instead of inverting hL/sL ancillaries via Brent. (This likely fixes the pre-existing QS_flash crash from CoolProp-sxc as a side effect.)
7. Replace Brent with boost::math::tools::toms748_solve on new code paths.
8. Mark JSON hL/hLV/sL/sLV fields deprecated; remove in a major release once nothing reads them.

# Subsumes
- CoolProp-k7y (HQ branch selection — the existing IMPOSED_HV options.T plumbing becomes unnecessary)
- CoolProp-sxc (SQ branch + crash — the inversion machinery that crashes is replaced wholesale)
- CoolProp-60n (DQ via 64-point scan becomes the same Chebyshev-extrema lookup)

# References
- teqpflsh source: include/teqpflsh/superancillary/superancillary.hpp (ChebyshevApproximation1D::determine_extrema, build_monotonic_intervals)
- Docs: https://ianhbell.github.io/teqpflsh-nist-docs/en/latest/superancillary/SuperancillaryIterations.html
- Bell et al. superancillary literature already cited in CoolProp
notes:
Initial slice of the EPIC has landed locally on branch ib/docs-pubchem-cache-commit:
- has_variable() accessor added to SuperAncillary
- add_variable() fixed to work with SuperAncillary<std::vector<double>> (was Eigen-only)
- ensure_caloric_superancillaries() on HEOS lazily builds h_sat_{L,V} and s_sat_{L,V}
- DQ_flash_with_guesses, HQ_flash_with_guesses, QS_flash_with_guesses use TOMS748 monotonic-interval rootfinding via existing get_x_for_y, then refine
- update_with_guesses dispatches DmolarQ_INPUTS, HmolarQ_INPUTS, QSmolar_INPUTS
- Tests: 18 assertions across DQ (water density max), HQ (water sat-vap h non-monotonicity around 540 K peak), QS (smoke test)
- Regression: 1853 [flash][saturation] assertions still pass

Still TODO before this can subsume CoolProp-k7y / CoolProp-sxc:
- saturation_PHSU_pure migration (currently saturation_PHSU_pure still inverts the static hL/sL ancillaries; new flashes bypass it)
- Reference-state shift-c0 trick (currently rebuild on set_reference_state)
- Deprecate JSON hL/hLV/sL/sLV fields
- Verify QS_flash crash from CoolProp-sxc is/isn't fixed by the new path

title: Cheb tooling: nightly CI test set for density rootfinder validation
description: Build and run a large (T,p,z) test grid against the Chebyshev mixture density rootfinder, validating against production update(PT_INPUTS) density. Coverage: (1) pure fluids — methane, nitrogen, CO2, water, R-1234yf, n-decane (mix of light/heavy/polar/non-analytic-term-bearing); (2) binary mixtures — CH4/H2S, CO2/N2, n-decane/methane, CO2/water, R-32/R-134a; (3) multi-component — Amarillo AGA-8 (10-comp), GERG-2008 reference set; (4) hard regions — near-critical bands (|τ-1|<0.05, |δ-1|<0.5), low-T heavy mixtures, retrograde corners. Per point: assert |Δρ|/ρ_ref < 1e-8 (or fall-back-to-global, classified). Run grid sizes large enough that nightly is meaningful — target ~100k cells × 10 fluid systems = ~1M point checks. Output CSV + summary failure-class histogram. Wire into .github/workflows/ as a nightly cron.
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow file in .github/workflows/cheb-nightly.yml; binary in dev/ that emits per-point pass/fail CSV; failure-class summary printed; nightly runs green for at least 7 consecutive days before promotion to required-status check.

title: SQ branch-selection + pre-existing QS_flash crash (#2773 follow-up)
description:
Splitting from CoolProp-60n. Two issues:

1. QS_flash (default, no-guesses path) segfaults for water saturated vapor at s ~ 112 J/mol/K AND for n-Propane saturated liquid at s ~ 54.7 J/mol/K. Reproduces with: AS->update(QSmolar_INPUTS, Q, s_target). Investigate root cause in SaturationSolvers::saturation_PHSU_pure IMPOSED_SL/SV branches.

2. Once the crash is fixed, add SmolarQ_INPUTS to HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend::update_with_guesses dispatching to SQ_flash_with_guesses, threading guess.T through to bracket the saturation_PHSU_pure search (mirroring the IMPOSED_HV change in the HQ follow-up bead).

title: HQ branch-selection requires saturation_PHSU_pure IMPOSED_HV to honor options.T (#2773 follow-up)
description:
Splitting from CoolProp-60n. The HQ flash currently routes through SaturationSolvers::saturation_PHSU_pure with IMPOSED_HV, which performs Brent on the residual h_g(T) - h_target over the FULL saturation range and ignores options.T. To actually enable branch selection on non-monotonic h_g (water saturated vapor between triple and critical), the IMPOSED_HV branch must use options.T as a bracket center when options.use_guesses is true, and walk outward to find a sign-change bracket containing only one root.

Two pre-existing issues to fix in the same change:
1. FlashRoutines.cpp:307 has 'if (Tguess < 0)' — the condition is INVERTED. The block inside assigns options.T = Tguess (the sentinel -1) and calls ancillary.evaluate(-1). Should be 'if (Tguess > 0)'.
2. After fix #1, propagate options.T into the IMPOSED_HV solver path.

Also add: HmolarQ_INPUTS case in HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend::update_with_guesses dispatching to HQ_flash_with_guesses, plus tests for water HQ at h_target near saturation-vapor enthalpy maximum (~540 K).

title: Branch-selection for DmolarQ flash via update_with_guesses (#2773 partial fix)
description:
GitHub #2773: water/D2O saturated liquid density has a maximum near 4 °C (water) / 11 °C (D2O), so DQ inputs can have two T solutions for the same density on the saturated liquid branch. Currently CoolProp silently picks one root via Brent over the full saturation range.

This bead covers the DmolarQ case only. HQ and SQ are tracked in CoolProp-k7y and CoolProp-sxc respectively (they require deeper changes to SaturationSolvers::saturation_PHSU_pure and a separate pre-existing QS_flash crash investigation).

Scope:
1. Add DmolarQ_INPUTS case to HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend::update_with_guesses, dispatching to a new DQ_flash_with_guesses.
2. DQ_flash_with_guesses scans the full saturation range in coarse steps, identifies all sign-change intervals on the residual rho_L(T) - rho_target, and Brent-solves the interval whose midpoint is closest to guess.T.
3. Tests: water DQ near density max with two distinct T-roots verified by guess.T disambiguation.
notes: Superseded by CoolProp-cew. The 64-point coarse-scan approach was abandoned in favor of dynamic Chebyshev superancillaries with companion-matrix extrema-finding (teqpflsh-style). Local changes reverted; no commits on this branch.

title: dev_clangformat.yml uses unpinned apt clang-format; disagrees with .pre-commit-config.yaml
description:
The project pins clang-format to 18.1.8 in .pre-commit-config.yaml (introduced in #2788, bumped in #2795), and master was whole-repo reformatted with that pin in #2803. But .github/workflows/dev_clangformat.yml runs the unpinned apt clang-format (currently 18.1.3 on ubuntu-latest), bypassing the pin entirely.

This bites whenever a PR touches a file that contains lines on which 18.1.3 and 18.1.8 disagree — the script reformats the whole file, so even untouched lines get rewritten by the wrong version, and CI fails on patches that perfectly match the project's actual pin.

Concrete example hit on PR #2831: src/HumidAirProp.cpp:534 has '~BrentSolverResids() {};' (18.1.8's output, also on master), but CI's 18.1.3 wants '~BrentSolverResids(){};'.

The pre-commit-config comment claims '18.x patches are bug-fix-only with no formatting-rule changes' — that's empirically wrong; 18.1.3 and 18.1.8 produce different output for empty-body destructors.

Fix: make dev_clangformat.yml install the pinned 18.1.8 (e.g. via 'pip install clang-format==18.1.8' before running the script), or just have the workflow run the pre-commit hook directly (pre-commit run --all-files clang-format on changed files). Should also fix the misleading comment in .pre-commit-config.yaml.

Optionally: add a version check in dev/ci/clang-format.sh that fails fast if the running clang-format doesn't match the pin, so devs running it locally with the wrong version get a clear error instead of a silent diff.

title: Thread-safety: TabularDataLibrary + REFPROP global state
description:
Two remaining areas after fluid-init and humid-air fixes:

1. src/Backends/Tabular/TabularBackends.cpp:~24 — static TabularDataLibrary library; with std::map populated lazily by get_set_of_tables() (~line 1300). Concurrent first-loads of the same table path race on std::map::insert and on the per-set tables_loaded flag. Fix: std::mutex around the lookup-and-insert, or a per-path std::call_once.

2. src/Backends/REFPROP/REFPROPMixtureBackend.cpp — REFPROP is inherently single-threaded (the underlying Fortran library has process-global state). Globals: LoadedREFPROPRef (string of currently-loaded mixture, line ~60), instance_counter (non-atomic ++/--, line ~217), dbg_refprop. Two threads using different REFPROP mixtures simultaneously corrupt the REFPROP state machine and may double-free on cleanup. Fix: wrap REFPROP entry points with a global mutex, OR clearly document that REFPROP requires external serialization. instance_counter → atomic<size_t>.

title: Thread-safety: Configuration singleton missing call_once
CI: Windows Installer arm64 DLL build fails — hashes.json has trailing duplicate data
description:
The Windows Installer workflow (.github/workflows/windows_installer.yml, "build" job) fails on PR #2826 (and reproduces on other recent PRs) with:

  CUSTOMBUILD : decoding error : Extra data: line 1 column 1010 (char 1009)
  [D:\a\CoolProp\CoolProp\build\32bitDLL_cdecl\generate_headers.vcxproj]
  [D:\a\CoolProp\CoolProp\build\COOLPROP_WINDOWS_PACKAGE_SHARED_LIBRARIES.vcxproj]

The dumped hashes.json content shows the first valid JSON object followed by extra trailing data — the tail of a longer earlier write is leaking through:

  {"version": "...", ..., "mixture_binary_pairs_pcsaft_JSON": "..."}re_binary_pairs_pcsaft_JSON": "..."}

Most likely cause: hashes.json is being written twice with different lengths (probably across the parallel 32-bit / 64-bit / arm64 sub-builds in the same workspace), and one write only partially overwrites the other. Need an atomic write (write-temp-then-rename) or unique-per-arch path for the file in dev_macros / dev_packages workflow.

Affects every PR — this isn't a regression from any recent merge. File so we can untangle it from GUI smoke-test triage.

Run that hit it: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/actions/runs/25050107758/job/73375363601
notes: Fix shipped as PR #2833 (https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/pull/2833) on branch fix/issue-2828-hashes-json-atomic. Atomic write via tempfile.mkstemp + os.replace in dev/generate_headers.py; stress-tested with 64 concurrent writers. Will close once the Windows Installer CI run on the PR confirms the race is gone.
close_reason: Fixed by PR #2833 (merged 2026-04-28). Atomic write of dev/hashes.json eliminates the parallel-sub-build race.

title: GUI smoke test: detect blank-window regressions, not just process liveness
description:
The current Windows/macOS/Linux smoke tests in .github/workflows/gui_builder.yml only verify the binary stays alive past an 8-second sleep. A blank-WebView render passes that check (gh-2825 / PR #2826), so a frontend regression that 404s every JS asset got published in alpha.1.

Add a render-validation step. Cheapest reliable signal for our app: pixel std-dev (a uniform gradient is < ~0.05; a populated UI is > ~0.15). The captured PNG is already on disk by the time the check runs, so the addition is one ImageMagick (Linux/macOS) or PowerShell System.Drawing (Windows) call.
acceptance_criteria: On a future regression that produces a blank Tauri window, the smoke-test step in CI fails with a clear message — not the existing 'binary alive 8s ✓' green.
notes:
macOS + Windows render checks shipped in PR #2826 / alpha.2:
- wrappers/GUI/scripts/check-screenshot-rendered.py (Pillow std-dev)
- gui_builder.yml: install Pillow inline, run check post-screenshot, upload artifact even on failure (if: always)
- Verified: blank=13.987 fails, rendered=49.992-50.090 passes, threshold=20

Still open under this issue:
- Linux Xvfb capture fixes — currently 1-bit-black PNG because no window manager composites the GUI window onto the virtual display. Add openbox or metacity before launch and the same render-check Linux's smoke step.
- Follow-up CoolProp-8j0 (P3) replaces the fixed Start-Sleep with a polling render-check to absorb WebView2 cold-start jitter (caught a flake on alpha.2 — same byte-identical Windows binary went 14 then 50 std-dev across two runs of the same commit).

title: GUI smoke test: detect blank-window regressions, not just process liveness
description:
The current Windows/macOS/Linux smoke tests in .github/workflows/gui_builder.yml only verify the binary stays alive past an 8-second sleep. A blank-WebView render passes that check (gh-2825 / PR #2826), so a frontend regression that 404s every JS asset got published in alpha.1.

title: Cheb tooling: common-partition P(δ) builder (eliminate runtime dyadic_splitting)
description: 42i tuning showed per-call solve time stays at 400-900 μs even after phi-build optimization, because the runtime dyadic_splitting of P(δ) itself dominates. Structural fix: build all phi_k on a SHARED piece partition (e.g., a fixed partition refined to cover the worst term's needs). At runtime, on each shared piece: linear-combine coef vectors (Σ κ_k · phi_k coefs), then directly compute the P(δ) coefs on that piece via Chebyshev product P(δ) = ρ_red·R·T·(δ + δ²·α^r_δ). No runtime sampling, no runtime dyadic_splitting. Expected speedup 10-50×, putting per-call solve in the 5-20 μs target.
acceptance_criteria: Per-call density solve <50 μs on methane test points; rel rho error <1e-8 vs production.
notes: v2 adaptive partition shipped — 29 pieces (vs v1 uniform 64), 17 μs median per-call (vs v1's 30 μs), 118 KB memory, 1.27e-14 rel rho err on 6 methane test points. Mixture-polynomial M-norm metric (Bell-Alpert §3.3 Eq 54) computed on Σκ_k(τ)·phi_d_k coefs at τ-pivot grid; max taken. Per-term M-norm was wrong (over-splits because of cancellation in linear combine — 581 pieces). 17 μs beats the paper's 30 μs no-τ-flattening reference on modern hardware. Implementation dev/cheb_density_v2.cpp. Remaining: (1) extend to mixtures with departure functions; (2) port to production headers and integrate with EquationOfState (CoolProp-s5d).

title: Cheb tooling: tune dyadic-splitting tolerance + piece budget
description: Phase-C empirical: methane built 73,735 dyadic pieces total (avg 1843/term) at tol=1e-12 for the 40 GenExp terms. Memory ~660 KB/fluid, vs spec target ~50 KB/fluid (13× over). Production needs looser tol (1e-8 should be plenty given downstream 1e-6 rootfind), and possibly a max-pieces cap. Tune and re-measure on methane + nitrogen + CO2 reference EOS.
close_reason: Tuning swept tol × degree on methane. Recommended: tol=1e-8, n=12 (2567 phi pieces, 521 KB, 3.7 ms build, 490 μs per-call solve, 6e-12 rel err). Reduced memory by 20× and build by 60× from baseline. Per-call solve dominated by runtime dyadic_splitting of P(δ), not phi pieces — that needs the structural fix in CoolProp-89d (common piece partitions + direct Chebyshev product). Findings saved via bd remember; harness in dev/cheb_density_tune.cpp.

title: Chebyshev tooling: user-visible API + configuration
description: How callers ask the backend to use the Chebyshev rootfinder. Options to design: (a) drop-in replacement of solver_rho_Tp behind a configuration flag (similar to imposed_phase_index pattern); (b) a separate solver_rho_Tp_chebyshev entry point that callers explicitly invoke; (c) automatic fallback inside solver_rho_Tp when the Chebyshev tooling is loaded. Pick one, document, expose in PropsSImulti / AbstractState.
acceptance_criteria: API decision recorded; flag wired through config_keys and HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend; example in docs.

title: Chebyshev tooling: C++ load + EquationOfState integration
description: Wire the per-term φₖ(δ) Chebyshev expansions into EquationOfState. Mirror the existing SuperAncillary_t pattern: a TermDensityChebyshev_t (or similar) shared_ptr on the EOS, loaded lazily from JSON when present, built in-memory from the EOS term list when JSON is missing. Expose a const accessor on HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend.
acceptance_criteria: EOS load picks up JSON blobs; missing blobs trigger C++ fallback build. Per-fluid cost amortized; verified by load-time benchmark.

title: Chebyshev tooling: JSON generation tool (offline)
description: External Python tool (analogous to fastchebpure) that takes a fluid's residual Helmholtz term list and emits a JSON blob containing the precomputed per-term φₖ(δ) Chebyshev expansions on the common rectangle [1e-10, 6]. Output extends the existing dev/fluids/<fluid>.json schema, mirroring the saturation superanc shipping pattern.
acceptance_criteria: Tool runs against an EOS coefficient set, produces JSON with per-term phi-blobs validated against direct evaluation to 1e-12 within rectangle. CI hook regenerates blobs when EOS coefficients change.

title: EPIC: Chebyshev mixture EOS tooling — per-term δ-expansions
description: Standalone Chebyshev infrastructure for multifluid Helmholtz EOS, designed around per-term δ-expansions with τ-prefactors evaluated closed-form. Spec at dev/cheb_mixture_spec.md (CoolProp-3gt). Useful well beyond density rootfinding (table walks, isolines, spinodal location, phase-envelope tracing). Ships independently of the Gernert-vs-Chebyshev assessment (CoolProp-zkc); the assessment continues to consume the rootfinder this epic produces.
design: Single common reduced-δ rectangle [1e-10, 6]. JSON-shipped + C++ fallback for term storage. Non-separable terms (NonAnalytic, SAFTAssociating, GenCubic) dropped; near-critical (|τ-1|<0.10 AND |δ-1|<1.0) falls back to solver_rho_Tp_global. Colleague-matrix rootfinder with interval-pruning, closed-form low-degree, Newton polish. Targets: 5-20 μs per density solve, 0 runtime build, ~1.8 MB total memory for 10-comp Amarillo.

title: Fluid-library static init isn't thread-safe (cold-cache race)
description:
Static init of the fluid library races when multiple threads hit a cold cache simultaneously. FluidsList comes back partially populated with duplicate single-fluid entries (e.g. "D4,D4,D4,D4,R125") and any subsequent fluid lookup fails with "key [X] was not found in string_to_index_map in JSONFluidLibrary".

Reproduced on macOS 14 (M1) and ubuntu-22.04 via the GUI's Rust test binary on branch ib/GUI; cargo test default parallelism triggers it reliably. --test-threads=1 makes it green.

Proposed fix: guard the JSONFluidLibrary singleton init with std::call_once or a mutex around the first-time population of string_to_index_map.

Tracked upstream as CoolProp/CoolProp#2787.
acceptance_criteria: After the fix, cargo test in wrappers/GUI/src-tauri passes with default parallelism (no --test-threads=1 flag needed). The CI step in gui_builder.yml can drop the flag.
close_reason: Closed upstream as #2800 (merged 2026-04-26). Five lazy-init sites in CoolProp protected with std::call_once: FluidLibrary, IncompressibleLibrary, MixtureBinaryPairLibrary + MixtureDepartureFunctionsLibrary (per-instance), and HumidAirProp::check_fluid_instantiation. PCSAFTLibrary and CubicsLibrary additionally converted to Meyers singletons for static-init-order safety. The GUI workflow's --test-threads=1 workaround was reverted in c08c5c27 — cargo test runs in default parallel again.

title: Tier 4.1 — One-shot whole-repo clang-format pass + .git-blame-ignore-revs
description:
Apply clang-format to every committed C/C++ file under src/ and include/ in a single PR. Add the merge SHA to .git-blame-ignore-revs so git blame skips it. Document the local config: git config blame.ignoreRevsFile .git-blame-ignore-revs.

Why now: enabling stricter diff-based gates (Tier 2) is cleaner if the legacy formatting backlog is gone — otherwise PR diffs constantly tangle with formatting churn near touched lines. Must land after Tier 1.1 so the master push CI does not re-light over the noisy commit.

Risk: noisy commit. Mitigations: separate PR with no functional changes, an explicit blame-ignore-revs entry, no semantic diffs.

Acceptance:
- After the reformat lands, dev/ci/clang-format.sh HEAD master produces no diff
- .git-blame-ignore-revs file exists at repo root and contains the reformat SHA
- dev/ci/README documents the blame-ignore-revs config
- Test suite passes unchanged on the reformat commit
notes: Merged to master in 6d2eb695 (#2803) + 4079edc8 (#2804) — whole-repo format + .git-blame-ignore-revs.

title: Tier 2.3 — Generate compile_commands.json from CMake
description:
clang-tidy (Tier 2.1, 2.2) and IWYU (Tier 3.4) both need compile_commands.json. CMake supports CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON; verify it produces a usable JSON for the standard CoolProp build options used in CI.

Action:
- Verify cmake -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON -DCOOLPROP_MY_MAIN=dev/ci/main.cpp -B build -S . produces compile_commands.json with no unresolved-include errors when fed to clang-tidy
- If header paths are missing for src/ files, augment per-target include directives in CMakeLists.txt
- Document the build command in dev/ci/README

Acceptance: clang-tidy --warnings-as-errors=* (using the .clang-tidy config) runs cleanly against a sample src/ file using only the generated compile_commands.json — no module/include surprises.
notes: Merged to master in d9af3845 (#2789) — always export compile_commands.json.

title: Tier 2.1 — New workflow: clang-tidy-diff on PR-touched lines
description:
clang-tidy-diff.py (ships with LLVM) runs clang-tidy only on lines changed in a unified diff. This avoids drowning PR authors in the legacy 937KLOC warning backlog while still enforcing the existing strict .clang-tidy config (WarningsAsErrors: *) on new code.

Action:
- New .github/workflows/dev_clangtidy.yml; pull_request trigger only (initially)
- Build with -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=ON (depends on Tier 2.3)
- git diff origin/<base>..HEAD -U0 | clang-tidy-diff.py -p1 -path build (inherits checks + WarningsAsErrors from .clang-tidy)
- Fail the job if any warning surfaces on changed lines

Acceptance:
- A contrived PR with a deliberate violation on changed lines fails
- A PR that does not touch a flagged-but-legacy file passes
- Runs in <10 min on a typical PR (lines, not whole project)
notes: Merged to master in cc46e3fb (#2798) — diff-only clang-tidy workflow on PRs.

title: Tier 1.4 — Document local code-quality workflow

One page so a new contributor can satisfy every gate without spelunking. Likely home: dev/ci/README.md (new) or extend CONTRIBUTING.md.

Acceptance: a new contributor can read one page and run all the local checks; doc is referenced from README.

title: Tier 1.3 — Add .pre-commit-config.yaml with clang-format hook

Catch clang-format violations before contributors push. Use the standard pre-commit framework with the upstream mirrors-clang-format hook.

Acceptance: contributors who pip install pre-commit && pre-commit install get clang-format auto-checks on staged C/C++ files.

title: Tier 1.2 — Add CMake format target for repo-wide clang-format
description:
Give contributors a one-liner to format the codebase: cmake --build build --target format. Mirror the find pattern from dev/ci/clang-format.sh so behavior matches CI.

Acceptance: cmake --build build --target format formats every committed C/C++ source under src/ and include/ and matches dev/ci/clang-format.sh output exactly.

title: [epic] C++ code quality gates: clang-format, clang-tidy, cppcheck, CodeQL, one-shot reformat
description:
Lightweight code-quality program for the C++ codebase, driven by the observation that clang-format CI has been failing on master and getting ignored. Goal: gates that are actually satisfiable so they stop being bypassed.

Approach — four tiers, all to be delivered:
- Tier 1 — Stop the bleeding: green up clang-format, add local hooks/targets, switch CI to PR-only triggers.
- Tier 2 — clang-tidy on PR-diff only (not the 937KLOC backlog).
- Tier 3 — Reactivate dormant tools (cppcheck, CodeQL, Coverity, IWYU) with PR-friendly triggers; warning-only where appropriate.
- Tier 4 — One-shot whole-repo clang-format pass + safe clang-tidy --fix, with .git-blame-ignore-revs.

Design constraint: diff-only enforcement. Do not gate on legacy 937KLOC code; only flag new violations. Children carry the per-task detail.
