title: Assessment: decision memo (which method ships)
description: Synthesize benchmark + robustness results into a recommendation: ship Gernert, ship Chebyshev, ship both behind a switch, or keep production as-is. Include rollout plan and any flags/options needed.
acceptance_criteria: Memo committed to dev/ as a markdown file; key non-obvious findings saved via bd remember.

title: Assessment: robustness test set
description: Curated set of (T,p,z) points known to be hard: near-critical loci, three-phase-adjacent compositions, very-low-T heavy hydrocarbons, very-high-p, metastable wells. Each method runs against this set and is scored on convergence rate and accuracy vs REFPROP/known answer.
acceptance_criteria: Test set lives in dev/density_rootfinding_hardpoints.csv; harness consumes it and emits a per-method scorecard.

title: Assessment: unified benchmark harness
description: Extend dev/bench_ch4_h2s_grid.cpp into a method-agnostic harness that runs all three solvers (production, Gernert, Chebyshev) over the same (T,p,z) grid, emitting CSV with method, timing, iterations, residual, and result density. Reuse existing CSV format where possible.
acceptance_criteria: One CSV per fluid system, one binary that takes --method=production|gernert|chebyshev.

title: Chebyshev: test suite
description: Mirror the Gernert test suite structure for solver_rho_Tp_Chebyshev. Particular attention to behavior at the rectangle boundaries (must error rather than extrapolate, per design constraint).

title: Chebyshev: implement mixture density rootfinder
description: Implement the Chebyshev-based mixture density rootfinder per spec. Reuse the pure-fluid Chebyshev infrastructure from the superanc work where possible (worktree-cheb has prior art). Entry point: solver_rho_Tp_Chebyshev, parallel to solver_rho_Tp_Gernert.
acceptance_criteria: Builds cleanly; smoke test on AGA-8 grid; behind a flag.
notes: Phase A (per-term factorization), C (real-fluid alphar reconstruction on methane), D (density rootfind matches production rho at 1e-13 rel err) — all PASS in dev/cheb_density_poc.cpp. Per-term phi(delta) builds via dyadic_splitting; runtime P(delta) build via second-order dyadic_splitting; per-piece TOMS748 rootfind. 6 test points (gas, dense gas, subcrit liquid, supercritical, cryogenic liquid, high-p) all match production at 1e-13 to 1e-15 relative error. Multi-root detection works (cryogenic liquid: 3 roots returned). Boundary fix: eval_phi_cheb must clamp to piece bounds (Chebyshev nodes can fall ULP-outside via cos(π)). Remaining: mixture support (Phase E), HEOS integration (s5d), performance tuning (42i).

title: Chebyshev: spec mixture extension
description: Pure-fluid Chebyshev superancillary uses τ-only (or τ,δ on a rectangle) expansions. For mixtures, composition z is a new degree of freedom and a hard rectangle in (τ,δ) is composition-dependent (reducing functions). Three candidate strategies to evaluate before coding: (1) build the Chebyshev expansion at runtime per composition (slow setup, fast solve); (2) interpolate a tensor of Chebyshev coefficients in z (memory-heavy, fast setup); (3) hybrid — build only when a (T,p) call cannot be served by current expansion. Pick one, justify, write spec.
design: Constraint from memory: hard-rectangle in (τ,δ), no extrapolation; δ-compactification was tried and rejected (Jacobian blow-up). Target n=8–12 per piece, single-digit μs.
acceptance_criteria: Chosen strategy + rationale + pseudocode in --notes.
notes: Spec complete. All 5 open questions resolved. Architecture: per-term Chebyshev in delta only (kappa(tau) factored out, evaluated closed-form). Single common reduced-delta rectangle [1e-10, 6]. JSON-preferred + C++ fallback for phi(delta) building. Non-separable terms (NonAnalytic, SAFTAssociating, GenCubic) silently dropped — Chebyshev path is good-enough away from critical, near-critical falls back to solver_rho_Tp_global. Critical neighborhood detected geometrically in (tau, delta) space (|tau-1|<0.10 AND |delta-1|<1.0). Colleague-matrix eigenvalue rootfind with 6 layered optimizations (interval-bound pruning, closed-form low-degree, Newton polish, single P(delta) build for all derivatives, exponent-grouped kappa eval). Spec at dev/cheb_mixture_spec.md.
close_reason: Spec complete with all 5 open questions resolved by Ian. Full document at dev/cheb_mixture_spec.md. Architecture matches the 'expansions in delta only' direction Ian had previously decided. Implementation is unblocked (CoolProp-oja).

title: Gernert: test suite
description: Tests for solver_rho_Tp_Gernert covering: pure fluid (degenerates correctly), simple binary, AGA-8 mixture, near-critical, and known-hard low-T case. Cross-check density against solver_rho_Tp where the latter converges, and against REFPROP where available.
acceptance_criteria: Test cases live in src/Tests/ following the mixture-pair test pattern (see memory).

title: Gernert: implement density rootfinder
description: Implement the Gernert density-rootfinding scheme as a new entry point alongside solver_rho_Tp. Do NOT replace the production path yet — keep both available behind a flag for benchmarking. Suggested location: src/Backends/Helmholtz/HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend.cpp, named solver_rho_Tp_Gernert.
acceptance_criteria: Builds cleanly; passes a smoke test on the AGA-8 Amarillo grid; behind a build/runtime flag so production path is undisturbed.

title: Gernert: spec the density-rootfinding algorithm
description: Gernert (2014) PhD thesis, TU Bochum. The thesis is already cited in VLERoutines.cpp for stability analysis but NOT for the density-rootfinding inner loop. Read §3 of the thesis (and the 2014 J. Chem. Thermo. paper if relevant), extract the algorithm: bracketing strategy, Newton/safeguarded steps, termination criteria, special-case handling near the spinodal. Produce a pseudocode spec in the issue notes.
design: Output is a spec, not code. The spec drives the implementation issue.
acceptance_criteria: Pseudocode + cited equation numbers + list of edge cases in --notes.

title: Baseline: characterize solver_rho_Tp failure modes
description: Before adding new solvers, document where the current path breaks. Run dev/bench_ch4_h2s_grid.cpp pattern across: (1) Amarillo natural gas grid (already wired), (2) CH4/H2S, (3) a near-critical CO2/N2 case, (4) low-T heavy mix. Record per (T,p,z): wallclock, iteration count, which fallback tier resolved it, and any failure. Output CSV + a short markdown summary.
acceptance_criteria: CSV of grid results checked into dev/; one-paragraph failure-mode summary saved via bd remember.
close_reason: Baseline characterized across 4 systems (amarillo/ch4h2s/co2n2/c10c1, 60x60 PT grids). CSV+PNG artifacts in dev/, summary in dev/bench_density_BASELINE.md. Key finding: solver_rho_Tp(rho_guess=-1) fails on 25-65% of cells where the full flash succeeds, clustered along phase boundaries. Findings saved via bd remember. Tier instrumentation gap filed as CoolProp-29n.

title: EPIC: Mixture density rootfinding — Gernert vs Chebyshev assessment
description: Production solver is HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend::solver_rho_Tp (SRK guess + Newton, with solver_rho_Tp_global fallback). It fails or is slow in: near-critical regions, low-T heavy-hydrocarbon mixtures, and metastable branches inside the phase envelope. We have two candidate replacements: (A) Gernert's density-rootfinding scheme (Gernert PhD thesis 2014, TU Bochum), and (B) Chebyshev rootfinding extended from the pure-fluid superancillary work. Goal: implement both, benchmark on a common grid, and decide which (if either) replaces the production path.
design: Hard requirement: any replacement must be at least as robust as solver_rho_Tp_global on the existing test suite. Stretch: single-digit μs per evaluation in the bulk of the (T,p,z) domain.

title: Apply modernize-use-override --fix sweep (CoolProp-2uw.13 follow-up)
description: Follow-up to closed 2uw.13: the original plan for Tier 4.2 included modernize-use-override but only nullptr/emplace/deprecated-headers (PR #2805) and init-variables (PR #2808) actually landed. With AllowOverrideAndFinal=true + IgnoreDestructors=true, apply the use-override fix-it across src/ and include/CoolProp/, run clang-format on top, build + Catch [SBTL]/full suite, and append the SHA to .git-blame-ignore-revs. Also re-enable modernize-use-override in .clang-tidy so -Winconsistent-missing-override doesn't regress. Motivation: -Winconsistent-missing-override compiler noise on local builds.

title: Investigate register_optional<double> non-effect under -O3 in CoolProp WASM build
description:
Bead CoolProp-71a.4 intended to register std::optional<double> so VectorDouble.get(i) is readable from JS. Adding register_optional<double>() inside EMSCRIPTEN_BINDINGS(abstract_state_bindings) compiles and links cleanly but generates no _embind_register_optional import in the resulting wasm — every other _embind_register_* (17 of them) is there but not this one. JS-side runtime then throws 'Cannot call VectorDouble.get due to unbound types: NSt3__28optionalIdEE' for any element read.

Diagnostic findings (2026-05-24):
- static_assert(__cplusplus >= 201703L) at the binding site compiles, confirming C++17 is active.
- Minimal repro: a standalone .cpp file with the same EMSCRIPTEN_BINDINGS block + register_vector<double> + register_optional<double> built with em++ -std=gnu++17 -O2 -lembind -sMODULARIZE=1 -sEXPORT_ES6=1 DOES generate the _embind_register_optional import and works.
- The full CoolProp build uses /emsdk/upstream/emscripten/em++ -DCOOLPROP_NO_INCBIN @includes_CXX.rsp -O3 -DNDEBUG -sDISABLE_EXCEPTION_CATCHING=0 -std=gnu++17 -MD -MT ... -c emscripten_interface.cxx — i.e. -O3 instead of -O2 plus -DNDEBUG.
- Hypothesis: -O3 or LTO is eliding the register_optional<T> template call (it has a thread_local hasRun guard that the optimizer may be reasoning about across translation units, or it may collapse the call to nothing because the function appears to have no externally-observable effect that the optimizer can see).

Next steps:
1. Build CoolProp WASM with -O2 instead of -O3 (one-line CMakeLists.txt change) and confirm _embind_register_optional appears in wasm imports.
2. If yes: keep -O3 but find the surgical workaround (e.g. asm volatile fence, or explicit instantiation in a separate TU compiled at -O0, or explicit class_<std::optional<double>> bindings with .value()/.has_value()).
3. If no: dig further — maybe LTO at link-time is the culprit, or there's a libc++ namespace mismatch (runtime expects NSt3__28optionalIdEE = std::__2::optional<double> while header instantiates std::__1 or unversioned).

Verified working in this PR scope: ~60 other AS methods + 4 enums + register_vector<double>. Only the optional readback path is affected. Phase envelope tests pass with size-only checks; mixture set_mole_fractions test passes via PropsSI cross-check.
acceptance_criteria: VectorDouble.get(i) returns a usable value (number or wrapper with .value()) from JS in the full CoolProp WASM build; test_wasm.mjs can verify get_mole_fractions() round-trip and read individual phase envelope T/p values.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/pull/2974. Root-caused: register_optional<T> template's body (thread_local hasRun guard) gets folded to a no-op by the optimizer in the full build context. -O2 override on the JS target didn't help; calling internal::_embind_register_optional directly (same args register_optional<T> would have generated, per bind.h:1963-1964) preserves the wasm import. -O3 -DNDEBUG flags restored. Verified end-to-end: get_mole_fractions() round-trips composition, phase envelope element reads work.
close_reason: Shipped in #2974 (merged 2026-05-24). Direct internal::_embind_register_optional call bypassed the template-elision; get_mole_fractions() round-trip and envelope element reads verified working.

title: Extend test_wasm.mjs: derivative method coverage
description:
#2664 binds the first/second partial, saturation, and two-phase derivative methods (first_partial_deriv, first_saturation_deriv, first_two_phase_deriv, plus second variants) but none of them are exercised by test_wasm.mjs. Without a test, an unregistered argument type (e.g. parameters enum binding broken) would link cleanly and only fail at call time on the first real user.

Add one assertion per derivative family, e.g.:
- AS.update(PT_INPUTS, 1e6, 300); dHdT = AS.first_partial_deriv(iHmolar, iT, iP); plausibility-check (dHdT > 0)
- AS.update(PQ_INPUTS, 1e5, 0); dpdT_sat = AS.first_saturation_deriv(iP, iT); Clausius-Clapeyron check
- AS.update(PQ_INPUTS, 1e5, 0.3); dD_dh = AS.first_two_phase_deriv(iDmolar, iHmolar, iP); finite, non-zero

Keep tolerances loose; the point is to exercise each binding's signature end-to-end, not validate thermodynamics.
acceptance_criteria: test_wasm.mjs exercises at least one binding from each of: first_partial_deriv, second_partial_deriv, first_saturation_deriv, first_two_phase_deriv (and first_two_phase_deriv_splined once bound — see sibling bead).
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/pull/2971 (bundled). Closes when merged.

title: Extend test_wasm.mjs: build_phase_envelope round-trip
description:
The phase envelope binding is the highest-risk surface added by #2664: it relies on (a) value_object<CoolProp::PhaseEnvelopeData> at src/emscripten_interface.cxx:55 with X-macro-driven .field() registrations, (b) register_vector<double> for the vector fields, and (c) the build_phase_envelope() default-argument behavior (emscripten ignores C++ defaults, so JS must pass ''). None of this is exercised by #2664's tests, so a field-shape mismatch would only surface when a real caller tries to read the data.

Add a minimal round-trip: factory('HEOS','Methane&Ethane'), set 50/50 composition, build_phase_envelope(''), then call get_phase_envelope_data() and verify that at least a couple of the X-macro fields (e.g. .T, .p) are readable VectorDouble instances with size() > 0 and finite values. Bonus: verify a few sample values are in plausible physical ranges (T in [100, 350] K, p in [1e4, 1e7] Pa).
acceptance_criteria: test_wasm.mjs builds and reads a non-trivial phase envelope for Methane&Ethane via the binding; sizes and a small sample of values are sane.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/pull/2971 (bundled). Closes when merged.
close_reason: Shipped in #2971 (size-only; element readback gated on CoolProp-71a.8).

title: Extend test_wasm.mjs: mixture set_mole_fractions cross-check
description:
Add a mixture-composition test exercising AbstractState::set_mole_fractions(VectorDouble) — the EMSCRIPTEN-only set_mole_fractions_double wrapper — and verify the resulting state matches PropsSI with an explicit-composition fluid string. This is the path most callers actually use and was not covered by #2664's tests.

Concrete test (verified locally against the CI-pinned emsdk 4.0.12 build, bit-exact match):
- factory('HEOS', 'Methane&Ethane'); verify using_mole_fractions() === true
- z = new coolprop.VectorDouble(); z.push_back(0.4); z.push_back(0.6)
- set_mole_fractions(z); update(PT_INPUTS, 1e6, 250)
- AS.rhomass() === PropsSI('D','P',1e6,'T',250,'HEOS::Methane[0.4]&Ethane[0.6]') within 1e-8 relative
- AS.delete(); z.delete()

Already drafted in the i2664 worktree as an addition to wrappers/Javascript/test_wasm.mjs; extractable as-is.
acceptance_criteria: test_wasm.mjs contains the mixture cross-check, passes against the pinned emsdk build, and exits 0 in CI.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/pull/2970 (bundled with .1). Closes when merged.

title: Fill remaining binding-surface gaps in src/emscripten_interface.cxx
description:
Three small mechanical gaps deferred from #2664 review:

1. Forward-compat symmetry for fraction setters. Today the bindings use:
     .function('set_mole_fractions', &AbstractState::set_mole_fractions_double)  // EMSCRIPTEN-only wrapper
     .function('set_mass_fractions', &AbstractState::set_mass_fractions)        // raw virtual
     .function('set_volu_fractions', &AbstractState::set_volu_fractions)        // raw virtual
   This compiles only because COOLPROPDBL_MAPS_TO_DOUBLE is defined (CoolProp default), collapsing the two AbstractState overloads of set_mass_fractions/set_volu_fractions into one. If anyone ever builds with long double CoolPropDbl, the latter two stop being unambiguous and WASM build breaks. Add set_mass_fractions_double and set_volu_fractions_double wrappers (sibling pattern to set_mole_fractions_double at include/AbstractState.h:844-848) under #ifdef EMSCRIPTEN, then use them here.

2. Register optional<double> so VectorDouble.get(i) is readable from JS. With current bindings, calling z.get(0) on a VectorDouble (e.g. returned by get_mole_fractions()) throws 'Cannot call VectorDouble.get due to unbound types: NSt3__28optionalIdEE'. Add 'register_optional<double>();' in EMSCRIPTEN_BINDINGS(abstract_state_bindings) alongside the existing register_vector<double> call. (Needed by test follow-up beads that want to round-trip mole fractions.)

3. Bind AbstractState::first_two_phase_deriv_splined (currently unbound, exists at AbstractState.h:1473). Splined variant is the common one for callers wanting smooth two-phase derivatives without quality-jump artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: src/emscripten_interface.cxx binds set_mass_fractions_double, set_volu_fractions_double, register_optional<double>, and first_two_phase_deriv_splined; test_wasm.mjs can call z.get(0) on a get_mole_fractions() result.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/pull/2971 (bundled with .3, .6, .7). Setter symmetry + first_two_phase_deriv_splined landed; register_optional<double> spun off as CoolProp-71a.8. Closes when merged (and .8 takes the optional readback piece).
close_reason: Shipped in #2971: setter _double wrappers + first_two_phase_deriv_splined. register_optional<double> split to CoolProp-71a.8.

title: Complete enum coverage in src/emscripten_interface.cxx
description:
PR #2664 advertised 'comprehensive' enum bindings but several enum values are missing.

- backend_families: missing INVALID_BACKEND_FAMILY (=0) and SVDSBTL_BACKEND_FAMILY. Without SVDSBTL_BACKEND_FAMILY, JS code calling factory('SVDSBTL', ...) works but cannot introspect the family.
- input_pairs: missing all 8 Qmass-basis variants (QmassT_INPUTS, PQmass_INPUTS, QmassSmolar_INPUTS, QmassSmass_INPUTS, HmolarQmass_INPUTS, HmassQmass_INPUTS, DmolarQmass_INPUTS, DmassQmass_INPUTS). Either intentional (mass-basis quality less common) or accidental.
- parameters: needs an audit against the 86-entry enum in include/DataStructures.h:64 — PR added 84 explicit values but exhaustive comparison was deferred during review.

Add the missing values and add a brief comment near each enum binding in emscripten_interface.cxx pointing to the corresponding enum declaration in DataStructures.h so future drift is easier to detect.
acceptance_criteria: Each of the 4 enum bindings in src/emscripten_interface.cxx contains every value declared in include/DataStructures.h (except potentially explicit INVALID_*/_undefined_* sentinels — document if intentionally omitted).
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/pull/2971 (bundled with .4, .6, .7). Closes when merged.

title: BICUBIC: bounded extrapolation for cells with invalid corners
description: Per maintainer feedback on #2892: when a cell does not have 4 valid corners (e.g. cells crossing the saturation dome), extrapolation IS needed but the extrapolation range should be bounded by where the invalid edge sits. Current invert_single_phase_x/y simply rejects invalid cells via cell.valid() check and walks past them — better behaviour would be to extrapolate the last valid cell's cubic up to the invalid edge. Out of scope for the #1301 fix; this enhancement would tighten correctness for queries that sit between valid and invalid cells.

title: Refactor: SourceBackend enum + traits table to replace string compares
description:
The SVDSBTL backend currently encodes source-backend identity as a string (source_backend_) and asks behavioral questions of it via string compares.  Today there are 4 sites:

  src/Backends/SVDSBTL/SVDSBTLBackend.cpp:222  source_backend_ != "HEOS" && != "REFPROP" && != "IF97"   (validation)
  src/Backends/SVDSBTL/SVDSBTLBackend.cpp:228  source_backend_ == "IF97" && fluid_name != "Water"        (IF97 is Water-only)
  src/Backends/SVDSBTL/SVDSBTLBackend.cpp:917  source_backend_ == "IF97"                                 (needs_polish — PR #2966)
  src/SBTL/SVDSurfaceFactory.cpp:139           spec.source_backend != "REFPROP"                          (parallel-sampling-safe)

Pattern is starting to spread (PR #2966 added the 4th).  Each site is really asking a behavioral question that gets answered by a string compare; adding a 4th source backend (SRK, PR, cubic) means grepping for every such site.

Right shape: a tiny enum + constexpr traits table.

    enum class SourceBackend { HEOS, REFPROP, IF97 };

    struct SourceBackendTraits {
        bool hmassP_forward_consistent;     // false → needs polish_patch_state_ in critical patch
        bool parallel_sampling_safe;        // false for REFPROP (global state)
        std::optional<std::string_view> only_fluid;  // "Water" for IF97; nullopt for HEOS/REFPROP
    };
    constexpr SourceBackendTraits traits_for(SourceBackend);

Then each call site becomes:

    if (!traits.hmassP_forward_consistent) { polish_patch_state_(...); }
    if (traits.parallel_sampling_safe && n_threads > 1) { ... }
    if (traits.only_fluid && fluid_name != *traits.only_fluid) { throw ValueError(...); }

Parse-once-at-construction in the SVDSBTLBackend ctor:

    SourceBackend parse_source_backend(std::string_view s);  // throws on unknown

Then source_backend_ becomes a SourceBackend enum, and the string is kept only for cache-key encoding / error messages.

Acceptance:
  - All four sites listed above use traits, not string compares.
  - SVDSurfaceFactory.cpp gets the traits via a shared header (SourceBackend.h?) — same enum as SVDSBTLBackend.
  - Adding a new backend (SRK, PR) requires one new enum value + one row in the traits table; no grepping for string sites.
  - Existing test suite passes unchanged.

Not urgent — 4 sites is below the "refactor now" threshold but the trajectory is clear from PR #2966 (split out from PR #2964).

title: Docs SVDSBTL validation page: use its own SVDTables directory once config exists
description:
Once CoolProp-4no.3 (ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY config) lands, the SVDSBTL validation notebook (Web/coolprop/SVDSBTLValidation.ipynb) should build into a dedicated, version-controlled directory (e.g. Web/coolprop/_static/svdtables/ or a build-tree path) rather than ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables/.

Rationale:
  - Avoids collision with developer's interactive SVDSBTL work (currently the user's per-fluid surfaces can be overwritten/refreshed by unrelated processes during a docs build).
  - Makes the docs build reproducible: anyone running 'make html' gets the same surface hashes, regardless of what's in their ~/.CoolProp/ cache.
  - Enables shipping pre-built surfaces as part of the docs source if surface-build cost becomes a CI blocker.

Implementation:
  - First code cell of the notebook: CP.set_config_string(CoolProp.ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY, '<dedicated path>').
  - Choose path policy: per-build temp dir, or persistent under Web/coolprop/_static/svdtables/.
  - Update Web/conf.py pre-execute step if config needs to be set process-wide before the notebook runs.

Blocked-by: CoolProp-4no.3.

title: fix(if97): correct n5 = 0.13918839778870e2 in code (R2/R3 boundary classifier)
description:
The IF97 R2/R3 boundary constant n5 has a transcription error in two
code locations (caught by the IF97 doc-audit code-reviewer subagent
2026-05-23):

  Wrong:  0.1391883776670e2  = 13.91883776670
  Right:  0.13918839778870e2 = 13.918839778870

Locations:
  - Web/scripts/fluid_properties.IF97Conformance.py:135
  - src/SBTL/SurfacePresets.cpp:87, 94 (docstring + if97_T_B23_K constant)

Canonical value: build/_deps/if97-src/IF97.h:651 (IAPWS R7-97 release).

Magnitude:
  - Relative error ~5e-8 (~1.45e-7 in derived boundary p_B23).
  - Translates to ~2 µPa shift in the computed R2/R3 boundary.
  - Below any meaningful conformance threshold; does NOT affect
    region classification at the precision SVDSBTL or IF97 work at.

Worth fixing for hygiene + consistency with IAPWS R7-97 cited
constants.  Independent of the IF97 doc-fixes PR (which corrects the
prose + matplotlib script copies).

Acceptance:
  1. Replace n5 literal in both files with 0.13918839778870e2.
  2. Re-run the SVDSBTL critical-patch calibration (5 µs delta on
     p_B23(T) is below the bisection's 6-step resolution; should not
     shift cached multipliers).
  3. Spot-check that no SVDSBTL .svd.bin.z cache requires regen.

title: Cross-link SVDSBTL page from related docs pages
description:
Web/coolprop/SVDSBTL.rst is published and linked from Web/coolprop/index.rst (toctree) and Web/fluid_properties/IF97.rst, but the natural arrival points for a reader looking for fast / tabular evaluation don't mention it.

Pages that should cross-link to /coolprop/SVDSBTL:
- Web/index.rst — top-level 'What is CoolProp?' feature bullet list. Add an entry alongside the existing ':ref:`Computationally efficient tabular interpolation <tabular_interpolation>`' / ':ref:`Fast IAPWS-IF97 ... <IF97>`' bullets so the SVDSBTL backend appears in the very first feature list a new reader sees.
- Web/coolprop/Tabular.rst — readers comparing TTSE / BICUBIC need to know SVDSBTL exists and where it sits relative to the older tabular backends.
- Web/coolprop/HighLevelAPI.rst — PropsSI backend selection examples should mention 'SVDSBTL&HEOS::<fluid>' / 'SVDSBTL&IF97::Water' alongside HEOS/REFPROP/BICUBIC.
- Web/coolprop/LowLevelAPI.rst — AbstractState::factory backend list should reference SVDSBTL_BACKEND.
- Web/coolprop/REFPROP.rst — at minimum a 'see also' pointing to SVDSBTL as the open-source fast-eval option.
- Web/coolprop/changelog.rst — entry for the SVDSBTL backend landing (PRs #2917, #2938-#2940, #2952-#2957).

Acceptance:
- Each page above has at least one :doc:`/coolprop/SVDSBTL` reference (or :ref: to a labelled section on that page) in the appropriate context (not just a passing mention).
- Web/index.rst's top-level feature list includes an SVDSBTL bullet — this is the headline change; the rest are supporting cross-links.
- Wording is consistent with the framing already used in IF97.rst (open-source, fast, fully traceable; SVDSBTL&IF97 for IAPWS-IF97 conformance, SVDSBTL&HEOS for general fluids).
- Sphinx build is clean (no broken refs).
close_reason: Satisfied by PRs #2959 and #2960 (the broader SVDSBTL docs audit). All 6 target pages now have substantive cross-links: Web/index.rst (top-level feature bullet), Web/coolprop/Tabular.rst (recommended-tabular-backend callout), Web/coolprop/HighLevelAPI.rst (speed advice), Web/coolprop/LowLevelAPI.rst (table-load construction-cost warning), Web/coolprop/REFPROP.rst (throughput-bound REFPROP wrapper note), Web/coolprop/changelog.rst (release entry with PR list). All use :doc:/:ref: forms, not bare mentions.

title: feat(svdsbtl): implement ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY config key
description:
The SVDSBTL docs at Web/coolprop/SVDSBTL.rst:138 and :379 claim a config key
ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY that lets users redirect the SVDSBTL on-disk
cache away from $HOME/.CoolProp/SVDTables.  Verification by subagent
(2026-05-23) confirmed: the key does not exist in include/Configuration.h,
and SVDSurfaceSerializer.cpp / SVDSBTLBackend.cpp contain no get_config_string
calls for any directory override.  Cache path is unconditionally
\$HOME/.CoolProp/SVDTables (src/SBTL/SVDSurfaceSerializer.cpp:491-503).

The pre-existing ALTERNATIVE_TABLES_DIRECTORY (include/Configuration.h:30)
is consumed only by BICUBIC/TTSE (src/Backends/Tabular/TabularBackends.cpp:360,
.h:1036) and points at ~/.CoolProp/Tables, not the SVDSBTL ~/.CoolProp/SVDTables
directory.

Motivation: shared workstations, read-only \$HOME (CI containers), centrally-
managed cache directories.  The doc text we already shipped (and are
temporarily removing pending this issue) describes the intended UX.

Acceptance:
  1. Register ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY in include/Configuration.h
     with default empty string, mirroring ALTERNATIVE_TABLES_DIRECTORY.
  2. In src/SBTL/SVDSurfaceSerializer.cpp default_cache_dir(), consult
     get_config_string(ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY) before falling
     back to \$HOME/.CoolProp/SVDTables.  Apply to both .svd.bin.z and

  3. Add a Catch2 test that sets the config, constructs an

XDG_DATA_HOME-on-Linux support (separate doc bug also being removed
in the doc-fixes PR) is a strict subset of this issue if scope permits.
notes: Shipped in https://github.com/CoolProp/CoolProp/pull/2961 (branch ihb/svdsbtl-alternative-svdtables-dir). Tests green: [SVDSBTL][cache][fhp] new resolver test (11 assertions, 2 cases), [SBTL] umbrella (4229 assertions, 12 cases), [SVDSBTL] umbrella exit 0. Pre-PR adversarial review returned ship-as-is. Preflight skipped at user's request.

title: SVDSBTL fast_evaluate worker-pool API for parallel batches
description: Internal threading in fast_evaluate hits the per-thread AbstractState::factory() cost (~1 ms/thread) on every call, which dominates the 10k-probe budget (~4 ms total).  A worker-pool API where the caller builds N worker AbstractStates once and reuses them across many fast_evaluate calls amortizes that cost.  Punted from PR #2944 — see comment in that PR for the design sketch (Option C).  Threshold for being worth doing: a real workload submitting >=100k probes per fast_evaluate call.  Until then, callers can already parallelize externally with one AbstractState per worker thread (Option A in the same comment).

title: fast_evaluate dome rows: consider fast_evaluate_two_phase_partial status for mixed-defined output sets
description:
Follow-up from PR #2942 CodeRabbit review.

**Current behaviour:** When a Q-blend dome row contains a property
that has no equilibrium bulk value inside the saturation dome
(speed_sound, cp, cv, viscosity, conductivity, Prandtl), that specific
output is NaN-filled in place and the row status stays
fast_evaluate_ok.  The contract says NaN values communicate
\"undefined here\" via in-band signal; status_flags only flips when
the entire row is invalid.

**CodeRabbit's concern:** A caller scanning only status_flags can't
distinguish \"all outputs valid\" from \"some outputs valid, some
in-dome-undefined\" without iterating the row checking for NaN.

**Why I didn't change it in #2942:** The straightforward fix
(NaN-fill the whole row and flag two_phase_disallowed when ANY
output is undefined in the dome) would lose meaningful Q-blended T
/ rho / s / u / Q values whenever the same call also asks for
speed_sound or one of the other dome-undefined props.  That makes
the caller's job harder, not easier — they have to make a separate
call without the undefined output to recover the valid ones.

**Possible alternatives to evaluate:**

1. New status code \`fast_evaluate_two_phase_partial\` —
   non-zero (so a quick \`status_flags[k] != 0\` check still catches
   it) but distinct from \`two_phase_disallowed\` (which means
   \"nothing here\").  Documented as \"row has both valid and NaN
   outputs; check individual entries\".  Probably the cleanest
   answer.  Contract change — needs versioned doc update.

2. Status flag remains \`ok\` but caller-side helper provided
   that scans a row for NaN entries.  Doesn't change the contract
   but makes detection less error-prone.

3. Leave it.  Document explicitly in the AbstractState::fast_evaluate
   header that a NaN entry inside a fast_evaluate_ok row is the
   designated signal for \"defined-elsewhere property requested at a
   point where it has no value\".

**Scope when picked up:**

- Decide which of the three.  Likely (1).
- If (1): bump fast_evaluate_status enum, add documentation in
  AbstractState.h, plumb the new code through IF97 dome path too
  (it has the same NaN-fill-undefined-in-dome pattern in PR #2942),
  add a test case that requests speed_sound + Dmass in a dome row
  and asserts the partial status.

Closes the deferred half of the PR #2942 review.

title: SVDSBTL: add HS and DU presets for full IAPWS G13-15 input-pair coverage
description:
Current SVDSBTL coverage (post foi.9 stack, ~2026-05-21) hits two of IAPWS G13-15's four input pairs:

| pair | preset | status |
|---|---|---|
| (p, h) | presets::ph_subcritical | conformant within IF97 R7-97 backward floor (~25 mK T, ~0.003% v) for R1/R2/R5; R3 closed via TOMS748 sampling + SUPER_R3/SUPER_R2/SUPER_R1_super atlas splits |
| (p, T) | presets::pt_subcritical | conformant |
| (h, s) | -- not implemented -- | needs new preset |
| (D, u) | -- not implemented -- | needs new preset |

G13-15 also defines tables for the (h, s) and (rho, u) input pairs (Tables 14-17). The SBTL framework supports these natively — only the preset (region geometry + axis transforms + sampling lambda + IF97 fallback) needs to be written.

**Concrete next-PR scope:**

1. presets::hs_subcritical(heos, NT, NR, rank) — preset for HmassSmass_INPUTS
   - Primary axis: log s (likely; verify monotonicity in IF97 R1 first)
   - Secondary axis: h
   - Region split: LIQUID / VAPOR / SUPER, plus IF97 R2/R3 / R1/R3 splits mirroring the ph atlas
   - Sampling: use HmassSmass_INPUTS where IF97 implements it; TOMS748 fallback elsewhere

2. presets::du_subcritical (or umolar / Dmass-Umass naming per CoolProp's enum) for DU_INPUTS
   - Primary axis: log rho (density spans orders of magnitude)
   - Secondary axis: u
   - Same region/split structure
   - IF97 has backward equations for some regions but not others — TOMS748 fallback as needed

3. CMake option to opt out of building HS / DU surfaces by default if the build-time cost gets heavy (already need to measure — currently ph+pt is ~30s for Water).

4. SVDSBTLBackend::kSupportedPairs append the new pairs; ensure_surface_ wires them in.

5. Tests: extend src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTL.cpp with HS / DU smoke cases against HEOS truth + a G13-15 conformance failmap entry (the existing failmap test only walks (p, h) probes; extend or duplicate the test for (h, s) and (rho, u) probe spaces).

**Known carry-overs from ph_subcritical (will hit hs/du too):**

- Sat-dome-edge boundary cells with natural-cubic-spline endpoint BC artifacts (worst cases at η ≈ 0 / η ≈ 1).
- R1 w descent residual at h > 1000 kJ/kg (the foi.9.11 LIQUID 2× rank=40 experiment showed simple density bumps don't help cleanly; same will apply to hs/du).
- IF97 R7-97 ±25 mK backward floor in R2/R5 still bounds (h, s) since the sampling refines against forward h(T,p).

Parent: CoolProp-foi.10 (likely) — file new epic once we start this.

title: Cleanup: move SVDSBTL bench/failmap scripts from C++ to Python to declutter CMake
description:
The SVDSBTL development cycle accumulated several dev/ C++ scripts and one Catch2 'test' that emit CSVs rather than assert. Each adds a CMake option + an add_executable block. They were C++ originally for ABI reasons (direct AbstractState access, hot-path timing) but the Python wrapper now exposes everything they need. Porting drops three CMake options and ~50 lines of CMakeLists.txt clutter.

**Inventory (2026-05-21):**

| file | CMake option | purpose | Python port? |
|---|---|---|---|
| dev/build_svd_tables.cpp | COOLPROP_BUILD_SVD_TABLES | Build .svd.bin.z caches for all configured fluids | yes — pure factory calls; Python via CoolProp.AbstractState |
| dev/bench_svdsbtl_ph.cpp | COOLPROP_BUILD_SVDSBTL_BENCH | (h,p) grid wall-time benchmark vs HEOS / IF97 / REFPROP | yes — timing via time.perf_counter_ns; bench_svdsbtl_ph_plot.py already plots its output |
| dev/profile_svdsbtl.cpp | COOLPROP_BUILD_SVDSBTL_PROFILE | Per-stage profiler (locate -> SVD eval -> output transform) | no — touches SVDSurface internals through friend access; keep C++ |
| src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTLFailMap.cpp | (linked into CatchTestRunner) | Emit per-region conformance CSV vs IF97 truth; doesn't assert | yes — move to dev/failmap_svdsbtl.py; PropsSI does the probing |

**Keep as C++ (real assertions or private-API access):**

- src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDComponents.cpp — unit-tests SVDDecomposition / SVDEvaluator internals + Catch2 BENCHMARK microbenchmarks
- src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTL.cpp — backend integration assertions
- src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTLCriticalPatch.cpp — patch-routing assertions
- src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTLOptions.cpp — options-schema parse assertions

**Concrete deliverables when claimed:**

1. dev/build_svd_tables.py — drop-in for the .cpp; iterates fluid list, calls AbstractState.factory('SVDSBTL&HEOS', fluid).
2. dev/failmap_svdsbtl.py — replicates the per-region probe loop; emits the same CSV columns (region,h_J_kg,p_Pa,T_truth_K,dT_mK,dv_pct,ds_J_kgK,dw_pct) so existing plot scripts work unchanged.
3. dev/bench_svdsbtl_ph.py — wall-time benchmark; pair with bench_svdsbtl_ph_plot.py.
4. Remove src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests-SVDSBTLFailMap.cpp.
5. Drop CMake options COOLPROP_BUILD_SVD_TABLES and COOLPROP_BUILD_SVDSBTL_BENCH and their add_executable blocks at CMakeLists.txt:86-94 + :2177-2201.
6. Update docs/README that reference -DCOOLPROP_BUILD_* flags for these two.

**Caveats:**

- Wall-time numbers will shift in Python (wrapper adds ~1-2 us/call). That's fine — we only ever compared SVDSBTL vs HEOS *ratios*, and both pay the wrapper overhead. Document the floor in the script header.
- Confirm AbstractState.factory('SVDSBTL&IF97', 'Water') is reachable through the wrapper before porting failmap. If not, fall back to keeping failmap as C++.

Independent cleanup; can land any time after the foi.9.x stack merges.

title: Pre-smooth M at IF97 boundary cells (Kunick §5.1 smoothing)
description:
**Subagent-investigation outcome (2026-05-20):** Kunick G13-15 §5.1 explicitly states 'the spline functions are smoothed at the IF97 region boundaries 1-3 and 2-3' but defers algorithm details to unpublished ref [11].  The likely mechanism: smooth the sampled property values at cells that straddle IF97 internal boundaries before the spline fit, so the kink isn't encoded into the basis.

We can apply the same idea: insert a smoothing pass between sample_grid and build_svd in SVDSurfaceFactory.cpp, targeting cells whose physical (a, b) coordinates straddle T=623.15 K (R1/R3) or p=p_B23(T) (R2/R3).  Smoothing kernel: 5-tap Savitzky-Golay quadratic filter applied along the axis transverse to the boundary, restricted to a 3-cell band.  Preserves quadratic trends exactly (so true large curvature in SUPER is untouched), only attenuates the third-difference kink artifact.

Implementation sketch:
1. Add smooth_if97_boundaries(M, region, xn_grid, xi_grid) free function in SVDSurfaceFactory.cpp's anon namespace.
2. Computes per-cell physical (T, p) coordinates via region::Region::from_normalized.
3. Marks cells in [623.15-Δ, 623.15+Δ] band as R1/R3-straddling.
4. Marks cells in [p_B23(T)-Δ, p_B23(T)+Δ] band as R2/R3-straddling.
5. Applies SG filter on the corresponding columns/rows of M.
6. Gated by BuildOptions::if97_boundary_smoothing.

Expected impact: R3 worst-case 10-30× drop (still may be above perm even with smoothing — that's why foi.9.5 atlas split is the primary R3 fix; smoothing is bonus polish).  R1/R2: minor.

Order: optional cleanup *after* foi.9.5 lands.  Measures the marginal contribution of explicit smoothing on top of the structural split.  Could turn out to be unnecessary.

Parent: CoolProp-foi.9.  Companion to foi.9.5.
notes:
**Investigation 2026-05-20 — boundary smoothing along η in SUPER_R3 doesn't move R1 conformance.**

Implemented 5-tap Savitzky-Golay quadratic-preserve filter along η for SUPER_R3 (where the IF97 R1/R3 kink at T=623.15 K sits interior post-foi.9.5).  Measured on Water/IF97:

R1 v_max:  0.1640% (PR1)  →  0.1649% (PR1+δ+ε)  — essentially unchanged

Root cause discovered: R1 conformance failures DON'T live near the IF97 R1/R3 boundary at all.  Of 245 R1 v-fails post-PR1, only 3 are within 5 K of T=623.15 K and only 13 are within 20 K.  214 of 245 are MORE than 50 K from the boundary — clustered at T ∈ [275, 595] K, p ∈ [22, 100] MPa: the compressed-liquid corner of SUPER_R3 at η ≈ 0.007 (essentially the η=0 region boundary).

The worst R1 cell is T=283 K, p=28 MPa, h=68 kJ/kg, sitting at η ≈ 0.007 in SUPER_R3.  Chebyshev spacing crowds cells very densely there (η-cell-width ≈ 1.5×10⁻⁵).  Cell density is not the bottleneck; the Hermite cubic's natural-cubic-spline BC at η=0 forces zero 2nd derivative — a poor approximation when the underlying ρ(p, h) has strong curvature against the melting-line / EOS-validity-envelope boundary.

The Kunick §5.1 'smoothing at IF97 region boundaries 1-3 and 2-3' is targeting the wrong artifact for our SVD+Hermite-cubic architecture.  Closing as wrong-tool-for-the-job.

Real R1 fix candidates (future work):
  - Adjust natural-cubic-spline BC at η=0 (use clamped slope = slope of neighbor cell, not zero d²)
  - Extrapolate the sampling slightly below T_min so boundary cells have neighbor data
  - Split SUPER_R3 at T=623.15 K isotherm to give R1 territory its own η normalization (might help if R1-only η stretches wider)

title: Phase 4: experiment with L/G/HT region partition (Kunick layout)
description:
Switch SBTL preset's regions from current LIQUID + VAPOR + SUPER to Kunick's L + G + HT:
  - L: compressed liquid below sat dome + supercritical h < h_c above pcrit (h_c = 2087.546845 kJ/kg for IF97, 2084.256263 kJ/kg for IAPWS-95)
  - G: superheated vapor above sat dome + supercritical h > h_c above pcrit
  - HT: T > 1073.15 K (only relevant for IF97 source backend — IAPWS-95 SBTL skips this)

Eliminates the ~19 mK residual SVDSBTL&HEOS sees in the SUPER region where it straddles the IAPWS-95 L/G boundary at h_c.

Per Ian (2026-05-20): no strong feelings on this one — experiment and see whether it helps after Phases 1+2+3 land.  May turn out to be unnecessary if the variable transformations alone close the conformance gap.

Parent: CoolProp-foi.9.

title: Phase 3: close residual gap — denser SVD grids or bi-quadratic interpolating spline
description:
After Phases 1+2, measure remaining gap to Kunick perms.  Two paths:

PATH 3a — denser SVD grids matching Kunick's Tables A4-A6 node counts.  Kunick uses ~275 p × 125 h = 34k nodes for T^L, up to ~350 × 250 = 88k for s^L.  Our SVDSBTL is at NT=200, NR=800 = 160k nodes but rank-truncated to ~28 → 28k effective parameters.  Increasing NT to match Kunick's per-property p-direction counts (with piecewise-equidistant breakpoints per his recipes) keeps our SVD compression story but should close the residual.

PATH 3b — replace SVD + Hermite cubic with bi-quadratic interpolating spline per Kunick §3.2.1.  Per-cell polynomial Σ_{k,l ∈ 1..3} a_{ijkl} (x̄_1 - x̄_1,i)^(k-1) (x̄_2 - x̄_2,j)^(l-1), C¹ continuous across cell boundaries via global linear system of conditions (eqs 2.13-2.33).  Knots at midpoints of nodes (Kunick's 'symmetric boundary conditions, superior accuracy', citing ref [13]).

Path 3a is the smaller change.  Try first.  Cache will grow but our SVD compression makes that less painful than Kunick's full-rank storage.

Parent: CoolProp-foi.9.  Depends on foi.9.1 + foi.9.2 first.

title: docs: Sphinx page for backend options schema
description: Render JSON Schema docs for SVDSBTL options. Include the high-level-interface examples (Python/FORTRAN/Excel/MATLAB) verbatim from the spec. Migration table for future BICUBIC/TTSE/REFPROP. Parent: CoolProp-vh0.

title: SVDSBTL: use symbolic name for input_pair in cache filename
description: Cache filename currently embeds input_pair as the int enum value (e.g. '17' for PT_INPUTS). Fragile: if anyone reorders input_pairs in DataStructures.h, every existing cache file silently misaligns to the wrong pair. Replace with the symbolic name from get_input_pair_short_desc() or a dedicated table. Bump kRevision when this lands so any caches built under the int-key scheme get rebuilt.

title: SVDSBTL: per-fluid rank tuning for CO2 / D6 VAPOR region
description:
The khoopes-style (h, p) probe shows CO2's VAPOR region (right of dome, p<p_crit) at p99 ~ 360 ppm rho rel-err, vs Water R2 vapor's ~167 ppm at the same rank=20, NT=200, NR=800.

CO2 vapor density has steeper temperature dependence near critical than water; rank-20 truncates more visibly there. Options to investigate (in priority order):

1. Per-fluid rank-bump: rebuild CO2 (and probably D6) tables at rank 30 or 40 to see if the residual cyan halo around the VAPOR sat boundary collapses to dark blue.
2. Per-property rank: only ρ gets the higher rank; T/h/s/u/w stay at 20.
3. Refine the η-axis grid (NR>800) near sat,V boundary specifically.

Decision criterion: target IAPWS-style 200 ppm p99 in the VAPOR region for all built fluids.

Reproducer: dev/probe_co2_khoopes.cpp + dev/plot_co2_khoopes.py on branch ihb/svd-clamped-bc.

Also relevant: dev/iapws_conformance_probe.cpp for the full per-region accuracy breakdown.
close_reason: Superseded by CoolProp-5ni (auto-calibrate critical-patch bbox per fluid at table-build time). If the bbox auto-grows to wrap cells where rank-20 fails, those cells route to HEOS and the SVD's residual is irrelevant; per-fluid rank bumping becomes unnecessary.

title: SVDSBTL: document critical-patch HEOS-fallback bbox design in Web/
description:
The critical-patch HEOS-fallback design (Phase 3 of SVDSBTL) needs documentation in the existing Sphinx tree, alongside Web/coolprop/SBTL.rst.

Content to cover:
1. Why the patch is needed: rank-20 SVD can't represent the critical-vicinity singularity. Empirical failure footprint at p/p_crit ∈ [0.75, 1.15], T/T_crit ∈ [0.95, 1.05] for Water (IAPWS conformance probe data — R1 p99 ok except w; R2 p99 ok; R3 p99 catastrophic without patch).
2. Canonical patch shape: rectangle in (T, p) centered at the critical point, [0.95, 1.05]·T_crit × [0.75, 1.15]·p_crit.
3. Bbox in each supported input pair: precompute h_min/h_max by walking the (T, p) patch perimeter with HEOS so the dispatch test is one axis-aligned compare in native input coordinates (no T-from-h inversion).
4. PH bbox numbers for Water (h ∈ [1.567, 2.960] MJ/kg, p ∈ [16.55, 25.37] MPa) and the figure dev/plot_critbbox.py generates.
5. Compare to IAPWS R3: similar lower bounds (0.95 vs 0.963 T_c; 0.75 vs 0.749 p_c) but our upper bounds are much tighter because IAPWS R3 is sized by EOS-form convenience (T-ρ vs T-p), not by where the formulation fails.

Reusable assets: dev/plot_critbbox.py and the empirical-footprint table from dev/iapws_conformance_probe.cpp output.
notes: Bundled with CoolProp-5ni + CoolProp-dxd (the auto-calibration code).  Land together: the docs describe the per-fluid bbox numbers that the auto-calibration loop produces.  Should slot into the new Web/coolprop/SVDSBTL.rst page being created in CoolProp-s60.

title: SVDSBTL: investigate ~1% ρ peak rel-err at extreme region corners
description: probe_midcell run on Water shows max rho rel-err = 9e-3 at both at-node and mid-cell sites (1.01x ratio), suggesting the peak is at extreme cells (region boundary edges where Hermite has thin support) rather than from interpolation. Cross-check against bench_svdsbtl_ph heatmap — if the same cells light up, document as the accuracy ceiling of NT=200, NR=800, rank=20 preset. Candidate fixes for Phase 3: adaptive rank per region, boundary refinement, or wider preset bounds.

title: SVDSBTL bench: entropy rel-err near-zero divisor at reference state
description: probe_midcell run on Water shows max rel-err of 202 for entropy s, while mean rel-err is 6e-4. Root cause: the entropy reference state lands inside the grid, so s_truth ≈ 0 at some probes makes rel-err = |Δs| / |s| blow up. Switch entropy error metric in dev/bench_svdsbtl_ph.cpp + dev/probe_midcell.cpp to |Δs| / max(|s|, s_critical), or report absolute Δs alongside relative. Verified the underlying accuracy is fine — mid-cell mean and at-node mean track each other; only the divisor is the issue.

title: DU surface for SVDSBTL backend (ρ, u inputs)
description:
SVDSBTL DU surface — D_mass × U_mass tabulation for CFD use cases.

DEFERRED FROM PHASE 2C (#2919): the geometry has several non-trivial corner cases that need design + testing time beyond what fits in the current PR.

DESIGN SKETCH (Brainstormed 2026-05-15, see chat with Ian):

Coordinate axes:
* Primary: log(ρ)  (5–6 OOM range in pure fluids)
* Secondary: u
* Output properties: p, T, h, s, w  (parallels PH/PT structure)

Region structure (3 regions, parallel to PH/PT):
* LIQUID: ρ > ρ_sat,L(T_sat), u_min(ρ) ≤ u ≤ u_sat,L(ρ)
* VAPOR:  ρ < ρ_sat,V(T_sat), u_sat,V(ρ) ≤ u ≤ u_max(ρ)
* SUPER:  T > T_crit, full ρ span between T_min / T_max isotherms

Boundary curves (all in log ρ, plug into SatBoundaryFactory):
* u_sat,L(ρ) — sample T_sat = sa.solve_for_T(ρ, "D", 0), then u = sa.eval_sat(T_sat, "U", 0)
* u_sat,V(ρ) — same with Q=1
* u_min(ρ)   — T_min isotherm, walking T over melting line (reuse existing walker)
* u_max(ρ)   — T_max isotherm

Two-phase (ρ, u) inversion:
* Given (ρ, u) in the dome, solve f(T) = u_L(T) + ((1/ρ - v_L(T))/(v_V(T) - v_L(T))) * (u_V(T) - u_L(T)) - u = 0
* SuperAncillary has solve_for_Tq_DX_many(ρ, propval, key) that may do exactly this — needs verification
* Fallback: brent on f(T) over [T_triple, T_crit]

CORNER CASES (the reason this needs more design time):

1. Density-maximum on sat-liquid: ρ_L(T) is non-monotone for water (peaks at ~4°C, ρ ≈ 999.97).
   - Inverting ρ_L → T is multi-valued in a 4-K-wide strip near the max
   - u_sat,L(ρ) becomes multi-valued there, breaking xnorm normalization
   - Two strategies:
     a) Exclude the cold sliver: T_LIQUID_min = T_max_density + ε.  Lose ~4 K of coverage near triple point; falls back to direct HEOS in that band.
     b) Split LIQUID into COLD_LIQUID (T<T_max_density) and WARM_LIQUID (T>T_max_density) sub-regions.  Atlas grows to 4 (or 5 with SUPER); each sub-region has single-valued u_sat,L(ρ).
   - Affects water and D2O.  Other Phase 2a fluids OK.

2. Near-critical ρ-pinch: ρ_L → ρ_V → ρ_crit as T → T_crit.  Same trick as PH (thin p-band excluded) needs ρ-band analogue.

3. SuperAncillary U expansion: lazily built only via ensure_caloric_superancillaries() which currently does H and S, not U.  Either extend the lazy builder to include U, or compute u from h - p/ρ at sat-curve eval sites (same pattern used in update_two_phase_).

4. Property order convention: DmassUmass_INPUTS has value1=ρ, value2=u per the enum.  Confirm before wiring.

IMPLEMENTATION CHECKLIST (~half a day once the design is settled):
* du_subcritical preset (~80 lines, parallels ph_subcritical)
* 4 new SatBoundaryFactory builders (~50 lines)
* SVDSBTLBackend: DmassUmass_INPUTS in kSupportedPairs, switch case in update(), two-phase dome detection
* Multi-fluid Catch2 test (parameterize over Water, CO2, R134a, Ammonia, Methane, Propane, D6)
* Extend the bench harness to compare DU vs HEOS the same way the PH bench does

NOT in scope for this issue:
* HS / PS / DT input pairs (parallel structure, each one is its own design exercise)
* Cold-water sub-region (option 1b above; ship as a separate follow-up if anyone needs T<278K liquid coverage)

title: HEOS: add rho_melt(p) / T_melt(p) cubic proxy on the melting curve
description: A simple monotone cubic spline approximation of the melting line as rho_melt(p) (and T_melt(p)) would be broadly useful in CoolProp:  (a) SVDSBTL's LIQUID-floor sat-curve factory currently walks T up in 0.5 K increments until HEOS accepts the (T, p) state — a direct T_melt(p) query would replace that lambda with a single call; (b) general-purpose melting-line queries for users that need them; (c) constraint checks (reject (T, p) inputs below the melting line up front).  Per-fluid: only build when the fluid has melting-curve coefficients in the EOS metadata.  Returns NaN otherwise.  Independent of the SVDSBTL stack — could land as its own bugfix-style PR.

title: SBTL conformance: Sphinx accuracy page per IAPWS Tables 8-13
description: Build per-region deviation table page matching IAPWS Tables 8-13 layout, computed at doc-build time.  Already drafted in commit 2438ca564 (reverted) — drop back in after items 1-5 land.

Current SVDSBTL+IF97 accuracy (post foi.9.1-4, 6-8, 11 and pd6) meets the practical needs of the project; remaining items (atlas splits at R2/R3 + R1/R3 boundaries, TOMS748 h-inversion in IF97 sampling, Hermite bicubic backbone, multi-piece grids, L/G h_c split, separate HT region, Sphinx accuracy page, per-property fail-map) are conformance polish that can be reopened or refiled if/when stricter G13-15 conformance becomes a goal.

description: Kunick separates G (T<1073.15 K) from HT (1073.15<T<=2273.15 K) with different p bounds (HT capped at 50 MPa) and different grid densities.

title: SBTL conformance: property-specific transformations
description: Kunick uses s̄=√s in some regions, v̄=log v in gas region, etc.  Each spline function has its own transformation tuned to reduce third-derivative curvature.  Mirror per-property transformations.
notes: Transformation safety constraint flagged by Ian (2026-05-12): sqrt-of-property transformations are unsafe when the property can be negative (entropy and enthalpy depend on reference state — IIR/NBP/ASHRAE etc. can give s<0 in some regions).  Use only sign-safe transformations: log(v)=log(1/rho) for density (rho always > 0), p (always > 0), T (always > 0).  For s, h, u: either no transformation, or a shift-then-transform with a fluid-specific offset that's documented as state-aware.  Kunick uses sqrt(s) for Water because IAPWS-95 reference (s=0 at triple-point liquid) keeps s > 0 throughout the table envelope — that property doesn't generalize.

Current SVDSBTL+IF97 accuracy (post foi.9.1-4, 6-8, 11 and pd6) meets the practical needs of the project; remaining items (atlas splits at R2/R3 + R1/R3 boundaries, TOMS748 h-inversion in IF97 sampling, Hermite bicubic backbone, multi-piece grids, L/G h_c split, separate HT region, Sphinx accuracy page, per-property fail-map) are conformance polish that can be reopened or refiled if/when stricter G13-15 conformance becomes a goal.

title: SBTL: normph xnorm round-trip fails at degenerate-corner super region (j=0)
description: Test "NormalizedPHTable: xnorm <-> h round-trip across all three regions for CO2" (src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests.cpp:5625) fails 1 assertion in 211 at the super-region first row where h_lo_isobar[0] ≈ h_hi_isobar[0] (rectangle degenerates).  This is the same h-value being returned for both sides at near-critical pressure. Fix candidates: skip j=0 in the round-trip check, or clamp the super-region p-grid to start at p_crit*(1+ε).

title: SBTL: tighten sat_cache test tolerance — H-superanc precision (~3e-9) below 1e-10
description: Test "SBTL saturation cache exposes h_sat,L(p) and h_sat,V(p) for CO2" (src/Tests/CoolProp-Tests.cpp:5679) was tightened from 1e-3 to 1e-10 assuming the runtime-built H-superancillary delivers near-machine precision.  Actual deviation is ~3e-9 relative at p=pc*0.01 and similar low-pressure samples.  Either tighten the H-superanc Chebyshev tolerance or relax the test to 1e-8.  Independent of PR #2678 native w/η work — also fails on master.

title: Mode D follow-up: HAPropsSI fails at T_wb=0 °C across all RH (#2906)

title: Expose all saturation T-roots from update_with_guesses inputs (outer-iteration helper)
description:
Follow-up to #2773 / PR #2835 / CoolProp-cew.

Today, update_with_guesses(HmolarQ_INPUTS / QSmolar_INPUTS / DmolarQ_INPUTS, value, Q, guess) returns a single T disambiguated by guess.T. Internally we already have the full list of roots — ChebyshevApproximation1D::get_x_for_y() enumerates every T satisfying the saturation residual via TOMS748 inside each monotonic sub-interval. We just throw that away after picking one.

For outer iterative calculations (e.g. solving for a two-input flash that wraps around a saturation flash, sensitivity analysis, branch-tracing, or comparison of physical regimes), it's useful to surface both T-roots at once.

# Proposed API
On AbstractState (or HEOS-only for now):

    /// Return all temperatures T at which sat_phase(T) = value lies on the
    /// saturation curve. For inputs that admit only one root the vector has
    /// length 1; for multi-root cases (water vapor h_g, liquid rho_L near
    /// 4 °C, etc.) length 2 or more.
    /// \param input_pair One of HmolarQ_INPUTS, QSmolar_INPUTS, DmolarQ_INPUTS,
    ///                    or their mass equivalents
    /// \param value The target value of h_molar / s_molar / rho_molar
    /// \param Q Vapor quality, must be exactly 0 or 1
    std::vector<double> saturation_T_roots(input_pairs input_pair, double value, double Q);

# Implementation
Call get_x_for_y on the relevant superancillary (rho_sat / h_sat / s_sat),
collect (T, num_steps) pairs, return just the T values sorted ascending.
Lazy build of caloric superancillaries via ensure_caloric_superancillaries
on first call (already implemented).

# Out of scope for this bead
- Mass-fraction inputs (HmassQ_INPUTS etc.) — straightforward unit conversion
  using molar_mass; can be added in same patch
- Mixtures — pure (and not pseudo-pure) only, matching current update_with_guesses
- High-level (PropsSI) exposure — separate decision; the multi-valued return
  doesn't fit the scalar PropsSI signature anyway
- Returning richer info (rho, p at each root) — could be a follow-up if useful

# Test plan
- Water DmolarQ at rho between triple-density and density-max: expect 2 roots
- Water HmolarQ at h slightly below the 540 K peak h_g: expect 2 roots
- Any input outside multi-root region: expect 1 root
- Q=0.5 (or anything not 0/1): expect ValueError
- Mixture: expect NotImplementedError

title: GUI smoke test: replace fixed Start-Sleep with polling render-check (Windows flake)
description:
The Windows GUI smoke test is currently:

  Start-Sleep -Seconds 15
  ...screencapture...
  python check-screenshot-rendered.py $shot 20

15s sometimes isn't enough for WebView2 cold-start on a fresh windows-latest runner — the same byte-identical binary went 49.992 std-dev on a master-push run and 13.987 std-dev on the alpha.2 tag-push run 7 minutes apart (gh-2826 PR #2826 alpha.2). Caught the flake on the alpha.2 release cut.

Fix: replace the single sleep+screenshot with a polling loop — take a screenshot every 2s, check std-dev, exit on either threshold-met or 30s timeout. Same idea on macOS for symmetry. Keep the existing fail-on-blank semantics so a real regression still trips the alarm at the timeout.

Locations:
- .github/workflows/gui_builder.yml — windows-latest and macos-latest smoke-test steps

Acceptance:
- A run that would have passed at 8s passes at first poll
- A run that would have flake-failed at 15s now waits and passes around 18-22s
- A genuinely blank render fails the job within 30s with the same diagnostic message
notes: PR #2910 opened on ihb/gui-smoke-poll: poll screencap+render every 2s up to 30s (Windows + macOS). Close once PR merges.

title: Evaluate Eigen 3.4.0 → 5.0.0 bump (clang-tidy noise + perf)
description:
Evaluate bumping the Eigen pin in cmake/dependencies.cmake from 3.4.0 to the 5.0.x series. The bump is eligible (no API blockers found) but warrants a dedicated branch + bench before merging. Driven by the CoolProp-2uw code-quality program — Eigen 5.0 systematically renames reserved-identifier template params, which is the dominant clang-tidy noise source from third-party headers in CoolProp's bugprone-*/cert-* checks.

== Eligibility (already verified) ==
- CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17 satisfies Eigen 5.0's C++14 minimum.
- CoolProp uses none of the breaking-change surfaces: no Eigen::all/last, no JacobiSVD/BDCSVD, no EulerAngles, no Eigen RNG, no internal src/ header includes, no aligned_allocator inheritance assumptions.
- unsupported/Eigen/Polynomials still ships in 5.0 — PolynomialSolver, poly_eval, roots_to_monicPolynomial paths in PolyMath/CPnumerics keep working.
- No open PRs touch dependencies.cmake.

== Code-quality motivation ==
Header noise survey (3.4.0 vs 5.0.0):
- Reserved-identifier names (\b_[A-Z]…): 2080 → 406  (~80% drop)
- TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK: 486 → 434  (~11% drop)
- pragma … push/disable/ignored: 31 → 34  (flat)
- Eigen 5.0 ships a Google-style .clang-format

CoolProp's exposure: Eigen is added via plain include_directories at CMakeLists.txt:302 (NOT SYSTEM), and .clang-tidy has HeaderFilterRegex: '*' with bugprone-* and cert-* enabled — so bugprone-reserved-identifier and cert-dcl37-c/cert-dcl51-cpp do fire on Eigen headers today.

== Perf motivation ==
Realistic gains for CoolProp's usage profile (small dense matrices + ArrayXd elementwise math):
- Vectorized log/sin/cos/pow/exp/erf overhaul (Payne-Hanek for trig, faithfully-rounded pow). Most likely measurable on superancillary array eval.
- AVX512 enabled by default if available.
- Faster small fixed-size inverse/LU/det (modest relevance to MixtureDerivatives.h).
- NaNPropagation template arg (useful if PolyMath/mixture edge cases ever bite).
Not relevant: Tensor module, GPU/SYCL, ROCm, ARM SVE, KLU sparse, half/bfloat16, ThreadPool module.

== Plan ==
1. Branch (e.g. feat/eigen-5-eval). Bump pin to v5.0.1 in cmake/dependencies.cmake.
2. Build COOLPROP_MY_MAIN + Catch2 test target. Run full Catch2 suite + consistency plots regen if applicable.
3. Microbench on superancillary ArrayXd eval and bench_density_* paths (dev/bench_density_*.cpp). Capture before/after numbers.
4. Capture diff in clang-tidy report from dev_clangtidy.yml artifact (or local equivalent on src/ + include/) — bugprone-reserved-identifier hit count is the headline metric to verify.
5. Decide:
   - If clang-tidy noise drops as expected AND benches are flat-or-better → bump.
   - If benches regress → land 3.4.1 patch only and shelve 5.0.
   - If unforeseen behavioral change shows up (1474 commits since 3.4.0) → triage and either fix or shelve.

== Related / non-blocking ==
- Independent of this bump, switching include_directories(${Eigen_SOURCE_DIR}) at CMakeLists.txt:302 to SYSTEM would silence Eigen compiler warnings entirely. Not in scope here; mention in case it's wanted as a separate quick win.
- Eigen 3.4.1 is a zero-risk patch bump (214 commits, all 3.4 maintenance). Worth landing first regardless of whether 5.0 is adopted.

Umbrella: CoolProp-2uw (code-quality program). Cross-references 2uw.5 (clang-tidy-diff workflow) and 2uw.11 (IWYU report) — the noise-reduction observed here would show up in both artifacts.
notes:
## Evaluation results (2026-04-27)

Branch: feat/eigen-5-eval (worktree at .claude/worktrees/eigen5)
Eigen pinned: 3.4.0 → 5.0.1

### Build
- Configure clean (68.7s); 1474 commits since 3.4.0 absorbed without API breaks.
- Eigen 5.0's C++14 minimum satisfied (project uses CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 17).
- Binary size flat: master Main 8.73 MB → eigen5 Main 8.69 MB.

### Catch2 test parity (exact)
- master (Eigen 3.4.0, fresh rebuild): 135 cases / 125 passed / 0 failed / 10 skipped, 57329 / 57329 / 0 / 0 assertions, 1:40 wall.
- eigen5 (Eigen 5.0.1):                  135 cases / 125 passed / 0 failed / 10 skipped, 57329 / 57329 / 0 / 0 assertions, 1:40 wall.
- Note: an earlier comparison showed 4 spurious "failures" on master — traced to a stale CatchTestRunner binary (Apr 25) preceding the Apr 26 IWYU pass + init-variables fix on Ancillaries.cpp / CoolProp-Tests.cpp. After clean rebuild, full parity.

### clang-tidy noise (PolyMath.cpp + CPnumerics.cpp, llvm@18 clang-tidy)
- bugprone-reserved-identifier hits in eigen-src/ headers:
  - Eigen 3.4.0: 404
  - Eigen 5.0.1:   9
  - 97.8% reduction. Top 3.4.0 offenders (template params _Scalar, _Rows, _Cols, _Options) systematically renamed in 5.0.
- Residual 9 are Eigen-created: 5 in BFloat16/Half (__bfloat16_raw, __half_raw, _EIGEN_MAYBE_CONSTEXPR), 4 in JacobiSVD.h (MatrixType__, Options__). Upstream cleanup target, not blocking.

### Perf — co2n2 mixture density bench (60×60 grid, /tmp/{master,eigen5}_co2n2.csv)
- ok / total:        1845/1845 vs 1845/1845 (parity).
- mean_flash_ms:     15.73 → 13.06   (-17.0%)
- mean_solver_us:    99.40 → 51.14   (-48.6%)
- mean_solver_us per-cell rel delta: -22.4% (eigen5 faster on average)
- rho_molm3 numerical agreement: max abs rel diff = 0.00e+00 across 3585 cells (bit-identical)
- Caveat: master run was 72% CPU (contention from parallel build), eigen5 was 99% CPU. User-CPU time delta -20.4% (328s → 261s) is the cleaner read; ~20-25% real speedup, likely from Eigen 5.0's faster small fixed-size LU/inverse kernels used by MixtureDerivatives.

### Verdict — GREEN LIGHT TO BUMP
- No behavioral regression (bit-identical density, exact test parity).
- Substantial clang-tidy noise reduction (97.8% on bugprone-reserved-identifier).
- Real perf win (~20-25% on representative mixture density bench).
- Diff: one-line cmake/dependencies.cmake change.

### Next steps
- Commit on feat/eigen-5-eval (do not push without user confirmation).
- Optional follow-ups (not in this issue): (a) make Eigen a SYSTEM include to silence remaining compiler warnings, (b) tighten HeaderFilterRegex from '*' to project-rooted regex, (c) bump 3.4.0 → 3.4.1 on a fallback branch in case 5.0 is rejected.

title: Tier 4.2 — Apply safe clang-tidy --fix passes
description:
After Tier 4.1 lands, apply auto-fixable clang-tidy checks of low semantic risk in follow-up commits. Each fix family in its own commit so any regression bisects cleanly. Each SHA appended to .git-blame-ignore-revs.

Initial scope (review dry-run before each):
- modernize-use-nullptr
- modernize-use-override (with existing AllowOverrideAndFinal=true, IgnoreDestructors=true)
- readability-braces-around-statements
- readability-redundant-string-init
- modernize-use-using

OUT of scope (semantic risk): cppcoreguidelines-* (most), narrowing-conversions, performance-* that change call patterns, anything modifying numeric types.

Workflow per check family:
1. run-clang-tidy -checks=<single check> -fix on a clean tree
2. cmake --build build --target format (re-format after tidy edits)
3. Run full Catch test suite
4. git commit; append SHA to .git-blame-ignore-revs in the same commit

Acceptance: each commit independently passes the existing test suite; SHAs added to .git-blame-ignore-revs; no semantic changes.
notes: Merged to master in 7adb0aa1 (#2808) init-variables --fix + 88f1158c (#2807) MathHeader + c08079d9 (#2809) blame-ignore.
