SVDSBTL docs validation notebook (Web/coolprop/SVDSBTLValidation.ipynb) is staged in working tree but uncommitted. Parallel-cross-fluid timing on 2026-05-23 showed 296s wall-clock dominated entirely by Water (295s vs 1-14s for other fluids) — suspicious; could be cache thrashing from concurrent processes (cache was being rewritten by other terminals during the run), or real per-call regression from the recent IAPWS R5 IF97 multi-region atlas merge (5d2a2e12e). Retry sequence when CoolProp-4no.3 (ALTERNATIVE_SVDTABLES_DIRECTORY) merges: (1) set notebook to use a dedicated SVDTables dir (CoolProp-4no.4), (2) re-run with isolated cache + no concurrent processes, (3) if Water is still ~300x slower than peers, file bead for per-call cost investigation. Currently in working tree: Web/coolprop/SVDSBTLValidation.ipynb (stripped, ~8KB), Web/coolprop/_gen/gen_SVDSBTLValidation.py (generator with joblib parallel), Web/coolprop/index.rst (toctree entry), Web/conf.py (added --ExecutePreprocessor.timeout=3600 to nbconvert pre-execute). Branch: ihb/svdsbtl-near-pc-investigation.

Adversarial midcell probe for tabular-backend accuracy claims: walk the training grid at (xi_i + xi_{i+1})/2, (eta_j + eta_{j+1})/2 and compare rel-err vs the truth source. If mid-cell mean is in the same ballpark (0.5x–2x) as at-node mean, the interpolation is real. If mid-cell is orders of magnitude worse, the bench is accidentally near-node and the accuracy claim is bogus. Falsifies the most common too-good-to-be-true failure mode for SVD/Bicubic/Spline tabular methods. Used on SVDSBTL Water 2026-05-15: 477k probes, mid-cell rho mean = 2.3e-7 ≈ at-node mean = 2.0e-7. Code: dev/probe_midcell.cpp, CMake flag COOLPROP_BUILD_PROBE_MIDCELL.

PR #2892 #1301 BICUBIC fix abandoned per maintainer: right path is SVDSBTL DT-indexed surface, not patching BICUBIC inversion. When invert_single_phase_y on a (P,T)-indexed table fails, no clean solution at that layer — either return garbage (master) or break callers (throw). DT-indexed surface turns it into direct lookup. Bilinear bisection improvement (find_nearest_neighbor) is a real insight worth reviving as stand-alone PR (in-table p99 33%→8.8% on yufang67 CO2 grid).

Chebyshev colleague-matrix eigenvalue cost (Eigen 3.4, methane-style coefs, 32 probes × 30 reps): N=2 closed-form 0.04us, N=3 closed-form 0.21us, N=4 Eigen 1.5us, N=8 Eigen 6.2us, N=12 Eigen 14.3us (production target — paper-recommended degree), N=16 Eigen 25us, N=20 Eigen 42us, N=32 Eigen 126us, N=50 Eigen 394us median but p95 2600us and max 40000us (tail-latency risk from QR clustering). RealSchur and EigenSolver(false) tie within 5%; computeEigenvectors=true wastes 40-50%. balance_matrix preprocessing helps accuracy not speed at the median. Implications for 89d: with paper's skip heuristic (oversample at 2N+1 nodes O(N), skip pieces where all values far-from-zero), only ~5/30 pieces survive to eigenvalue work → ~85 us per density solve from rootfinding. Closed-form N=2/3 is 5x faster but only relevant if we adopt real_roots2 pattern. N=50 needs setMaxIterations cap to bound tail. Benchmark in dev/cheb_eig_bench.cpp.

CoolProp-89d v1 milestone (2026-04-28): shared-partition Chebyshev density rootfinder lands at paper-regime timing on methane. 6/6 PASS, max rel err 1.27e-14 (post-Halley polish on full EOS), median 30-92 μs per call (vs Phase D PoC 490 μs, ~10× speedup). Build time 1.1 ms one-shot, memory 260 KB for 40 GenExp terms × 64 pieces × degree 12. Skip heuristic eliminates ~95% of pieces. Architecture: uniform partition (v1), runtime linear-combine (matrix-vector phi_d_per_piece * kappa), 2N+1 oversample skip check, TOMS748 on surviving brackets, then 1-2 Halley steps via full EOS. dev/cheb_density_v2.cpp. Bracket orientation fix needed: 2N+1 Chebyshev-Lobatto nodes are right-to-left (cos(0)=1 → b, cos(π)=-1 → a), so over_real(j-1) > over_real(j); TOMS748 needs xL<xR — sort first.

CoolProp PT flash p95 times (CoolProp-aor baseline, 60x60 grids): CH4/H2S 9 ms, CO2/N2 44 ms (worst, near-critical band), n-Decane/Methane 8 ms. Max single-cell flash time 414 ms on CO2/N2 in near-critical region. Solver_rho_Tp isolated p95: 33-194 μs, max ~1 ms. So full flash dominates by ~100x, but inside the flash solver_rho_Tp is called multiple times per phase + per Newton iter, so cumulative cost is closer.

Cheb mixture density Phase-A empirical findings (CoolProp-oja, 2026-04-26): (1) The kappa(tau)*phi(delta) factorization holds at machine precision (abs err 1e-16 to 1e-17) for all 5 representative GenExp term variants tested (power_pure, power_exp, bell_delta, bell_tau, mixed). (2) Practical delta_min is 1e-6, not 1e-10 — going lower forces dyadic splits that buy no real accuracy (function value is already below noise). Spec updated. (3) Steep terms (Gaussian-bell-delta) generate up to 675 dyadic pieces per term at tol=1e-12 — memory ~6 KB per term, totally fine. Build time ~7 ms per term, one-shot per process. (4) Wide-rectangle Chebyshev evaluation has abs err ~1e-15 but rel err can be ~1e-9 at the small-delta tail; the abs error is what matters for the rootfinder (P-error scales abs_err·rho·R·T, microPa range).

Cheb mixture density Phase-D milestone (CoolProp-oja, 2026-04-26): pure-fluid pressure rootfind via Chebyshev P(delta) reproduces production rho at 1e-13 to 1e-15 relative error on methane across gas/liquid/supercritical/cryogenic. Multi-root detection works (cryogenic liquid returns 3 roots — gas/intermediate/liquid for subcritical PT). Algorithm: precompute phi_k and phi_d_k per term; at runtime κ_k(τ) sweep + dyadic_splitting of P(δ) on [1e-6, 6] at tol=1e-8, then per-piece TOMS748 rootfind of (P-p_target)=0. dev/cheb_density_poc.cpp. Floating-point gotcha: dyadic Chebyshev nodes can land 1e-22 outside the rectangle via cos(π) ULPs — eval_phi_cheb must clamp to piece bounds. Performance currently ~290 μs per query (over the 5-20 μs target, bottleneck = 1843 phi-pieces/term from too-tight build tol; CoolProp-42i tuning will fix).

Chebyshev colleague-matrix fixed-size Eigen template benchmark (CoolProp-89d follow-up, 2026-04-28): Eigen Matrix<double, N, N> at compile-time-known N gives ~5-16% median speedup vs dynamic MatrixXd at production-relevant sizes (N=4,6,8,12,16) — best at N=4 (16%) and N=12 (13%). HOWEVER the fixed-size path has worse tail latency: N=16 max went from 31 us (dynamic) to 29.5 ms (fixed). Pathological QR iteration regime that the dynamic path apparently tames via different code path. NOT WORTH the complexity — skip fixed-size templates. The optimization that matters is the rootfinding-avoidance heuristic (oversample at 2N+1 nodes, skip pieces where all values far from zero), per Bell-Alpert §2.4.1, not solver specialization. Benchmark in dev/cheb_eig_bench.cpp.

Mixture density baseline (CoolProp-aor) — full results across 4 systems, 60x60 PT grids:

Solver-fail rate of solver_rho_Tp(T,p,-1) where the FULL FLASH succeeds:
  amarillo (10-comp AGA-8):  1399/3586 (39%)  — flash p95 155 ms, solver max 5.2 ms
  CH4/H2S 70/30:             2214/3418 (65%)  — flash p95   9 ms, solver max 0.4 ms
  CO2/N2 80/20:              1740/3585 (49%)  — flash p95  44 ms, solver max 0.9 ms
  n-Decane/Methane 30/70:     895/3552 (25%)  — flash p95   8 ms, solver max 0.3 ms

Solver_rho_Tp itself scales badly with N components (5 ms max for 10-comp amarillo, vs 0.3 ms for binary c10c1). Failure regions cluster along the phase envelope. Replacement methods (Gernert, Chebyshev) must be evaluated on both isolation-robustness and N-scaling, not just steady-state cost.

Mixture density rootfinder baseline (CoolProp-aor): HelmholtzEOSMixtureBackend::solver_rho_Tp(T, p, rho_guess=-1) fails on 25-65% of (T,p) cells where the full PT flash succeeds. Tested on CH4/H2S 70/30 (65% fail), CO2/N2 80/20 (49% fail), n-Decane/Methane 30/70 (25% fail). Failures cluster near phase boundaries — Householder4 starting from the SRK guess hits inflection points and bails. The full PT flash succeeds because update_TP() funnels into solver_rho_Tp_global with bracketed Brent. So 'solver_rho_Tp by itself' has a huge fragility gap relative to 'orchestrated by the flash.' Implication: a Chebyshev or Gernert replacement must either (a) be robust as a standalone rootfinder or (b) provide a similar bracket/dispatch wrapper. CSV+PNG artifacts in dev/bench_density_*.

Chebyshev density rootfinder solution polishing (per Bell-Alpert 2018, §4.4.1, Ian 2026-04-28): Chebyshev rootfind enumerates all candidate roots reliably, then EACH root gets 1-2 Halley steps using the FULL EOS (not the Chebyshev). Polish formula is delta_new = delta_old - 2·r·r' / (2·(r')² - r·r''), where r = p - p_target, r' = ρ_t·R·T·[1 + 2·A_01 + A_02], r'' = (ρ_t·R·T/δ)·[2·A_02 + 4·A_02 + A_03] from production alphar derivatives (Eq 60-63). One or two steps reaches <1e-10% pressure error vs full EOS. Phase pruning / Gibbs comparison among multiple polished roots also uses full EOS values. So: Chebyshev = reliable enumeration; full EOS = accuracy + selection. Architecture matches what 89d should do.

Chebyshev mixture density rootfinder — CORRECT architecture (per Ian, 2026-04-25): precompute Chebyshev expansions of each individual EOS term in δ ONLY (not τ). Generalized-exponential terms factor cleanly into (τ-prefactor scalar) × (δ-function); the δ-function is static per-term and gets one Chebyshev expansion at compile/load time. At runtime evaluate the τ-prefactor closed-form, scale the per-term δ-expansion, sum across terms with corresponding-states weights to get α^r(τ_eval, δ) as a Chebyshev-in-δ polynomial. Rootfind in δ on that polynomial. ZERO build cost at runtime, ZERO composition dependence in precomputation. Memory: O(N_fluids + N_binary_pairs). Non-analytic and SAFT-associating terms don't factor and fall back to direct evaluation. This is the right framing for CoolProp-3gt — DO NOT propose per-z 2D builds or per-call 1D builds; those were both wrong.

When committing non-bd code changes alongside a bd-claim, EXCLUDE .beads/issues.jsonl from the commit. The JSONL is bd's auto-export of the database, not the source of truth. Including it in topic-branch commits causes spurious conflicts: each branch carries a different JSONL snapshot reflecting its claim state, and when one PR merges with its snapshot, every other open branch diverges on that same file. Pattern that works: do code edits + bd update --claim, then 'git add' only the code files (drop .beads/issues.jsonl). A periodic dedicated bd-state-sync PR (e.g. #2785 pattern) re-exports the DB state to JSONL cleanly. CAVEAT: the bd pre-commit hook (.beads-hooks/pre-commit) auto-re-exports the JSONL into the working tree on every commit, which can sneak it back in even after a 'git checkout origin/master -- .beads/issues.jsonl'. Workaround for amends/recommits: 'git -c core.hooksPath= commit --amend ...' disables hooks for that single command without modifying config. Discovered when 6 parallel CoolProp-2uw topic branches all collided on .beads/issues.jsonl after the first one merged (PR #2786, 2026-04-26).

Cheb density: precompute phi_d (the delta-derivative of phi) directly by sampling its closed-form analytic expression at Chebyshev nodes — do NOT take the derivative of a precomputed phi-Chebyshev (Ian, 2026-04-28). Reason: Chebyshev differentiation is destructive at the high-frequency end (truncates the top coefficient), and at looser build tolerances the 'lost' coefficient was carrying real information. Empirical impact on methane: at tol=1e-6, n=12, max rel rho err improved from 2.2e-9 to 1.2e-11 (180× better) just from this fix. For GenExp terms, phi'(delta) = delta^d · exp(g) · (d/delta + g'(delta)) where g = -c·delta^l - eta1(delta-eps1) - eta2(delta-eps2)^2 and g'= -c·l·delta^(l-1) - eta1 - 2·eta2·(delta-eps2). Pattern generalizes: precompute phi, phi', phi'' all by direct sampling — never via do_derivs(N) on a phi-Chebyshev.

Cheb density tuning sweep (CoolProp-42i, methane, 2026-04-26): higher Chebyshev degree dominates lower build tolerance. RECOMMENDED config: phi build at tol=1e-8 with degree n=12 → 2567 phi pieces total (vs 73735 baseline at tol=1e-12 n=8), 521 KB total memory, 3.7 ms one-shot build, 490 μs per-call density solve (median over 6 test points × 50 reps), max relative density error 6e-12 vs production update(PT_INPUTS). For higher accuracy budget: tol=1e-12 n=12 keeps machine precision but uses 6756 pieces / 1.3 MB. Per-call solve time bottleneck is NOT phi piece count — it's the runtime dyadic_splitting of P(δ) plus per-piece TOMS748 (~50 P-pieces × 9 nodes × 40 terms × 50 ops ≈ 900k ops ≈ ~1 ms at 1 ns/op). Getting under the 5-20 μs spec target needs structural change (common piece partitions across terms + direct Chebyshev product on each piece, no runtime dyadic_splitting).

CMake conditional scope gotcha (CoolProp). When wiring a target property like CXX_INCLUDE_WHAT_YOU_USE, place the if(<feature>) ... set_property(TARGET ...) block AFTER all target-creation paths, NOT inside one. CoolProp creates Main from two different conditionals: if(COOLPROP_MY_MAIN) (used by CodeQL/Coverity/IWYU CI) and if(COOLPROP_MAIN_MODULE) (the regular path). An IWYU set_property nested inside COOLPROP_MAIN_MODULE silently no-op'd for the IWYU CI path → empty artifact. Surface signal: 'Manually-specified variables were not used by the project: COOLPROP_<X>' warning during configure. Fix pattern (in CMakeLists.txt bottom): if(COOLPROP_IWYU) { foreach(t Main CatchTestRunner) if(TARGET ) set_property(...); endforeach() }. Same lesson applies to any future per-target instrumentation.

Bell-Alpert 2018 reference implementation: paper at to_be_implemented/Exceptionally-reliable-density-solving-algorithms-for-multiparameter-mixture-models-from-Chebyshev-expansion-rootfinding.pdf describes ChebMix C++11 library (separate from ChebTools, supplemental material referenced). Key sections: §3.1 factorization (Eq 41-53) — use δG'_k closed-form derivative (Eq 50, 53) NOT do_derivs; §3.3 dyadic interval subdivision with M-element-norm splitting metric (Eq 54); §4.3 flattening (matrix-vector products Eq 56-57); §4.4 rootfinder is iterative oversampled-Chebyshev-Lobatto + local quadratic + modified secant (real_roots2), not companion matrix — companion only for verification (companion is ~700 μs at N=50); §4.4.1 polish via Halley on FULL EOS; §6 limitations: non-analytic terms can't be separated, very-few-component mixtures may be slower than conventional. CoolProp's existing superancillary::detail::balance_matrix is already cited (James-Langou-Lowery 2014, arXiv 1401.5766) and likely came from this work.

Cheb density timing comparison vs Bell-Alpert 2018 §5 Fig 11 (2026-04-28). Paper hardware: Intel i7-3770 @ 3.4 GHz (2012). Paper numbers (full τ flattening per call): 5-comp ~110 μs, 10-comp ~150 μs. With τ fixed/cached: ~30 μs across component count. Paper's quoted 50×50 eigenvalue: 700 μs; we get 394 μs on Apple Silicon (1.8× hardware speedup). OUR Phase D PoC at 490 μs/call (methane, 1-comp) is 3-4× slower than paper would predict, fully explained by 89d not being implemented: per-term partition (we have 2567 pieces vs paper's ~250 from shared partition), runtime dyadic_splitting of P(δ) (vs paper's matrix-vector product), no skip heuristic (we try every piece), companion matrix at 14 μs (vs paper's iterative real_roots2 at ~2 μs per surviving piece). Realistic target after 89d: 50-150 μs/call full-flattening, ~30 μs/call τ-cached. Spec aspirational 5-20 μs target was wrong; updated.

CoolProp-89d v2 adaptive partition (2026-04-28): mixture-polynomial M-element-norm metric (Bell-Alpert §3.3 Eq 54) computed on Σκ_k(τ)·phi_d_k coefs at multiple τ-pivots, MAX taken across pivots. Per-term M-norm metric over-splits because terms cancel in linear combination — 581 pieces. Mixture metric: 29 pieces same accuracy. v2 timing on methane: 17 μs median (vs v1 uniform 64-piece's 30 μs, ~1.8× speedup), 118 KB memory, 18 ms one-shot build. Beats paper's no-τ-flattening 30 μs benchmark on modern hardware. Piece distribution coarse at low/high δ, refines around δ~1 (critical region). Implementation in dev/cheb_density_v2.cpp.
